You are executing one bead inside an isolated DDx execution worktree. The bead's <description> and <acceptance> are the completion contract — every AC must be provably satisfied by a specific code, test, or file you can point to after your commit.

## Step 0: size check

The bead is too big when any of these holds:

- More than ~6 ACs spanning unrelated subsystems.
- AC mixes design, implementation, integration tests, and docs as separate top-level deliverables.
- Description names multiple feature-sized sub-pieces.
- More than ~500 lines across more than ~5 files in unrelated packages.

If too big, decompose — do not attempt the work:

1. `ddx bead create` for each child slice (focused title, description, AC; copy parent's labels and spec-id).
2. `ddx bead dep add <child-id> <parent-id>` to record edges.
3. `ddx bead update <parent-id> --notes 'decomposed into <child-ids>'` so the decomposition is visible.
4. Write `.ddx/executions/20260503T195424-22e5c50e/no_changes_rationale.txt` listing each child ID and slice, then stop.

A clean decomposition is a successful attempt. Do not mix partial implementation with decomposition.

## How to work

- Read first. If the bead names files, specs, or prior beads, read them before editing — do not guess.
- Cross-reference each AC against concrete evidence (test name, file path, function) before committing. If you cannot point at it, it is not done.
- Run the project's test and lint commands before committing. **Do not commit red code** — fix failures first.
- Stage with `git add <specific-paths>`; never `git add -A` (the worktree may have unrelated WIP).
- Commit exactly once when green; conventional-commit subject ending with `[<bead-id>]`. Stop after the commit.
- Do not modify files outside the bead's scope.
- If you cannot finish, write `.ddx/executions/20260503T195424-22e5c50e/no_changes_rationale.txt` (what is done, what blocks, what a follow-up needs) before exiting. No commit and no rationale ⇒ DDx records `no_evidence_produced`. A well-justified no_changes beats a bad commit.

## no_changes contract

If you write `.ddx/executions/20260503T195424-22e5c50e/no_changes_rationale.txt`, it MUST carry one of:

- `verification_command: <one-line shell command>` — the loop runs it (cwd = project root, 60s); exit 0 closes the bead, non-zero rejects.
- `status: needs_investigation` + `reason: <why>` — bead stays open for triage.

Bare rationales ("nothing to do") are rejected.

## Reports

Freestanding artifacts (investigation reports, findings docs) go under `.ddx/executions/20260503T195424-22e5c50e/` (the per-attempt evidence directory under `.ddx/executions/`). **Never write reports to `/tmp` or any path outside the repository** — out-of-repo paths are invisible to the post-merge reviewer and cause BLOCK on missing evidence. If the bead names a specific in-repo path, use it; else default to `.ddx/executions/20260503T195424-22e5c50e/<short-name>.md`. Stage and commit the report alongside code.

## Bead overrides project defaults

The bead description and AC override CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, and conservative project defaults (YAGNI, DOWITYTD, no-docs-unless-asked) in this worktree — write whatever the bead asks for.

## Review gate

- The review is a gate, not an escape hatch — meet every AC in this pass.
- Address every BLOCKING `<review-findings>` item; do not declare `no_changes` with blocking findings open.

## Constraints

- Work only inside this execution worktree.
- Keep `.ddx/executions/` intact — DDx uses it as execution evidence.
- **Never run `ddx init`** — the workspace is initialized; running it corrupts the bead queue.
- Do not modify files outside the bead's named scope.
- Do not rewrite CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, or other project-instructions files unless the bead asks.

## When the work is done

After the commit succeeds and every AC is verified, stop. Return control to the orchestrator — do not keep exploring or testing.

rationale: Bead ddx-27936512 is a duplicate of work already merged in commit 0b1b3964
("feat(server): run-detail resolver + tool-call persistence + bundle-file
confinement [ddx-6d132f62]"). All four acceptance criteria are already
satisfied by existing code:

AC1: Run resolver returns full detail (lossless)
  - cli/internal/server/state_runs_detail.go:250 loadRunDetail() populates
    prompt/response/stderr from session bodies and bundleFiles via
    listRunBundleFiles(); invoked from state_runs.go:520 on run(id:) lookup.
  - cli/internal/server/graphql/resolver_runs.go wires Run, RunToolCalls,
    RunBundleFile resolvers to the state methods.

AC2: ToolCallEntry persisted at drain time (NOT log-parsing)
  - agent.SessionIndexEntry carries a ToolCalls []ToolCallEntry field;
    SessionIndexEntryFromResult copies result.ToolCalls into the entry,
    so the persistence happens once at drain time rather than on each
    resolver call. GetRunToolCallsGraphQL reads only the persisted slice.

AC3: bundleFile endpoint refuses path traversal
  - resolveRunBundlePath (state_runs_detail.go:111) rejects absolute paths,
    "..", paths whose Clean escapes the root, and symlink targets that
    EvalSymlinks resolves outside bundleRoot. Surfaced via
    GetRunBundleFileGraphQL → Query.runBundleFile.

AC4: Tests cover all three
  - state_runs_detail_test.go covers: dotdot/absolute/under-workdir

.ddx/beads.jsonl:388:{"archived_at":"2026-07-02T22:52:12Z","closing_commit_sha":"277f6be2aa285202336cd399c669bd1f8f03cf81","created_at":"2026-06-28T04:37:40.306667675Z","description":"PROBLEM
The test TestPreDispatchDirtyPreserveRequiresStableImplementationDirt at cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop_stay_alive_test.go:836 fails on a normally-loaded developer machine because it uses a fixed 150ms context.WithTimeout for an in-process worker.Run() call that has to claim + execute + land + close one bead and continue polling. The 150ms budget is too tight; observed outcome on base revision edbcd9aa5: err is nil (work completes before deadline OR worker exits cleanly via another path), so require.ErrorIs(t, err, context.DeadlineExceeded) at line 882 fails.

This is a release-blocker: lefthook run pre-commit fails, blocking every subsequent bead from landing. Diagnosed in ddx-47d9100a's no_changes_rationale (the implementer correctly identified it as out-of-scope pre-existing breakage from commit 4555a083f, introduced by ddx-df319cf9).

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop_stay_alive_test.go:871 — context.WithTimeout 150*time.Millisecond
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop_stay_alive_test.go:882 — require.ErrorIs(t, err, context.DeadlineExceeded)
- Adjacent test ~line 726 (TestPreDispatchDirtyPreservePersistsStableImplementationDirt) uses the correct pattern: 30s outer timeout, worker.Run in a goroutine, require.Eventually polling for bead closure, then cancel(). See the comment at lines 772-774 explaining why 750ms was too tight.

PROPOSED FIX
Rewrite TestPreDispatchDirtyPreserveRequiresStableImplementationDirt to match the surrounding require.Eventually pattern:
1. Outer ctx timeout: 30*time.Second.
2. Run worker.Run in a goroutine, push result+err to a runCh.
3. require.Eventually (25s budget, 50ms tick) polls store.Get("ddx-int-0001") until Status == StatusClosed.
4. cancel() the outer ctx; <-runCh to collect outcome.
5. Assertions become: outcome.err is one of context.Canceled or context.DeadlineExceeded (use require.True with errors.Is check), Attempts=1, Successes=1, Failures=0, OperatorAttention nil, no "loop.pre_dispatch_dirty_preserved" event, no refs/ddx/pre-dispatch references.

IN-SCOPE FILES
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop_stay_alive_test.go (only the failing test)

OUT-OF-SCOPE
- Do not modify production code.
- Do not touch the passing adjacent test.
- Do not modify other 150ms or sub-second timeouts in this file; this bead targets only the named test.

AC
1. cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop_stay_alive_test.go:TestPreDispatchDirtyPreserveRequiresStableImplementationDirt rewritten to use require.Eventually + goroutine pattern with 30s outer timeout, mirroring TestPreDispatchDirtyPreservePersistsStableImplementationDirt at ~line 726.
2. cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... -run 'TestPreDispatchDirtyPreserve' -count=5 green (5x to confirm non-flaky).
3. cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... -count=1 green.
4. lefthook run pre-commit passes.
5. The test still verifies the same invariants: a single bead claim+close happens, OperatorAttention is nil, no pre-dispatch refs are The store factory still treats Axon as an experimental special case and only enables it behind an environment gate. The relevant branch is cli/internal/bead/store.go:117-137, which falls back to JSONL unless DDX_AXON_EXPERIMENTAL is truthy, even when config has backend: axon. The checked-in sample config also does not document the bead_tracker backend block yet (.ddx/config.yaml:1-17).

ROOT CAUSE
cli/internal/bead/store.go:117-137 and .ddx/config.yaml:1-17 do not present Axon as a normal selectable backend. The config type already has a Backend field, but the factory still requires an env override and emits a warning instead of honoring configuration directly.

PROPOSED FIX
- Update the store factory so bead_tracker.backend=axon selects the Axon backend from config without requiring the experimental env var.
- Update the checked-in .ddx/config.yaml example to show the backend and Axon connection block.
- Keep the default backend unchanged when backend is unset.
- Preserve bd/br behavior for the existing external backend path.

NON-SCOPE
- GraphQL client generation.
- Axon backend CRUD internals.

.ddx/beads.jsonl:403:{"acceptance":"AC1. ddx try/ddx work treats success outcome and bead-state propagation as a single recoverable unit: if the finalize step fails after outcome=task_succeeded is written, the next pick of the same bead detects the dangling result_rev and either lands it or surfaces a structured error (not silent no_evidence_produced).
AC2. A regression test exercises the dangling-success scenario by simulating a finalize failure and asserting the bead lands cleanly on retry.
AC3. ddx doctor (or a new diagnostic command) detects executions where result.json claims success but result_rev is unreachable from any branch, and reports them with the recovery command (git merge --no-ff <sha> + ddx bead close --commit <sha> --session <sid>).
AC4. Documentation in the ddx skill or work.md describes this failure mode and how operators can recover manually until AC1 ships.","archived_at":"2026-05-15T00:08:56Z","closing_commit_sha":"004453de063a4e607c7178079ca1ff207be7014e","created_at":"2026-05-08T21:00:40.247216169Z","description":"PROBLEM: A ddx try/ddx work attempt that produces outcome=task_succeeded and a clean result_rev may fail to auto-merge or update the bead state, leaving the success commit unreachable from any branch and the bead stuck in_progress. Operators must manually recover by checking out the dangling SHA, verifying ACs, and merging.

REPRO (observed in axon project, 2026-05-08):
- Bead axon-d323acc9 ("refactor(axon-schema): import named-query parser from axon-cypher-ast")
- Attempt 20260508T190516-8d61a244, session eb-b068c4db
- result.json fields: outcome=task_succeeded, status=success, result_rev=c3386193ab1c18fdf5739901c41d2e69093f1030, base_rev=9194da47097b2d0d05899a51688950084b3995b8, duration_ms=1179708, exit_code=0
- Master HEAD never advanced to include c3386193; bead remained in_progress until manual recovery
- A subsequent attempt 20260508T192511-c4a200de fired 15 seconds later and recorded outcome=task_no_evidence with detail "agent exited without a commit or no_changes_rationale.txt", which suggests the worker re-ran and saw no work to do (the success was already in the dangling commit) but did not connect that to the prior success record.

CURRENT STATE:
- Both attempt result.json files are present and parsable.
- Recovery required: git merge --no-ff <result_rev> + ddx bead close --commit <result_rev> --session <session_id>.
- No evidence in the bead events indicated the attempt had succeeded; the success was discovered by inspecting result.json on disk.

PROPOSED FIX DIRECTION (owned by ddx-loop/finalize maintainer):
1. The post-execute finalize step (merge + bead update with success event) should be transactional with attempt outcome, or at minimum idempotent so a follow-up ddx work cycle can complete it.
2. On bead pick, if status=in_progress and the most-recent attempt outcome=success but no merge commit references the result_rev, attempt to land that result before claiming new work or starting a fresh attempt.
3. A startup self-check could enumerate result.json files where outcome=task_succeeded and ensure the corresponding result_rev is reachable from a branch; surface inconsistencies as recoverable state.

.ddx/beads.jsonl:427:{"acceptance":"1. TestReadinessChecksSchema in cli/internal/agent/readiness_schema_test.go validates every verdict form the decoder accepts (bool true, bool false, string, null, absent) against cli/internal/agent/schema/readiness-checks.schema.json AND asserts an object verdict is rejected, locking producer/consumer alignment.
2. The producer prompt buildPreClaimIntakePrompt (cli/internal/agent/preclaim_intake_hook.go:636-655) references the canonical schema path via the readinessChecksSchemaPath constant (cli/internal/agent/preclaim_intake_hook.go:21), not a duplicated string literal.
3. cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... green
4. lefthook run pre-commit passes","archived_at":"2026-07-02T22:52:12Z","closing_commit_sha":"rev-ddx-2e3ad63d","created_at":"2026-05-28T09:08:21.2215798Z","description":"PROBLEM
The pre_claim_intake readiness payload schema can drift between the producer prompt and the Go decoder. That drift is exactly the class of bug that wedged the fizeau queue: the producer emitted readiness_checks[].verdict as a JSON bool while the consumer parsed a string, so every claim attempt failed with a warn-only rejection and the queue idled. There must be a single shared schema that both sides consume, plus a CI-run test that fails the moment the schema and decoder diverge.

ROOT CAUSE
- The producer prompt references the schema only by a path string in prose: cli/internal/agent/preclaim_intake_hook.go:639 (buildPreClaimIntakePrompt) writes "Canonical schema: "+readinessChecksSchemaPath, and the constant lives at cli/internal/agent/preclaim_intake_hook.go:21. Because the prompt text and the schema file are independent, instructions can drift from the schema with nothing catching it.
- The consumer decoder accepts bool/string/null/absent verdict forms: readinessVerdict.UnmarshalJSON at cli/internal/agent/preclaim_intake_hook.go:97-125 and preClaimReadinessChecksPayload.UnmarshalJSON at cli/internal/agent/preclaim_intake_hook.go:386-416.
- The schema verdict union is cli/internal/agent/schema/readiness-checks.schema.json:44-50 (oneOf boolean/string/null). Nothing fails CI if someone tightens the schema, or changes the decoder, so that the two diverge.

PROPOSED FIX
Keep cli/internal/agent/schema/readiness-checks.schema.json as the single source of truth consumed by both the producer prompt (via the readinessChecksSchemaPath constant) and the consumer decoder. Ensure a CI-run test validates every verdict form the decoder accepts against the schema and asserts that an object verdict is rejected, so any tightening of the schema or change to the decoder that breaks alignment fails the suite. Confirm the producer prompt references the canonical path constant rather than a duplicated literal.

NON-SCOPE
- Do NOT implement the runtime same-fingerprint warn escalation guard (owned by the sibling child bead).
- Do NOT change the verdict coercion semantics (bool true->pass, bool false->fail, string->lowercased, null/absent->empty).
- Do NOT introduce go.mod replace directives.

.ddx/beads.jsonl:436:{"acceptance":"1. Add `TestExecutionCleanupManager_DryRunReportsStaleAttemptDescendantProcesses` (or equivalent) proving a stale process whose cwd/worktree is under the current project's DDx execution root is reported with pid, command, worktree, stale reason, and `would_kill=true`, while dry-run does not call the killer.
2. Add `TestExecutionCleanupManager_ApplyReapsOnlyStaleAttemptProcessGroups` (or equivalent, using injected scanner/killer fakes where needed) proving `--apply` terminates only stale DDx-owned attempt process groups and preserves live run-state, live worker sidecar, active registered worktree, and other-project processes.
3. Add `TestCleanupCommand_JSONIncludesProcessFindings` and a text-output assertion proving `ddx cleanup --json` and dry-run text include process findings/counts without breaking the existing stale temp dir/run-state/scratch report fields.
4. Add `TestWorkStartupCleanup_ReapsStaleAttemptDescendantProcessesBeforeClaim` (or equivalent) proving work startup/pre-claim cleanup runs the same conservative process pass before claiming the next bead and emits a structured cleanup observation when it reaps or preserves a process group.
5. Add a regression case for a non-harness descendant command such as `sh`, `cargo`, or a tiny long-lived test server under a stale execution worktree; it must be detected by DDx ownership/cwd/liveness evidence, not by broad command-name matching.
6. Unsupported platforms have a deterministic no-op/report-unavailable path covered by a build-tagged test or equivalent assertion; Linux behavior is covered by the process-census/reaper tests.
7. Existing process-reaper tests remain green: `cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... -run 'OrphanHarness|Executor|DockerCloneBackend_ProcessGroupCleanup' -count=1` passes.
8. Focused cleanup tests pass: `cd cli && go test ./cmd/... ./internal/agent/... -run 'Cleanup.*Process|AttemptDescendant|WorkStartupCleanup|CleanupCommand' -count=1` passes.
9. Package-level gate passes: `cd cli && go test ./cmd/... ./internal/agent/... -count=1` passes.
10. `lefthook run pre-commit` passes.","archived_at":"2026-07-02T22:52:12Z","closing_commit_sha":"69aace22ab0773641d55dbb1bea97c2e602e5c7f","created_at":"2026-06-08T11:22:34.861350262Z","description":"PROBLEM
The Niflheim incident on 2026-06-08 showed that DDx cleanup/recovery can still leave heavy DDx-owned execution processes behind even when file cleanup exists. Existing cleanup removes stale temp worktrees, run-state files, evidence/logs, worker dirs, and scratch dirs, but the operator recovery command does not report or reap stale attempt-descendant processes such as orphan shells, `cargo test`/benchmark processes, or long-lived test servers whose cwd or command context still points at a DDx execution worktree. Those processes can keep burning CPU or hold ports after the original `ddx try`/`ddx work` attempt is gone.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- `cli/cmd/cleanup.go:69-72` constructs `agent.NewExecutionCleanupManager` and runs it in dry-run or `--apply`, but `cleanupCommandReport` at `cli/cmd/cleanup.go:11-30` has no process findings, PIDs, process-group counts, or port/process recovery fields.
- `cli/internal/agent/execution_cleanup.go:230-527` cleans DDx temp dirs, registered worktrees, scratch roots, stale run-state files, evidence dirs, agent logs, worker dirs, and candidate refs. `ExecutionCleanupSummary` at `cli/internal/agent/execution_cleanup.go:110-138` reports file/resource counts only; it cannot tell an operator that a stale DDx-owned process tree is still alive.
- `cli/cmd/startup_housekeeping.go:218-267` removes stale `.ddx/workers/agent-loop-*` sidecar dirs, but it does not terminate or report the underlying DDx-owned process tree when a stale sidecar corresponds to an already-orphaned attempt descendant.
- `cli/internal/agent/orphan_harness_reaper.go:42-152` is an important closed fix for `ddx work` startup, but it only considers PID-1 children whose command basename looks like a top-level harness (`claude`, `codex`, `gemini`, `opencode`, `pi`) and whose bead lease owner PID is gone (`orphan_harness_reaper.go:82-100`, `155-168`). It does not cover the case where the harness root has already exited but a shell, cargo test/benchmark, or test server remains under the stale execution worktree/process group.
- `cli/internal/agent/executor_unix.go:10-29` sets Setpgid/Pdeathsig for normal harness children, and closed beads `ddx-0d7d5f0e`, `ddx-3113d524`, and `ddx-418c3849` cover parent-death/startup harness reaping. This bead is not a duplicate: it adds an operator-visible cleanup/process-census path for stale DDx-owned descendant processes that no longer look like a live top-level harness.

PROPOSED FIX
- Extend the cleanup manager with a conservative process census/reaper for DDx-owned execution attempts. On Linux, use `/proc`-style facts already used by `cli/internal/workerstatus`/`orphan_harness_reaper`: pid, ppid, process group when available, command, cwd, start time, and enough ownership context to tie the process to the current project execution root or a known DDx attempt worktree. Provide a no-op/report-unavailable implementation on unsupported platforms.
- `ddx cleanup` dry-run must report stale DDx-owned attempt processes without killing them. The report should include PID/process group, command basename or truncated command, cwd/worktree, bead/attempt when inferable, liveness reason, and whether `--apply` would terminate it.
- `ddx cleanup --apply` may terminate only process groups that are provably stale and DDx-owned: associated run-state/cleanup liveness is expired or missing, the recorded owner PID is gone, the worktree is under the current project's execution root, and no live worker/registered active worktree still owns it. Prefer graceful terminate then bounded kill when feasible; never kill arbitrary host processes by command name alone.
- Wire the same conservative process pass into work startup/pre-claim cleanup so queue control can recover from stale attempt descendant processes before claiming more work. Keep cleanup lock/jitter behavior intact so multiple workers do not race to kill the same process group.
- Update cleanup reporting and JSON shape with process counts and blocked warnings. If a stale DDx-owned process is detected but cannot be killed safely, surface a warning/operator-visible observation instead of silently deleting files and leaving CPU/ports occupied.

IN-SCOPE FILES
- `cli/internal/agent/execution_cleanup.go` and adjacent new platform-specific process cleanup files/tests.
- `cli/cmd/cleanup.go` and `cli/cmd/cleanup_test.go` for text/JSON reporting.
- `cli/cmd/startup_housekeeping.go` and existing work-startup cleanup tests for queue-control wiring.
- `cli/internal/workerstatus/*` or `cli/internal/agent/orphan_harness_reaper*` only for reused process-scanning helpers, not broad rewrites.
- `docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-010-task-execution.md` and `docs/helix/02-design/solution-designs/SD-025-task-execution-lifecycle.md` if the implementation changes the documented cleanup contract.

OUT-OF-SCOPE
- Do not edit Niflheim.
- Do not duplicate `ddx-e0de6369`; that bead owns `.ddx/harness-sessions/` scratch ignore/prune behavior.
- Do not duplicate `ddx-bbea954f`; that bead owns dirty timeout worktree edit preservation before cleanup deletion.
- Do not replace the existing parent-death/process-group fixes from `ddx-0d7d5f0e`, `ddx-3113d524`, or `ddx-418c3849`.
- Do not implement a general-purpose port scanner or host process manager. Port conflict recovery here comes only from safely reaping stale DDx-owned attempt process groups.
- Do not kill processes outside the current project's DDx execution root, processes tied to live run-state/worker liveness, active registered worktrees, or non-DDx user workloads.

PARENT AND DEPS
Parent: `ddx-3106674f` (multi-worker reliability and Niflheim local gates).

.ddx/beads.jsonl:452:{"acceptance":"1. `TestExecuteBead_DirtyNoEvidencePreservesAttempt` proves a fake runner that leaves dirty code files, exits 0, and writes no commit/rationale returns `no_evidence_produced` with a non-empty preserve ref or patch artifact instead of deleting the attempt.
2. `TestExecuteBead_CleanNoEvidenceStillCleansWorktree` proves a clean no-evidence attempt still cleans up normally and does not create a preserve artifact.
3. `TestWorkLoopNoEvidenceReportsPreserveLocation` proves `ddx work` output/bead event includes the preserve ref or patch path for dirty no-evidence attempts.
4. `TestRealGitOpsWorktreeRemoveReportsFailure` proves `RealGitOps.WorktreeRemove` returns an error when `git worktree remove --force` fails instead of swallowing it.
5. `cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... -run 'TestExecuteBead_DirtyNoEvidencePreservesAttempt|TestExecuteBead_CleanNoEvidenceStillCleansWorktree|TestWorkLoopNoEvidenceReportsPreserveLocation|TestRealGitOpsWorktreeRemoveReportsFailure' -count=1` passes.

","archived_at":"2026-07-02T22:52:12Z","closing_commit_sha":"8cde0b39a03e58d509551e8de4a4c12fabb5cc72","created_at":"2026-06-13T23:42:40.773782316Z","description":"PROBLEM

During dogfood on 2026-06-13, `ddx work --once` for ddx-c02ce93c ran a provider attempt that edited implementation files and exited 0, but produced no commit and no `no_changes_rationale.txt`. DDx reported `no_evidence_produced` with dirty paths, then removed the isolated attempt worktree. The operator could not rescue the provider patch from the worktree and had to manually reimplement from partial observed output. A worker surface that destroys dirty provider output after a successful process exit is not acceptable: DDx must preserve or export recoverable evidence for underspecified provider/commit failures.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE

- `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go:1416-1427` classifies clean-exit/no-commit/no-rationale as `task_no_evidence` and records dirty paths, but it does not convert dirty no-evidence into a preserved recoverable attempt.
- `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go:943-954` defers attempt cleanup for all non-preserved worktree attempts; because `task_no_evidence` is not preserved, cleanup proceeds.
- `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go:1577-1588` removes every non-success attempt worktree unless a preserve flag is set, including dirty `task_no_evidence` attempts.
- `cli/internal/agent/gitops_real.go:35-37` ignores `git worktree remove --force` errors, so cleanup failures would also be underreported.

PROPOSED FIX

Treat dirty `task_no_evidence` as recoverable provider output: before cleanup, preserve the worktree with a `refs/ddx/iterations/<bead>/<attempt>` ref or export a patch/artifact that contains every dirty path and enough metadata to reconstruct the attempt. Worker output and bead events must name the preserve ref or patch path. Only clean no-evidence attempts may be removed normally.

IN-SCOPE FILES

- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go
- cli/internal/agent/gitops_real.go
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_integration_test.go
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_e2e_test.go

NON-SCOPE

Do not change legitimate `no_changes_rationale.txt` handling. Do not preserve clean no-evidence attempts without dirty paths. Do not kill or change provider subprocess supervision; ddx-c02ce93c handled that separately. Do not hand-edit `.ddx/beads.jsonl`.

DOGFOOD EVIDENCE

Attempt `20260613T225213-a01c938c` for ddx-c02ce93c exited 0 after editing `cli/cmd/work_status_test.go`, `cli/internal/agent/agent_runner_service.go`, `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go`, `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go`, provider child files, liveness/status files, and then DDx deleted `/Users/erik/Projects/.ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-ddx-c02ce93c-20260613T225213-a01c938c`.

.ddx/beads.jsonl:463:{"acceptance":"1. Add Niflheim-evidence regression fixtures or table-driven cases under `cli/internal/agent/...` that encode the four observed scenario families from this bead: empty review success, land/ref/index/evidence-staging failure, decomposed-parent no-changes rationale with child beads, and repeated pre-claim system-unready warning. The fixtures must be self-contained and must not reference Niflheim paths as required inputs.
2. `TestExecuteBeadWorker_NiflheimEvidence_EmptyReviewResultCannotClose` proves a non-skipped success path with `cycle_trace.review_result={}` or an equivalent empty `ReviewResult` cannot close a bead and records `review_error` or operator-attention with a non-empty reason.
3. `TestExecuteBeadWorker_NiflheimEvidence_NilReviewerRequiresExplicitSkip` updates the current nil-reviewer behavior so `Reviewer: nil` only closes when an explicit durable no-review/skip reason is present; otherwise it fails review gating.
4. `TestExecuteBeadLoop_NiflheimEvidence_LandCoordinationFailureDoesNotRerunImplementation` simulates both observed details (`cannot lock ref refs/heads/main` after a candidate/merge is already landed, and `landing worktree has staged changes after waiting 2s`) and asserts the action is reconcile/retry-land/operator-attention, not a fresh implementation run and not model escalation.
5. `TestExecuteBeadLoop_NiflheimEvidence_DecomposedParentIsExecutionIneligible` simulates `mixed_commit_and_no_changes_rationale` with `orchestrator_action: decompose`, child bead IDs, and `outcome_reason=decomposed`; it asserts the parent is marked execution-ineligible/proposed or dependency-waiting according to policy and that no further implementation attempt or min-power escalation is scheduled.
6. `TestWorkRetryEscalation_NiflheimEvidence_OnlySemanticFailuresRaiseMinPower` proves only semantic acceptance/review failures can increase requested min power/profile. Land coordination, repeated pre-claim system warnings, decomposed-parent outcomes, and dirty generated DDx evidence must not raise min power.
7. `TestExecuteBeadResult_NiflheimEvidence_CycleTraceCarriesDecisionAudit` proves result JSON/event bodies include `failure_class`, `retry_action`, `escalation_count`, requested/actual routing facts, review status/verdict or explicit skip reason, and land/reconcile status for the Niflheim scenario families.
8. Existing related behavior from `ddx-2965925e`, `ddx-b2846610`, and `ddx-d67f124e` remains covered; this bead either reuses their helpers or waits for those dependencies, and does not add duplicate low-level implementations.
9. `cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... ./cmd/... -run 'NiflheimEvidence|EmptyReviewResult|NilReviewer|LandCoordination|DecomposedParent|RetryEscalation|CycleTraceCarriesDecisionAudit' -count=1` passes.

A live Niflheim worker audit on 2026-06-06 showed that `ddx work` is not yet a stable release-grade autonomous worker. Strong-model escalation exists, but it is not reliably tied to the class of failure. The worker spent repeated attempts on structural or system failures that stronger models cannot fix, while automatic review evidence was empty on attempts that were recorded as successful. This violates the DDx engineering principle "Cheapest Viable, Escalate on Evidence" (docs/dev/engineering-principles.md:39-45): review, runtime, or retry evidence should determine escalation, and only lower-power inability to meet acceptance criteria should push to stronger routes. It also threatens FEAT-010 task-execution reliability because beads can be retried, escalated, or closed without a typed, auditable reason.

This bead is a Niflheim incident/regression closure bead. It is not a duplicate of the narrower queue items listed under INTERSECTIONS; instead it captures the external evidence DDx workers will not otherwise have, and requires a DDx-side regression suite proving those observed scenarios classify correctly before rerun, escalation, operator attention, or close.

BACKGROUND AND OBSERVED NIFLHEIM EVIDENCE
The evidence was gathered from Niflheim worker logs and result bundles, but those files are not load-bearing for this bead. The relevant facts are copied here because an isolated DDx worker cannot access the Niflheim repository or `/tmp` logs.

1. Decomposed parent kept consuming attempts and escalated to a stronger model anyway.
- Niflheim bead: `niflheim-64217cc5`, title "Instrument cold replicator with structured per-operation S3 tracing".
- Observed result bundle: `.ddx/executions/20260606T152006-a4ca7254/result.json` plus worker logs from 2026-06-05/2026-06-06.
- Routing showed escalation exists: repeated routes started at `gpt-5.4-mini` with requested min powers 5, 7, 8, and 9, then reached `gpt-5.5` at requested min power 10.
- Metrics on the bead showed `attempt_count=24`, `total_tokens=19371353`, and average duration about `244112ms`.
- Despite triage recommending `close_decomposed_or_mark_execution_ineligible`, retries continued until the latest event recorded:
  `mixed_commit_and_no_changes_rationale`
  `powerClass=standard`
  `rationale: status: open`
  `orchestrator_action: decompose`
  `reason: parent bead exceeds the Step 0 size threshold and is already decomposed into focused child beads for tracing, counters, tests, and runbook work.`
  `child_beads: niflheim-f03c61a2, niflheim-df407aa8, niflheim-7a421ede, niflheim-0bbed6c9`
  `outcome_reason=decomposed`
- Expected DDx behavior: once the worker has reliable evidence that the bead is a decomposed parent or execution-ineligible parent, no further implementation attempt or model escalation should occur. It should mark execution-ineligible/proposed/operator-attention according to policy and move on.

2. Land/evidence coordination failures were treated like implementation failure and retried expensively.
- Niflheim bead: `niflheim-c75aba30`, title "Materializer per-merge memory footprint OOMs even at concurrent_limit=8".
- Two expensive attempts both used `gpt-5.4-mini`; no useful stronger-model escalation was observed.
- Metrics showed `attempt_count=2`, `total_tokens=65513510`, and average duration about `3306461.5ms`.
- First attempt result: `.ddx/executions/20260606T113550-7b29ee42/result.json`.
  - `status=execution_failed`, `outcome=error`.
  - Implementation result commit was `7aaeb0712...`; a merge commit `2c6f4d6e6` was later observed as an ancestor of HEAD, but the result still recorded a land coordination failure.
  - Error detail:
    `land coordination failed: land advancer: restoring refs/heads/main to ba2d3059f14e834dcb147f2a6db3b294d83b0fac after evidence commit failed: git update-ref refs/heads/main: fatal: update_ref failed for ref 'refs/heads/main': cannot lock ref 'refs/heads/main': is at 5a176b332b182f2a959614f63769d835c47ffd97 but expected 2c6f4d6e6961b8a93742cde66bceec056934655a`
  - `cycle_trace[0].final_decision="success"` and `cycle_trace[0].review_result={}`.
- Second attempt result: `.ddx/executions/20260606T142223-94f8f0f8/result.json`.
  - `status=execution_failed`, `outcome=error`.
  - Error detail:
    `land coordination failed: land advancer: landing worktree has staged changes after waiting 2s: A .ddx/executions/20260606T150832-af40450b/result.json`
  - `cycle_trace[0].final_decision="success"` and `cycle_trace[0].review_result={}`.
- At observation time the worker had claimed it again (`20260606T152024-a21d8c54`) and edited six files in a fresh isolated worktree:
  `crates/niflheim-core/src/config/mod.rs`, `crates/niflheim-core/src/config/tests.rs`, `crates/niflheim-core/src/config/types.rs`, `crates/niflheim-delta-writer/src/materializer/config.rs`, `crates/niflheim-delta-writer/src/materializer/executor.rs`, and `crates/niflheim-delta-writer/src/materializer/tests.rs`.
- Expected DDx behavior: land/ref/index/evidence-staging failures are system or coordination failures, not model-quality failures. The retry policy should reconcile a successfully landed candidate when possible, retry only the land/evidence step when safe, or park/operator-attention the bead when the landing worktree is dirty. It should not blindly rerun implementation at the same or stronger route.

3. Pre-claim intake/system dirt caused repeated warning churn instead of a clear operator lane.
- Niflheim bead: `niflheim-a62d31ce`, title "Publish competitive benchmark artifacts and provenance metadata".
- A live worker repeatedly ran readiness/intake. The log showed readiness timeout after five minutes, then:
  `preserved pre-dispatch implementation changes in /home/erik/Projects/niflheim under refs/ddx/pre-dispatch/20260606T153517-f7a32618; released niflheim-a62d31ce and continuing watch. dirty paths: .ddx/harness-sessions/-2929681.json. recover with git stash apply refs/ddx/pre-dispatch/20260606T153517-f7a32618`
- Bead events included `pre_claim_intake.warn` with timeout/system-unready reason and generic suggested action.
- Expected DDx behavior: repeated same/systemic pre-claim warnings and dirty generated DDx artifacts should become an operator-visible/system-unready state after the configured threshold, not an indefinite poll/release loop.

4. Automatic reviews were not forcing meaningful quality improvement.
- Recent Niflheim "success" attempts had empty review results:
  - `niflheim-9dea95da`, result `.ddx/executions/20260606T043655-845c77ee/result.json`: closed success, `cycle_trace.review_result={}`, `final_decision="success"`, model `gpt-5.4-mini`, result commit `78e51a168...`.
  - `niflheim-da4e1311`, result `.ddx/executions/20260606T085358-d80ada3e/result.json`: closed success, `cycle_trace.review_result={}`, `final_decision="success"`, model `gpt-5.4-mini`, result commit `d43be3a3...`.
  - `niflheim-c75aba30` attempts listed above: `cycle_trace.final_decision="success"` with `review_result={}` despite overall land failures.
- Manual Claude review earlier improved bead wording and completeness, but the automatic worker path did not show enforceable per-AC review evidence.
- Expected DDx behavior: unless review is explicitly skipped with a durable reason (`runtime.NoReview` or a valid `review:skip` + reason policy), an empty review result must be a review error or operator-attention signal. It must not be treated as approval, success, or evidence of quality improvement.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- `cli/internal/agent/failclass/failclass.go:103-142` contains the normative routing table: only `OutcomeGenuineFailure` can escalate; dirty worktree, transport, quota, auth, setup, timeout, and merge-conflict do not escalate. The live Niflheim behavior shows execute-bead/work outcomes are not consistently reduced to this typed table before retry/escalation decisions.
- `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_orchestrator.go:305-309` wraps `LandingAdvancer` errors as generic `land advancer` errors. The caller can record an overall `execution_failed` without distinguishing landed-candidate reconciliation, ref/index contention, staged evidence dirt, or dirty landing worktree from implementation failure.
- `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:1967-2072` emits pre-claim timeout/system-unready outcomes as warn-only by default and continues implementation when strict intake blocking is off. This permits repeated same-fingerprint/system warnings to look like a live worker instead of a system-unready/operator lane.
- `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:2175-2315` handles `PreClaimIntakeTooLargeDecomposed`, but missing/empty decomposition paths can warn and continue. `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:3826-3831` can recognize execution-ineligible/decomposed state only after the bead has the right metadata. The Niflheim parent evidence shows structural/decomposed-parent outcomes can still reach repeated implementation attempts and power escalation.
- `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:3042-3138` treats success disposition/status as closeable once candidate-cycle review/landing has allowed success, and the old post-merge review is optional. `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_review_test.go:799-823` explicitly proves a worker with `Reviewer: nil` can skip review and close the bead. `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_review.go:168-193` defines `ReviewResult` with all fields optional except the zero-value enum field; no invariant rejects `{}` as a success-path review artifact.

PROPOSED FIX
- Add compact Niflheim-style regression fixtures under `cli/internal/agent/testdata/niflheim_worker_evidence/` or equivalent. The fixtures should encode the four scenario families above: empty review success, land coordination/ref-lock/staged-evidence failure, decomposed-parent no-changes rationale with child beads, and repeated pre-claim system-unready warning. Do not depend on Niflheim checkout paths.
- Introduce or tighten a single execute-bead retry classification boundary that maps attempt result, land result, review result, no-changes rationale, and pre-claim intake outcome into exactly one action: close, retry same tier, escalate min power/profile, reconcile land/evidence, execution-ineligible/proposed, or operator-attention. Wire this boundary into `ddx work`/`ddx try` so `failclass.Classify` is not merely a standalone helper.
- Enforce review evidence on success. For non-skipped success paths, `cycle_trace.review_result` or the equivalent result field must contain a non-empty verdict, per-AC evidence or documented reason, reviewer routing/session metadata when available, and a classification. `{}` is invalid and must prevent close or produce a review-error/operator-attention event. Explicit review skips remain allowed only when they are durable, reasoned, and visible in result JSON.
- Classify land/ref/index/evidence-staging failures separately from implementation/model failures. If the candidate commit or merge is already landed or is an ancestor of the target, reconcile and close/continue without rerunning implementation. If the landing worktree has staged generated evidence or DDx metadata, retry/preserve the land/evidence step or move to operator attention according to the existing local reliability policy; do not escalate the model for this class.
- Classify decomposed-parent/no-changes rationale as execution-ineligible or operator work before another implementation attempt. Stronger models cannot fix parent beads that are already split into executable children.
- Make worker result JSON and event bodies carry enough audit fields to explain the decision: `failure_class`, `retry_action`, `escalation_count`, requested/actual power/profile/model, review status/verdict, and land/reconcile status.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not change Fizeau route selection or hard-code concrete provider/model names. DDx should continue to pass power/profile intent and let Fizeau choose concrete routes.
- Do not edit Niflheim beads or Niflheim implementation code.
- Do not duplicate the low-level index-contention fix tracked by `ddx-2965925e`; reuse or align with it.
- Do not duplicate the post-commit review/land timeout work tracked by `ddx-b2846610`; this bead requires typed classification and review evidence, not another timeout implementation.
- Do not duplicate the repeated pre-claim warn operator-attention bead `ddx-d67f124e`; this bead uses the Niflheim observation as cross-scenario regression coverage.
- Do not implement a full operator-lane product workflow beyond the state transitions/events needed to keep the worker from blind retry or false close.

INTERSECTIONS
- Parent: `ddx-3106674f` (multi-worker hardening and local reliability gates). This bead is the external Niflheim regression/incident closure layer under that reliability epic.
- Dependency: `ddx-2965925e` covers tracker commit index contention. This bead must not reimplement that fix; it must prove land/ref/index/evidence failures classify separately from model failure once the low-level path is hardened.
- Dependency: `ddx-b2846610` covers bounded post-commit review/land. This bead must still require typed review/land outcomes after the timeout wedge is fixed.
- Dependency: `ddx-d67f124e` covers same-fingerprint pre-claim warning escalation. This bead adds the Niflheim `niflheim-a62d31ce` family as regression evidence.
- Related but not a dependency: `ddx-e1a576a7` is an execution-ineligible decomposed parent and a local precedent for the `niflheim-64217cc5` class.

2. lefthook run pre-commit passes.","archived_at":"2026-07-02T22:52:12Z","created_at":"2026-06-07T02:17:59.120820292Z","description":"PROBLEM
Parent bead ddx-130cf9ab is too broad for one execution pass. This child owns part 3 of 3 of that parent scope.

ROOT CAUSE
PROBLEM
Parent bead ddx-e19185aa is too broad for one execution pass. This child owns part 2 of 3 of that parent scope.

ROOT CAUSE
The decomposition action (currently invoked when a bead is too large for one execution and the agent splits it into child beads) is writing the parent's ID as a dep edge on every child. This produces an unbreakable deadlock:

  e29892a4 (parent, open) -- parent --> child_A
  e29892a4 -- parent --> child_B
  child_A.deps = [e29892a4]           <- BAD: parent is also a dep
  child_B.deps = [child_A, e29892a4]  <- BAD

The parent stays open until its children close. Every child is blocked on the parent. Nothing can be claimed. ddx work loops on the parent, the orchestrator emits 'mixed_commit_and_no_changes_rationale', the worker retries, and burns claim budget without progress.

Observed scenario (niflheim, 2026-06-04 04:30-05:13 UTC):
- Parent: niflheim-e29892a4 ('feat(delta): async write-behind ...')
- 10 children created during planning loops (23223661, a44a8ceb, 054c89b7, 0470ba0d, f10e9e35, f076b08c, 6c041162, 94e1df73, 9da62c2d, 5e4f9fb2)
- Every child carried deps=[..siblings.., niflheim-e29892a4]
- ~50 minutes of agent time + 4 strong-policy claude/opus attempts with zero feature commits; only tracker chores
- Unwedged manually by closing the parent via 'ddx bead close', after which siblings drained correctly in foundational order

Root cause is structural, not model-capability — auto-upgrading to a stronger model does NOT fix this. The orchestrator already detects the no-progress state but its only remediation is 'retry parent', which re-enters the same loop.

There is also a 'project root mutation rejected (continuing)' warning emitted by the readiness check during this state; that's a downstream symptom of the same problem, not the cause.

PROPOSED FIX
Implement only this child acceptance slice:
- Programmatic decomposition path (the action that fans a too-large bead into children) MUST NOT add the parent as a dep. Existing action templates / system prompts are audited and amended; one golden e2e test (decompose_does_not_self_dep) creates a bead with N children and asserts every child's deps set excludes the parent.
- 'ddx bead doctor' (existing subcommand) gains a check 'parent_ancestor_in_deps' that scans the tracker, lists violating beads, and (with --fix) removes the offending edges. Output is stable / scriptable.
...[truncated]

PROPOSED FIX
Implement only this child acceptance slice:
- cd cli && go test ./... passes.
- lefthook run pre-commit passes.

NON-SCOPE
Do not implement parent acceptance criteria assigned to sibling child beads. Preserve every parent non-scope constraint unless this child explicitly narrows it.","events":[{"actor":"erik","body":"{"decision":"warn","decision_source":"pre_claim_intake","detail":"readiness route rationale: Bead ddx-3b06699f asks for a TD on axon-backed bead-tracker integration.
That TD already exists at docs/helix/02-design/technical-designs/TD-030-axon-bead-backend.md
(committed by sibling bead ddx-eb7d5981, commit 5740627f). It satisfies every AC:

AC1 (TD under docs/): TD-030-axon-bead-backend.md.
AC2 (coverage):
  - data model mapping → "Schema mapping" + "Schema artifacts" sections
  - claim/lock semantics → "Concurrency model" (OCC via expected_version, multi-worker fairness)
  - archive policy → migration §2 ingests .ddx/beads.jsonl, .ddx/beads-archive.jsonl,
    .ddx/attachments/*; "What this design fixes" notes ADR-004 archive split is subsumed
    by the two-collection model (ddx_beads + ddx_bead_events per D3)
  - JSONL interchange → operation-mapping rows for `ddx bead export` / `ddx bead import`,
    cites FEAT-004 round-trip + schema_compat_test.go
  - failure modes → D4 offline posture, `ddx doctor` diagnostic with four named failure modes,
    test plan §5–§6
  - migration story → "Migration plan" (6-step, idempotent, with rollback bak file),
    references B_MIGRATE child bead ddx-155204fd
AC3 (cites): ADR-004 (lines 5, 15, 213, 266, 304), SD-004 (lines 6, 15, 73, 306),
  ddx-da11a34a (lines 156, 247, 264, 274).
AC4 (open questions): "Open questions for the Axon team" §1 (server-side ready aggregation)
  and §2 (tsnet alignment with ADR-006); v1-policy table also flags deferred decisions
  (schema versioning, ready query, backup/DR) explicitly revisitable.

Producing a duplicate TD-### file would split the design surface and create drift.
No code or doc changes warranted on this attempt.

.ddx/beads.jsonl:540:{"acceptance":"execute-bead prompt contract explicitly invites no_changes with a rationale. The fake-agent test harness seeds a no_changes rationale file; execute_bead_test.go asserts ExecuteBeadResult.NoChangesRationale is populated; work uses the rationale to classify 'already_satisfied' when it references a prior commit SHA or test name; the rendered log (ddx server workers log) shows the rationale on its own line; 'agent made no commits' no longer appears without context.","archived_at":"2026-05-07T04:29:14Z","claimed-at":"2026-04-14T08:21:48Z","claimed-machine":"eitri","claimed-pid":"1412283","closing_commit_sha":"f495bf65ad5e6906f85e7c5260ce12f8c76a20be","created_at":"2026-04-14T04:03:21.715664968Z","description":"Today when execute-bead exits with no_changes, the operator sees 'agent made no commits' and nothing else. That's useless for triage — the operator cannot tell whether:\n- the bead is genuinely already satisfied (work present in a prior commit)\n- the agent gave up because it couldn't find the relevant files\n- the agent classified the task as ambiguous or too risky\n- the agent hit a tool limit or context cap\n- the prompt contract was unclear\n\nThe fix is to make no_changes a structured result that carries a short rationale from the agent itself, written at the point where the agent decided not to commit. Example of what the operator should see:\n\n  status:    no_changes\n  rationale: The epic filter requested by this bead was already added in\n             commit 1da6495 (cli/internal/bead/store.go readyFiltered). I\n             reviewed the current code and tests, confirmed the filter\n             excludes 'epic' beads from ReadyExecution, and verified\n             TestReadyExecutionExcludesEpics exists and passes. Nothing\n             to do. Ref: 1da6495, TestReadyExecutionExcludesEpics.\n\nThis is specifically the case that motivated this bead: a continuous-drain worker processed ddx-b44ca394 ('Prevent work from launching open epics by default') after the epic filter had already been implemented on main. The agent instead made a synthesized no-op commit that only retargeted 17 .claude/skills/helix-* symlinks, which the loop classified as 'success' because a commit was produced. Had no_changes carried a rationale pointing at the prior commit, the loop could have closed the bead as 'already_satisfied' and moved on cleanly.\n\nScope:\n1. Execute-bead prompt contract must explicitly instruct the agent: if you determine the bead's work is already present or cannot be done, report that in a structured way — do NOT make a synthesized commit to 'land something'. Today the prompt says 'Making no commits (no_changes) should be rare' which actively pushes agents toward the wrong behavior.\n2. The agent's exit mechanism needs a way to carry a rationale string back. Simplest version: the agent writes a file to the worktree like .ddx/executions/<attempt-id>/no_changes_rationale.txt before exiting. ExecuteBead reads it if no commits were produced and attaches the text to the result.NoChangesRationale field.\n3. The ExecuteBeadResult struct gains a NoChangesRationale string field, populated only when outcome == no_changes. Status: success and the unused 'reason' field need not change.\n4. work close semantics: when no_changes arrives with a non-empty rationale pointing at a prior commit, the loop can optionally close the bead as already_satisfied with the rationale recorded in the close event body (instead of waiting for MaxNoChangesBeforeClose=3 strikes). Gate this behind a 'rationale references existing commit' heuristic to avoid flapping — if the rationale is a vague 'nothing to do', keep current cooldown behavior.\n5. ddx server worker log should render the rationale prominently, not bury it in session metadata.\n6. The prompt contract note that no_changes should be 'rare' is replaced with: 'If the work is already present, say so and provide a reference (commit SHA, test name, file path). A well-justified no_changes is always preferred to a cosmetic synthesized commit.'","events":[{"actor":"ddx","body":"success

.ddx/beads.jsonl:546:{"acceptance":"1. cli/internal/docgraph/docgraph.go excludes any document path under a root-relative hidden path segment (`.^`/basename starts with `.`), even when a graph config root points directly inside that hidden subtree.
2. Existing canonical docs under `docs/` are still indexed by BuildGraph and BuildGraphWithConfig.
3. TestBuildGraph_ExcludesConfiguredHiddenRoots (or equivalent) in cli/internal/docgraph/docgraph_test.go creates canonical `docs/feat.md` plus duplicate `.agents/skills/docs/feat.md`, configures a root inside `.agents/skills/docs`, and verifies only the canonical doc is indexed or the hidden root yields zero files with no duplicate_id.
4. Existing TestBuildGraph_ExcludesDDxPluginsTree remains green or is replaced by a broader hidden-root regression test that covers `.ddx/plugins` as one case.
5. `ddx doc audit` from the repository root no longer reports duplicate_id issues caused by `.agents/skills/docs`, `.claude`, `.ddx`, `.skills`, or other hidden root segments.
6. `cd cli && go test ./internal/docgraph/... -run "TestBuildGraph_ExcludesConfiguredHiddenRoots|TestBuildGraph_ExcludesDDxPluginsTree|TestBuildGraph_ExcludesClaudeWorktreesAndStoresRelativePaths" -count=1` passes.
7. `lefthook run pre-commit` passes.","archived_at":"2026-05-07T04:29:14Z","closing_commit_sha":"39d42832d25304c433a1ebfc864bfa1db5e263df","created_at":"2026-05-06T01:02:47.265762471Z","description":"PROBLEM
  `ddx doc audit` can still index duplicate/stale Markdown documents from hidden tool-managed trees when graph roots are configured inside those trees. The observed dogfood failure is `.agents/skills/docs/...` being indexed alongside canonical `docs/...`, producing duplicate_id noise even though root-level hidden directories should never be part of the document graph.

ROOT CAUSE
  cli/internal/docgraph/docgraph.go:540-548 accepts configured roots verbatim and starts `filepath.Walk` at each configured root. cli/internal/docgraph/docgraph.go:569-578 skips hidden/tool-managed directories only when the walker encounters the directory entry. If the configured root is already inside a hidden segment such as `.agents/skills/docs`, that basename skip never fires. cli/internal/docgraph/docgraph.go:556-568 adds a one-off path-based defense for `.ddx/plugins`, and cli/internal/docgraph/docgraph_test.go:630-677 only tests that narrow `.ddx/plugins` case.

PROPOSED FIX
  - Replace or generalize the `.ddx/plugins`-specific path defense with a repository-relative hidden-segment exclusion used before accepting either directories or files.
  - Any path under a root-relative segment whose basename begins with `.` must be excluded from docgraph indexing, including configured roots that start inside `.agents/`, `.claude/`, `.ddx/`, `.skills/`, or future hidden tool directories.
  - Keep the existing `worktrees` directory skip for non-hidden scratch directories.
  - Add regression coverage for a configured root that points directly at `.agents/skills/docs` and asserts no files/documents from that root are indexed and no duplicate_id issue is produced.
  - Update FEAT-005/FEAT-007 doc discovery wording only if it still describes hidden/tool-managed trees ambiguously.

NON-SCOPE
  - Do not hand-edit `.ddx/beads.jsonl`.
  - Do not delete `.agents/skills/docs` or other installed skill/plugin content as part of this fix.
  - Do not change document frontmatter IDs merely to silence duplicates from hidden trees.

PARENT
  ddx-781af038 (Story 4 / docgraph integrity lineage; follows the incomplete narrower fix in ddx-2502ad71).

DEPS

rationale: verification_command: cd cli && go test ./internal/docgraph/... -run 'TestBuildGraph_ExcludesConfiguredHiddenRoots|TestBuildGraph_ExcludesDDxPluginsTree|TestBuildGraph_ExcludesClaudeWorktreesAndStoresRelativePaths' -count=1 && go run . doc audit && cd .. && lefthook run pre-commit

What is done:
- The current source tree already implements the hidden-segment exclusion in `cli/internal/docgraph/docgraph.go`.
- Regression coverage is already present in `cli/internal/docgraph/docgraph_test.go` for configured hidden roots and the `.ddx/plugins` case.
- The source-built audit command reports a clean graph from the repo/module root.

What was blocking:
- The local `ddx` shell binary in this environment is not reflecting the current source tree, so the shell command `ddx doc audit` showed stale duplicate_id output even though `go run . doc audit` is clean.

What a follow-up would need:
- No code change is required in this worktree.
- If a future run still observes stale output, it should use the source-built CLI path above rather than the preinstalled binary.

Existing land/divergence tests still pass. 3. cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... passes; go build ./... succeeds.","created_at":"2026-06-28T03:08:04.1203069Z","description":"PROBLEM
Agents keep tripping the execute-bead one-commit/no-amend contract (the implementation commit is rewritten/amended/rebased after the first commit). Today this is DETECTED post-hoc and the whole attempt is discarded and re-run, wasting long, expensive runs (observed 3047s / 15.3M-token attempts thrown away).

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_land.go (divergence/ancestry resolution, ~line 187 and ~910-927): a diverged/rewritten candidate is resolved by aborting the claim (detect-then-fail), not by preventing or correcting the rewrite.

PROPOSED FIX
- Prevent or auto-correct the contract violation rather than discarding the attempt: e.g. detect a post-first-commit rewrite before land and reconcile it (or instruct/guard the harness to not amend) so a successful candidate is preserved instead of re-run from scratch.
- This is DISTINCT from the mixed_commit_and_no_changes_rationale detector in execute_bead.go:~1419 (commit+rationale mismatch). Do NOT target that path.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not relax the audit-trail rule (never squash/rebase/filter landed attempt history).
- Do not touch the mixed_commit detector.
- Do not change Fizeau routing.

PROVENANCE
Derived from a session-harvest of local Claude/Codex operator prompts (2026-06-27), classified into deficiency-papering vs intent, then put through two adversarial-review rounds that dropped every item already shipped. This item survived as a verified, non-redundant gap. See docs/session-harvest-playbook.md.","id":"ddx-3c865b16","issue_type":"task","labels":["area:agent","area:execute-bead","kind:reliability","source:session-harvest-2026-06-27"],"priority":2,"schema_version":1,"status":"open","title":"Make execute-bead auto-comply with the one-commit/no-amend contract","updated_at":"2026-06-28T03:08:04.1203069Z"}

9. lefthook run pre-commit passes.","archived_at":"2026-05-15T00:08:56Z","closing_commit_sha":"4386b853238b444647603a77e58da0e744369010","created_at":"2026-05-09T19:52:26.846044896Z","description":"PROBLEM
  Today's Land() in cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_land.go performs git
  fetch, push --ff-only, and push-race auto-recovery as part of the core
  drain flow. This causes 30-120s lock holds spanning network I/O, which
  in turn produced ddx-08e90869 (work landed only on a preserved iteration
  ref because tracker lock contention prevented the main merge) and
  ddx-15a51f8c (push-race remediation commit). The user's architectural
  position is: Land() should perform only the worktree-merge into local
  main; origin-sync (fetch) and push are separate concerns, operator-driven
  or background-deferred, never coupled to Land() or the drain.

  The spec corpus partially reflects this position already:
  FEAT-012-git-awareness.md:134 declares "DDx does not invoke `git push` at
  all." But FEAT-010 (the governing feature for task execution) does not
  state the network-free boundary as a requirement, and the multi-machine
  docs (API-001 §"Land And Preserve Semantics", SD-020) describe a
  push/fetch-coupled lifecycle that is correct for distributed mode but
  contradicts the single-node invariant.

  This is a doc-only bead. It lands the requirement in the right anchor
  spec, then reconciles every cross-reference so the spec corpus is
  coherent and complete. Implementation removal of push/fetch from Land()
  is a separate sister bead (ddx-1f774edf, rewritten in parallel).

ROOT CAUSE
  - docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-010-task-execution.md ##Requirements
    section (around line 1057) enumerates functional + non-functional
    requirements but has no "Network isolation" or "Sync decoupling"
    requirement.
  - docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-012-git-awareness.md:134 declares the
    correct invariant ("DDx does not invoke `git push` at all") but has no
    cross-reference to FEAT-010 or to the implementation status.
  - docs/helix/02-design/solution-designs/SD-025-task-execution-lifecycle.md
    Layer 2/3 sections do not mention push/fetch — silent on the boundary,
    so implementers may inadvertently reintroduce network I/O.
  - docs/helix/02-design/contracts/API-001-execute-bead-supervisor-contract.md:221-225
    §"Land And Preserve Semantics" prescribes "fetch origin before rebase"
    and "push --ff-only ... atomic ref-update is the serialization point".
    This was written for the multi-machine coordinator topology and
    contradicts the single-node invariant.
  - docs/helix/02-design/solution-designs/SD-020-multi-machine-coordinator-topology.md
    lines 51, 60, 79, 83, 117, 123, 136, 155 reference git push/fetch as
    part of the lifecycle; these are correctly scoped to multi-machine but
    need an explicit preamble distinguishing single-node from multi-machine.
  - docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-023-sync.md exists but does not
    explicitly position itself as the designated home for origin-sync
    semantics complementing FEAT-010's drain boundary.
  - docs/helix/06-iterate/reliability-principles.md enumerates P1-P8
    execution-loop invariants; the network-free drain is the natural P9.

PROPOSED FIX
  All edits are doc-only:

  1. FEAT-010: under ##Requirements, add a new functional requirement:
     "Network-free drain boundary: Land() performs only the worktree-merge
     into local main, under a brief local lock. Origin-sync (git fetch +
     merging origin/main into local main) and origin-push (git push) are
     separate operations, operator-driven or background-deferred, and are
     never invoked from Land() or from the drain loop."
     Plus a non-functional requirement bullet:
     "Network isolation: single-developer/single-node drain does not block
     on remote I/O; origin sync and push are decoupled and may run in
     parallel or in the background without holding the drain or land lock."

  2. FEAT-012:134 — keep the existing line; add a cross-reference sentence
     pointing to FEAT-010's new requirement and noting the implementation
     gap is being closed by the sister implementation bead.

  3. SD-025 — under Layer 2 (bead attempt) and Layer 3 (queue drain), add a
     one-sentence cross-reference: "Per FEAT-010 network-free drain
     boundary, this layer performs no network I/O; origin-sync and push are
     out-of-band per FEAT-023."

  4. API-001:221-225 §"Land And Preserve Semantics" — add an explicit
     preamble: "These semantics describe the multi-machine coordinator
     topology (SD-020). For single-node mode (FEAT-010 default), Land() is
     local-only; the fetch+push lifecycle described below applies only when
     multi-machine coordination is active."

  5. SD-020 — at the top of the doc, add a one-paragraph preamble making
     the single-node vs multi-machine distinction explicit, with a pointer
     to FEAT-010.

  6. FEAT-023-sync.md — add a sentence positioning it as the designated
     home for origin-sync semantics, complementing FEAT-010's drain
     boundary. Defer the actual `ddx sync` helper to the future
     multi-machine work; this is just a naming/scoping clarification.

  7. reliability-principles.md — add P9 "Network-free drain": short
     principle text, file:line citations to FEAT-010 and Land(),
     justification (single-node drain must not block on network I/O).

NON-SCOPE
  - The implementation removal of push/fetch from Land() and the
    LandingGitOps interface (sister bead ddx-1f774edf, rewritten).
  - Designing or building a `ddx sync` helper (downstream of multi-node
    work; the user explicitly deferred this).
  - Updating prompt templates / eval data / closed beads that reference
    push_failed semantics (downstream impact, separate beads).
  - Changing TD-031 (state machine is unaffected).
  - Changing ADR-022 (worker client-server is orthogonal).

INTERSECTIONS
  - Sister implementation bead ddx-1f774edf depends on this landing.
  - Future multi-node work (FEAT-026 / SD-020 build-out) will reintroduce
    coordinated network I/O with its own design; this bead is a temporary
    scoping that explicitly says "single-node only" so future-spec authors
    can layer multi-node semantics on top.","events":[{"actor":"erik","body":"{"rationale":"","score":0,"suggested_fixes":null,"waivers_applied":null,"warning":"lint hook: dispatch-failure: ResolveRoute: no live provider supports prompt of 0 tokens with tools=false at tier ≥ cheap"}","created_at":"2026-05-09T21:37:42.069901437Z","kind":"bead-quality.lint","source":"ddx agent work","summary":"warning score=0"},{"actor":"ddx","body":"{"resolved_provider":"claude","resolved_model":"sonnet","fallback_chain":[],"requested_harness":"claude","requested_model":"claude-sonnet-4-6"}","created_at":"2026-05-09T21:42:34.103081904Z","kind":"routing","source":"ddx agent execute-bead","summary":"provider=claude model=sonnet"},{"actor":"ddx","body":"{"attempt_id":"20260509T213742-66fa140e","harness":"claude","model":"sonnet","input_tokens":24,"output_tokens":12963,"total_tokens":12987,"cost_usd":0.76411125,"duration_ms":287205,"exit_code":0}","created_at":"2026-05-09T21:42:34.174537739Z","kind":"cost","source":"ddx agent execute-bead","summary":"tokens=12987 cost_usd=0.7641 model=sonnet"},{"actor":"ddx","body":"{"actual_harness":"","actual_model":"","actual_power":0,"actual_provider":"","attempt_id":"","bead_id":"ddx-3d5197d8","rejected_route_pins":null,"requested_tier":"cheap","routing_intent_degraded":true,"routing_intent_note":"actual route facts unavailable","routing_intent_source":"heuristic","smart_justification":""}","created_at":"2026-05-09T21:43:36.196638792Z","kind":"execution-routing-intent","source":"ddx agent work","summary":"source=heuristic tier=cheap note=actual route facts unavailable"},{"actor":"erik","body":"tracker lock timeout (max elapsed, owner pid: 3534627)

- Tests under cli/internal/agent/ and/or cli/cmd/ following existing execute_bead_*_test.go patterns

During the 2026-06-13 dogfood run for ddx-aef128f7, an interrupted `ddx work` execution polluted the real DDx tracker with 20 top-level open beads titled `Sample: ...` (`ddx-5d26dc27`, `ddx-37dd1bef`, `ddx-e7f8ad72`, `ddx-2a77ac3f`, `ddx-d369d255`, `ddx-9646569a`, `ddx-3cf17faf`, `ddx-541bf722`, `ddx-e9384053`, `ddx-2a5286ae`, `ddx-fb8f9a90`, `ddx-552ec9c5`, `ddx-86e2a4ce`, `ddx-0b369a91`, `ddx-2eed7ae3`, `ddx-8c6abcc2`, `ddx-e64dea54`, `ddx-6479485f`, `ddx-8fbd3aba`, `ddx-10c17c2d`). I cancelled those bogus beads through `ddx bead update --status cancelled`, but the worker should never have been able to create them in the landing tracker.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go:1885-1903 tells execute-bead agents that Step 0 may run `ddx bead create` for decomposition. cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go:1981-1984 reiterates that Step 0 allows bead creation while current-bead lifecycle stays orchestrator-owned. The prompt requires `parent=<parent-id>`, but DDx does not enforce that an execute-bead-created child has the current bead as parent, inherits required labels/spec-id, or writes a valid `no_changes_rationale.txt` before mutating the real landing tracker. The dogfood attempt created top-level sample fixtures instead.

PROPOSED FIX
- Add a guarded decomposition command/path for execute-bead Step 0, or validate all bead-create mutations made from an execution worktree before they reach the landing tracker.
- Reject or quarantine execute-bead-created beads whose title/description/AC look like sample fixtures, whose parent is empty or not the current bead, or whose required inherited labels/spec-id are absent.
- Add cleanup/recovery that parks or cancels invalid child beads automatically and records an operator_attention event naming the invalid IDs.
- Ensure stale run-state from a killed worker does not keep `ddx work status` reporting active work after the owning worker process is gone.

NON-SCOPE
Do not remove legitimate Step 0 decomposition. Do not rewrite or delete tracker history manually. Do not change normal operator-invoked `ddx bead create` behavior outside execute-bead/work execution contexts.

INTERSECTIONS
- ddx-aef128f7: the same aborted run also proved process-tree cleanup failures.
- ddx-6d9a8bdb: the same run also proved pinned-provider escape.","id":"ddx-42124c23","issue_type":"bug","labels":["phase:build","area:work","area:beads","area:agent","kind:bug","kind:reliability","severity:critical","plan:server-managed-workers"],"parent":"ddx-9d1af129","priority":0,"queue-rank":"-90","schema_version":1,"spec-id":"IP-2026-06-13-server-managed-workers","status":"closed","title":"work: prevent execute-bead from polluting queue with sample child beads","triage.estimated_difficulty":"hard","updated_at":"2026-06-13T17:11:12.293723582Z"}

ddx work --watch's pre-claim intake decoder fails to unmarshal the readiness_checks response when verdict is a JSON bool. Every claim attempt produces the identical warn-only rejection ("decode result: json: cannot unmarshal bool into Go struct field .readiness_checks.verdict of type string"), so the worker logs a warning and skips. With a full ready queue and a single fizeau-scoped daemon, the entire queue goes idle indefinitely. Fizeau observation 2026-05-18 05:27-05:39 UTC: identical fingerprint 15155a1cd44e96daf53eabb14c8eb3ccd8b0e5271d881208cfcbbf2869899f4f recurred across distinct bead_ids, confirming a schema mismatch rather than a bead-specific issue.

ROOT CAUSE
The verdict field in the readiness-check struct is typed as a plain Go string, so encoding/json rejects a bool payload. See cli/internal/agent/preclaim_intake_hook.go:77-82 (preClaimReadinessCheck struct) and the decode entry point decodePreClaimIntakePayloadResult / decodePreClaimIntakePayloadResultWithMode at cli/internal/agent/preclaim_intake_hook.go:709-725. Producers emit verdict as a JSON bool (true/false) while the consumer parsed string only.

PROPOSED FIX (parent proposed-fix A)
Introduce a custom readinessVerdict type at cli/internal/agent/preclaim_intake_hook.go:95-125 with an UnmarshalJSON that accepts JSON bool, JSON string, and null/absent, coercing each to a single documented canonical form (e.g. bool true -> "pass", false -> "fail", string lowercased, null/absent -> empty). Wire the preClaimReadinessCheck.Verdict field to the new type so the array/object payload decoder at cli/internal/agent/preclaim_intake_hook.go:386-416 succeeds for every verdict shape.

NON-SCOPE
- CI schema drift gate (sibling child: schema lock).
- Same-fingerprint warn escalation / warn-only 100%-failure guard (sibling child: escalation).
- claim-success-rate operator signal (sibling child: claim-rate window).
- README documentation (sibling child: docs).

rationale: Bead ddx-3b06699f asks for a TD on axon-backed bead-tracker integration.
That TD already exists at docs/helix/02-design/technical-designs/TD-030-axon-bead-backend.md
(committed by sibling bead ddx-eb7d5981, commit 5740627f). It satisfies every AC:

AC1 (TD under docs/): TD-030-axon-bead-backend.md.
AC2 (coverage):
  - data model mapping → "Schema mapping" + "Schema artifacts" sections
  - claim/lock semantics → "Concurrency model" (OCC via expected_version, multi-worker fairness)
  - archive policy → migration §2 ingests .ddx/beads.jsonl, .ddx/beads-archive.jsonl,
    .ddx/attachments/*; "What this design fixes" notes ADR-004 archive split is subsumed
    by the two-collection model (ddx_beads + ddx_bead_events per D3)
  - JSONL interchange → operation-mapping rows for `ddx bead export` / `ddx bead import`,
    cites FEAT-004 round-trip + schema_compat_test.go
  - failure modes → D4 offline posture, `ddx doctor` diagnostic with four named failure modes,
    test plan §5–§6
  - migration story → "Migration plan" (6-step, idempotent, with rollback bak file),
    references B_MIGRATE child bead ddx-155204fd
AC3 (cites): ADR-004 (lines 5, 15, 213, 266, 304), SD-004 (lines 6, 15, 73, 306),
  ddx-da11a34a (lines 156, 247, 264, 274).
AC4 (open questions): "Open questions for the Axon team" §1 (server-side ready aggregation)
  and §2 (tsnet alignment with ADR-006); v1-policy table also flags deferred decisions
  (schema versioning, ready query, backup/DR) explicitly revisitable.

Producing a duplicate TD-### file would split the design surface and create drift.
No code or doc changes warranted on this attempt.
