.ddx/attachments/ddx-999eee75/events.jsonl:3:{"actor":"ddx","body":"no_changes_rationale — bead ddx-999eee75 (Stage E2: GraphQL bounded text egress)
================================================================================

(a) WHAT IS DONE
----------------

- Read all in-scope files (schema.graphql, resolver_documents.go,
  resolver_agent.go) plus generated.go, models.go, gqlgen.yml.
- Read the FEAT-022 §10/§11 invariants and the existing E1 work in
  cli/internal/server/server.go and cli/internal/server/state_executions.go
  (uses evidence.ClampOutput + truncated/original_bytes pattern).
- Read the existing TestServerBoundedEgress in
  cli/internal/server/bounded_egress_test.go to understand the fixture
  pattern (lower serverInlineCapBytes → 256, body sized 2× cap, assert
  length cap+marker, truncated, originalBytes).
- Determined the schema-edit + resolver-edit footprint that would be
  required:
    1. schema.graphql: add `truncated: Boolean!` and `originalBytes: Int!`
       to type Document and type AgentSession; introduce a new type
       WorkerPrompt { text: String!, truncated: Boolean!,
       originalBytes: Int! } and change Query.workerPrompt return type
       from String! to WorkerPrompt!.
    2. resolver_documents.go DocumentByPath: route resolved file content
       through evidence.ClampOutput; populate Truncated + OriginalBytes
       on the returned *Document.
    3. resolver_agent.go WorkerPrompt and AgentSession: same treatment
       for prompt body and for agentSession Prompt+Response (matching
       the REST handler's "OriginalBytes is sum of prompt+response"
       pattern from server.go:2472–2480 + bounded_egress_test.go:196–199).
    4. New TestGraphQLBoundedEgress in this package with one subtest per
       in-scope resolver, using newGQLHandler + a custom testStateProvider
       that returns oversized fixtures.
    5. Run gqlgen to refresh generated.go and models.go.

(b) WHAT IS BLOCKING
--------------------

The acceptance contract requires:

    cd cli/internal/server/graphql && go run github.com/99designs/gqlgen generate

to exit 0. With the current package layout this is not achievable, and
it is **not caused by any edit this bead would introduce** — the failure
reproduces from the pristine base revision (e6cad0d6) before any
schema or resolver change:

    $ git checkout e6cad0d6 -- cli/internal/server/graphql/
    $ cd cli/internal/server/graphql && go run github.com/99designs/gqlgen generate
    ./resolver_feat008.go:36:28: method mutationResolver.BeadClose already declared at ./resolver.go:165:28
    ./resolver_feat008.go:58:28: method mutationResolver.WorkerDispatch already declared at ./resolver.go:244:28
    ./resolver_feat008.go:77:28: method mutationResolver.StartWorker already declared at ./resolver.go:263:28
    ./resolver_feat008.go:107:28: method mutationResolver.StopWorker already declared at ./resolver.go:293:28
    ./resolver_feat008.go:115:28: method mutationResolver.PluginDispatch already declared at ./resolver.go:301:28
    ./resolver_feat008.go:147:28: method mutationResolver.ComparisonDispatch already declared at ./resolver.go:333:28
    ./resolver_feat008.go:192:28: method mutationResolver.PersonaBind already declared at ./resolver.go:378:28
    ./resolver_feat008.go:206:28: method mutationResolver.PersonaCreate already declared at ./resolver.go:392:28
    ./resolver_feat008.go:216:28: method mutationResolver.PersonaUpdate already declared at ./resolver.go:402:28
    ./resolver_feat008.go:226:28: method mutationResolver.PersonaDelete already declared at ./resolver.go:412:28
    ./resolver_feat008.go:226:28: too many errors
    exit status 1

ROOT CAUSE
----------
gqlgen v0.17.89 (the version pinned in cli/go.mod) is configured in
single-file resolver layout via cli/internal/server/graphql/gqlgen.yml:

    resolver:
      filename: resolver.go
      package: graphql
      type: Resolver

In this layout the resolvergen plugin
(/home/erik/go/pkg/mod/github.com/99designs/gqlgen@v0.17.89/plugin/resolvergen/resolver.go,
generateSingleFile) walks every required resolver field and calls
rewriter.GetMethodBody(structName, GoFieldName) to recover an existing
implementation. The rewriter (internal/rewrite/rewriter.go GetPrevDecl)
scans the entire package via `packages.Load(NeedSyntax|NeedTypes)`, so
it finds existing implementations regardless of which file they live in
— but the generator then writes the recovered body into a NEW method on
`*mutationResolver` in the configured resolver.go. The original
declaration in resolver_feat008.go (or any other sibling file) is left
in place. Both files now define the same method on the same receiver,
which is a hard Go compile error.

Concretely: ~9 mutation methods (BeadClose, WorkerDispatch, StartWorker,
StopWorker, PluginDispatch, ComparisonDispatch, PersonaBind,
PersonaCreate, PersonaUpdate, PersonaDelete, …) live in
resolver_feat008.go (1193 LOC, also containing helpers + types like
paletteResultCap and the pluginActionMu mutex that are not gqlgen
artefacts). Every one of them gets duplicated into resolver.go on each
gqlgen run. The first run from a clean checkout already breaks the
build; subsequent runs see the broken state and add the resolver_agent.go
query methods (Workers/AgentSessions/etc.) on top, compounding the
problem.

This bug predates Stage E2. It is the product of single-file gqlgen
layout coexisting with hand-split sibling resolver files, and it has
existed at least since resolver_feat008.go was introduced (FEAT-008
work). Earlier "regeneration" commits (e.g. f912445f
feat(providers): inline 24h sparkline) only touched generated.go +
models.go without rerunning gqlgen end-to-end against this resolver
package — those edits were applied by hand or by reverting resolver.go
post-regen.

NO IN-SCOPE WORKAROUND
----------------------
The bead's named scope (schema.graphql, resolver_documents.go,
resolver_agent.go, generated.go, models.go) cannot break this. The only
fixes that would make `gqlgen generate` exit 0 require structural
changes outside scope:

  - Consolidating resolver_feat008.go's methods into resolver.go
    (deleting the duplicate-in-resolver_feat008.go set after each
    regen). This is a ~1k-LOC restructure that crosses several
    unrelated FEAT-008 / persona / worker dispatch concerns.
  - Switching gqlgen to layout: follow-schema with a per-schema-file
    resolver dir, requiring a renamed resolver tree and fresh stubs.
  - Tagging resolver_feat008.go with a build constraint that hides it
    from `packages.Load` during generation, then reintroducing it for
    runtime — fragile and makes generation a non-default path
    (`GOFLAGS=-tags=…`), which would itself break the AC's literal
    "exits 0 from cd cli/internal/server/graphql && go run …" wording.

Either fix is a refactor of the resolver package's organisational
contract and is well outside the surface this bead is scoped to. The
bead's instruction "Do not modify files outside the scope the bead
description names" applies to resolver_feat008.go.

I considered a partial commit that:
  - Adds the schema and resolver edits this bead names.
  - Hand-edits generated.go and models.go to match what gqlgen would
    have produced.
  - Adds TestGraphQLBoundedEgress with the required subtests.
  - Leaves the gqlgen-exits-0 AC unmet.

That is functionally adequate but it would commit red against an
explicit AC checkbox ("regeneration succeeds cleanly") and would push
the "fix the gqlgen workflow" decision onto the reviewer with no
documented rationale. Per the bead instructions ("a well-justified
no_changes is better than a bad commit"), the right answer is to
surface the blocker now so the next attempt can either (i) explicitly
expand scope to consolidate resolvers, (ii) update the AC to drop the
"exit 0" requirement and accept hand-curated generated.go/models.go,
or (iii) split a precursor bead that fixes the gqlgen workflow first.

(c) WHAT A FOLLOW-UP ATTEMPT WOULD NEED
---------------------------------------

Pick one and write it into the bead before re-queuing:

  Option A — Precursor bead first.
    Create a new bead "graphql: consolidate resolvers so gqlgen generate
    is reproducible" that moves the method bodies of resolver_feat008.go
    (and any other sibling resolver_*.go files that hold gqlgen-managed
    mutation/query methods) into resolver.go and trims the siblings down
    to non-gqlgen helpers (constants, types, vars, plain functions).
    Verify gqlgen generate exits 0 from a clean checkout. Then re-run
    Stage E2 — schema + clamp + tests — on top.

  Option B — Relax the AC.
    Change the Stage E2 AC from "go run github.com/99designs/gqlgen
    generate exits 0" to "generated.go and models.go expose the new
    fields and the package builds cleanly". This is consistent with how
    f912445f and other prior schema-touching commits actually shipped.
    Then a single Stage E2 commit can carry the schema edit + hand-
    matched generated.go/models.go diffs + resolver edits + tests.

  Option C — Switch layout.
    Move resolver: { filename: resolver.go } to layout: follow-schema
    with a dedicated resolver dir. This is a larger configuration
    change that would reshape every resolver_*.go file in the package
    and is also out of E2's scope.

The schema diff, resolver clamp logic, and test fixtures I already

rationale: no_changes_rationale — bead ddx-999eee75 (Stage E2: GraphQL bounded text egress)
================================================================================

(a) WHAT IS DONE
----------------

- Read all in-scope files (schema.graphql, resolver_documents.go,
  resolver_agent.go) plus generated.go, models.go, gqlgen.yml.
- Read the FEAT-022 §10/§11 invariants and the existing E1 work in
  cli/internal/server/server.go and cli/internal/server/state_executions.go
  (uses evidence.ClampOutput + truncated/original_bytes pattern).
- Read the existing TestServerBoundedEgress in
  cli/internal/server/bounded_egress_test.go to understand the fixture
  pattern (lower serverInlineCapBytes → 256, body sized 2× cap, assert
  length cap+marker, truncated, originalBytes).
- Determined the schema-edit + resolver-edit footprint that would be
  required:
    1. schema.graphql: add `truncated: Boolean!` and `originalBytes: Int!`
       to type Document and type AgentSession; introduce a new type
       WorkerPrompt { text: String!, truncated: Boolean!,
       originalBytes: Int! } and change Query.workerPrompt return type
       from String! to WorkerPrompt!.
    2. resolver_documents.go DocumentByPath: route resolved file content
       through evidence.ClampOutput; populate Truncated + OriginalBytes
       on the returned *Document.
    3. resolver_agent.go WorkerPrompt and AgentSession: same treatment
       for prompt body and for agentSession Prompt+Response (matching
       the REST handler's "OriginalBytes is sum of prompt+response"
       pattern from server.go:2472–2480 + bounded_egress_test.go:196–199).
    4. New TestGraphQLBoundedEgress in this package with one subtest per
       in-scope resolver, using newGQLHandler + a custom testStateProvider
       that returns oversized fixtures.
    5. Run gqlgen to refresh generated.go and models.go.

(b) WHAT IS BLOCKING
--------------------

The acceptance contract requires:

    cd cli/internal/server/graphql && go run github.com/99designs/gqlgen generate

to exit 0. With the current package layout this is not achievable, and
it is **not caused by any edit this bead would introduce** — the failure
reproduces from the pristine base revision (e6cad0d6) before any
schema or resolver change:

    $ git checkout e6cad0d6 -- cli/internal/server/graphql/
    $ cd cli/internal/server/graphql && go run github.com/99designs/gqlgen generate
    ./resolver_feat008.go:36:28: method mutationResolver.BeadClose already declared at ./resolver.go:165:28
    ./resolver_feat008.go:58:28: method mutationResolver.WorkerDispatch already declared at ./resolver.go:244:28
    ./resolver_feat008.go:77:28: method mutationResolver.StartWorker already declared at ./resolver.go:263:28
    ./resolver_feat008.go:107:28: method mutationResolver.StopWorker already declared at ./resolver.go:293:28
    ./resolver_feat008.go:115:28: method mutationResolver.PluginDispatch already declared at ./resolver.go:301:28
    ./resolver_feat008.go:147:28: method mutationResolver.ComparisonDispatch already declared at ./resolver.go:333:28
    ./resolver_feat008.go:192:28: method mutationResolver.PersonaBind already declared at ./resolver.go:378:28
    ./resolver_feat008.go:206:28: method mutationResolver.PersonaCreate already declared at ./resolver.go:392:28
    ./resolver_feat008.go:216:28: method mutationResolver.PersonaUpdate already declared at ./resolver.go:402:28
    ./resolver_feat008.go:226:28: method mutationResolver.PersonaDelete already declared at ./resolver.go:412:28
    ./resolver_feat008.go:226:28: too many errors
    exit status 1

ROOT CAUSE
----------
gqlgen v0.17.89 (the version pinned in cli/go.mod) is configured in
single-file resolver layout via cli/internal/server/graphql/gqlgen.yml:

    resolver:
      filename: resolver.go
      package: graphql
      type: Resolver

In this layout the resolvergen plugin
(/home/erik/go/pkg/mod/github.com/99designs/gqlgen@v0.17.89/plugin/resolvergen/resolver.go,
generateSingleFile) walks every required resolver field and calls
rewriter.GetMethodBody(structName, GoFieldName) to recover an existing
implementation. The rewriter (internal/rewrite/rewriter.go GetPrevDecl)
scans the entire package via `packages.Load(NeedSyntax|NeedTypes)`, so
it finds existing implementations regardless of which file they live in
— but the generator then writes the recovered body into a NEW method on
`*mutationResolver` in the configured resolver.go. The original
declaration in resolver_feat008.go (or any other sibling file) is left
in place. Both files now define the same method on the same receiver,
which is a hard Go compile error.

Concretely: ~9 mutation methods (BeadClose, WorkerDispatch, StartWorker,
StopWorker, PluginDispatch, ComparisonDispatch, PersonaBind,
PersonaCreate, PersonaUpdate, PersonaDelete, …) live in
resolver_feat008.go (1193 LOC, also containing helpers + types like
paletteResultCap and the pluginActionMu mutex that are not gqlgen
artefacts). Every one of them gets duplicated into resolver.go on each
gqlgen run. The first run from a clean checkout already breaks the
build; subsequent runs see the broken state and add the resolver_agent.go
query methods (Workers/AgentSessions/etc.) on top, compounding the
problem.

This bug predates Stage E2. It is the product of single-file gqlgen
layout coexisting with hand-split sibling resolver files, and it has
existed at least since resolver_feat008.go was introduced (FEAT-008
work). Earlier "regeneration" commits (e.g. f912445f
feat(providers): inline 24h sparkline) only touched generated.go +
models.go without rerunning gqlgen end-to-end against this resolver
package — those edits were applied by hand or by reverting resolver.go
post-regen.

NO IN-SCOPE WORKAROUND
----------------------
The bead's named scope (schema.graphql, resolver_documents.go,
resolver_agent.go, generated.go, models.go) cannot break this. The only
fixes that would make `gqlgen generate` exit 0 require structural
changes outside scope:

  - Consolidating resolver_feat008.go's methods into resolver.go
    (deleting the duplicate-in-resolver_feat008.go set after each
    regen). This is a ~1k-LOC restructure that crosses several
    unrelated FEAT-008 / persona / worker dispatch concerns.
  - Switching gqlgen to layout: follow-schema with a per-schema-file
    resolver dir, requiring a renamed resolver tree and fresh stubs.
  - Tagging resolver_feat008.go with a build constraint that hides it
    from `packages.Load` during generation, then reintroducing it for
    runtime — fragile and makes generation a non-default path
    (`GOFLAGS=-tags=…`), which would itself break the AC's literal
    "exits 0 from cd cli/internal/server/graphql && go run …" wording.

Either fix is a refactor of the resolver package's organisational
contract and is well outside the surface this bead is scoped to. The
bead's instruction "Do not modify files outside the scope the bead
description names" applies to resolver_feat008.go.

I considered a partial commit that:
  - Adds the schema and resolver edits this bead names.
  - Hand-edits generated.go and models.go to match what gqlgen would
    have produced.
  - Adds TestGraphQLBoundedEgress with the required subtests.
  - Leaves the gqlgen-exits-0 AC unmet.

That is functionally adequate but it would commit red against an
explicit AC checkbox ("regeneration succeeds cleanly") and would push
the "fix the gqlgen workflow" decision onto the reviewer with no
documented rationale. Per the bead instructions ("a well-justified
no_changes is better than a bad commit"), the right answer is to
surface the blocker now so the next attempt can either (i) explicitly
expand scope to consolidate resolvers, (ii) update the AC to drop the
"exit 0" requirement and accept hand-curated generated.go/models.go,
or (iii) split a precursor bead that fixes the gqlgen workflow first.

(c) WHAT A FOLLOW-UP ATTEMPT WOULD NEED
---------------------------------------

Pick one and write it into the bead before re-queuing:

  Option A — Precursor bead first.
    Create a new bead "graphql: consolidate resolvers so gqlgen generate
    is reproducible" that moves the method bodies of resolver_feat008.go
    (and any other sibling resolver_*.go files that hold gqlgen-managed
    mutation/query methods) into resolver.go and trims the siblings down
    to non-gqlgen helpers (constants, types, vars, plain functions).
    Verify gqlgen generate exits 0 from a clean checkout. Then re-run
    Stage E2 — schema + clamp + tests — on top.

  Option B — Relax the AC.
    Change the Stage E2 AC from "go run github.com/99designs/gqlgen
    generate exits 0" to "generated.go and models.go expose the new
    fields and the package builds cleanly". This is consistent with how
    f912445f and other prior schema-touching commits actually shipped.
    Then a single Stage E2 commit can carry the schema edit + hand-
    matched generated.go/models.go diffs + resolver edits + tests.

  Option C — Switch layout.
    Move resolver: { filename: resolver.go } to layout: follow-schema
    with a dedicated resolver dir. This is a larger configuration
    change that would reshape every resolver_*.go file in the package
    and is also out of E2's scope.

The schema diff, resolver clamp logic, and test fixtures I already
designed in section (a) are ready to apply on top of any of those —
they are the easy part. The hard part is the gqlgen workflow contract,
which Stage E2 does not own.

VERIFICATION ARTEFACTS
----------------------
- Reproduction of the gqlgen failure from base rev e6cad0d6 (commands
  + raw output) is captured above.
- Inspection of resolvergen single-file behaviour:
  /home/erik/go/pkg/mod/github.com/99designs/gqlgen@v0.17.89/plugin/resolvergen/resolver.go
  generateSingleFile, lines 54–120. Combined with
  /home/erik/go/pkg/mod/github.com/99designs/gqlgen@v0.17.89/internal/rewrite/rewriter.go
  GetPrevDecl + RemainingSource (lines 71–134, 196+), shows the
  duplicate-write-back behaviour for cross-file method definitions.
- The pre-existing E1 implementation
  (cli/internal/server/server.go:54–65 mcpText helper,
  :988, :2169, :2472–2480; cli/internal/server/state_executions.go:213–221)
  documents the truncated/original_bytes contract Stage E2 must mirror;
  this is the pattern I would follow once the gqlgen workflow blocker
  is resolved.

rationale: Bead ddx-263ad347 — operatorPromptApprove/Cancel + per-project allowlist + auto-approve flag

All four acceptance criteria are already satisfied at the base revision (bb3b5883). No source change is required.

AC #1 — Approve/cancel mutations exist:
  - cli/internal/server/graphql/schema.graphql declares operatorPromptApprove and operatorPromptCancel.
  - Resolvers: cli/internal/server/graphql/resolver_operator_prompt.go
      OperatorPromptApprove (line 399)
      OperatorPromptCancel  (line 461)

AC #2 — Per-project allowlist enforced:
  - Resolver.OperatorPromptAutoApproveAllowlist is the per-project allowlist.
  - canSubmit (line 528) gates ts-net submitters; canAutoApprove (line 548) gates approve.
  - Empty allowlist denies approval and ts-net submission (errSubmitDenied / errAutoApproveDenied).
  - Tests:
      TestOperatorPromptApprove_DeniedEmptyAllowlist
      TestOperatorPromptSubmit_TsnetNotInAllowlistRejected
      TestOperatorPromptSubmit_TsnetOnAllowlistAccepted

AC #3 — Auto-approve restricted to configured-localhost:
  - canAutoApprove returns false for any non-localhost identity (line 549), regardless of allowlist contents.
  - OperatorPromptAllowlistLocalhostSentinel ("localhost") is the opt-in sentinel.
  - Tests:
      TestOperatorPromptSubmit_AutoApproveAllowedForLocalhost  (localhost+sentinel → autoApproved=true, status=open)
      TestOperatorPromptSubmit_AutoApproveDeniedForTsnet       (ts-net never auto-approves even when on allowlist)

AC #4 — Tests cover happy + denied paths:
  - Happy: TestOperatorPromptApprove_Happy, TestOperatorPromptCancel_Happy, TestOperatorPromptSubmit_AutoApproveAllowedForLocalhost, TestOperatorPromptSubmit_TsnetOnAllowlistAccepted
  - Denied: TestOperatorPromptApprove_DeniedEmptyAllowlist, TestOperatorPromptSubmit_AutoApproveDeniedForTsnet, TestOperatorPromptSubmit_TsnetNotInAllowlistRejected, TestOperatorPromptApprove_CSRFRequired, TestOperatorPromptCancel_CSRFRequired, TestOperatorPromptApprove_RejectsNonProposed, TestOperatorPromptCancel_RejectsNonProposed

Manual approve = proposed → ready: OperatorPromptApprove sets b.Status = bead.StatusOpen (the "ready" state for work pickup; the codebase uses StatusOpen as the ready bucket — see execute_bead_loop.go and the wake tests).

Prior REVIEW:BLOCK notes on this bead complained that the diff contained only execution metadata. That observation is correct — but the source change those reviewers were looking for had already landed before bb3b5883. The bead's contract is satisfied by the existing code; another implementation pass would be duplicate or churn.

This bead is a roll-up epic. Each acceptance criterion was delivered by an earlier
child bead; the current worktree (base aff3240b) already satisfies every AC. Prior
execute-bead attempts produced empty diffs because there is no per-attempt code
to write — the children shipped the work. Reviewer kept blocking because the
diff-only inspection does not see across the rollup. Concrete evidence below
maps each AC to its in-tree artifact and (where known) the commit that landed it.

AC 1 — All 7 homepage sections live
  Artifact: website/layouts/index.html
  Sections present (grep '<!-- Section':
    1. .ddx-home-hero        (Hero)
    2. .ddx-home-problem     (Problem)
    3. .ddx-home-how         (How it works — 3 steps)
    4. .ddx-home-features    (Features preview, 4-up grid → /features/)
    5. .ddx-home-principles  (6-card principles grid)
    6. .ddx-home-demo        (Asciinema player)
    7. .ddx-home-cta         (Get started CTA)
  Cross-check: website/DESIGN.md "Homepage Section Map" table lists the same 7
  selectors. Playwright test 'homepage has all 7 sections' asserts each.

AC 2 — /features/ section with maturity badges
  Artifact: website/content/features/_index.md
  Seven feature sections, each ending in a {{< maturity ... >}} shortcode call:
    Artifact Graph (beta), Beads & DAG (stable), Execute-Loop (stable),
    Evidence Capture (stable), Multi-Model Review (beta), Skills (beta),
    Agent-Agnostic Dispatch (stable).
  Shortcode: website/layouts/shortcodes/maturity.html
  Playwright test 'features page loads with maturity badges' asserts visibility
  of .ddx-maturity--stable, .ddx-maturity--beta, .ddx-maturity--planned.

AC 3 — DESIGN.md tokens wired to Hugo
  Artifact: website/DESIGN.md
  Sections: Palette (lever/load/fulcrum/brass), Surface & Foreground,
  Typography (Inter/Space Grotesk/Newsreader), Homepage Section Map,
  Maturity Badges, Responsive Breakpoints.
  Wiring: layouts/index.html:1 imports assets/css/custom.css through
    {{- $css := resources.Get "css/custom.css" | minify | fingerprint -}}
  Tokens (--ddx-accent-lever, --ddx-bg-canvas, etc.) are defined in
  assets/css/custom.css and consumed by .ddx-home-* selectors in layouts/.

AC 4 — Playwright e2e gaps closed
  Artifact: website/e2e/microsite.spec.ts (134 lines, 12 tests).
  Coverage:
    - homepage loads with hero and features
    - homepage has all 7 sections (per-section locator assertions)
    - homepage screenshot (full-page, fonts/CDN blocked for stability)
    - homepage fits mobile viewport (390x844, no horizontal scroll)
    - features nav link in homepage
    - features page loads with maturity badges (stable/beta/planned)
    - features page screenshot
    - getting started page
    - CLI reference page
    - skills page
    - plugins page
    - ecosystem page
    - nav links work (Docs / Concepts / Features routing)
  Snapshots committed: e2e/microsite.spec.ts-snapshots/{features,getting-started,homepage}-linux.png

AC 5 — UI screenshots committed to website/static/ui/
  Artifacts:
    website/static/ui/homepage.png
    website/static/ui/features.png
  These are the canonical promotional screenshots referenced from marketing
  copy. Playwright snapshot baselines (e2e/microsite.spec.ts-snapshots/) are
  the test-time visual regression baselines.

  Residual gap (not blocking AC, noted for follow-up): content/features/_index.md
  references seven additional feature-specific placeholder images
  (feature-artifact-graph.png, feature-beads-dag.png, feature-work.png,
  feature-evidence-capture.png, feature-multi-model-review.png,
  feature-skills.png, feature-agent-dispatch.png) that do not yet exist in
  static/ui/. The alt text on each marks them "placeholder". Recommend a
  follow-up bead to either (a) generate per-feature screenshots, or (b) drop
  the placeholder image lines until assets are produced.

What is done
  All five acceptance criteria are evidenced by files currently in the
  worktree as listed above. Repository state at base aff3240b is sufficient
  to close this epic.

What is blocking a per-attempt commit
  Nothing in the AC requires new code in this attempt. Producing a fabricated
  diff to "show progress" would be lower quality than a clean no_changes
  exit with this rationale.

What a follow-up attempt would need
  Either (a) close this epic on the strength of in-tree evidence (recommended),
  or (b) split the residual feature-image gap into a fresh bead targeting only
  static/ui/feature-*.png generation and drop this rollup's hold on the queue.

  1. AC1 — Runs page imports the shared chip primitive:
     `import FilterChip from '$lib/components/FilterChip.svelte'`

     The same FilterChip is also used by the Artifacts page

  2. AC2 — Virtualization kicks in past threshold:
     `const VIRTUALIZE_THRESHOLD = 1000` and
     `let virtualized = $derived(edges.length >= VIRTUALIZE_THRESHOLD)` at
     runs/+page.svelte:17 / :155, applied per-row via `style={rowStyle}` at
     runs/+page.svelte:373 (content-visibility: auto + contain-intrinsic-size).
     The table exposes `data-virtualized="true|false"` for tests.

  3. AC3 — Live worker phase updates still work through the virtualized list:
     Virtualization uses CSS content-visibility (browser-native row windowing),
     not row unmount. Expanded rows render <RunRowDetail …/> at line 454; that
     component owns the worker subscription/polling and remains mounted in the
     DOM regardless of virtualization. `content-visibility: auto` only skips
     paint/layout for off-screen rows, so reactive updates to row state and to
     RunRowDetail subscriptions continue to flow.

✓ TestBuildPreClaimIntakePrompt_UsesDocumentedReadinessSchema: PASS

AC5: Pre-commit ready
✓ No code changes needed; schema gate is fully functional and integrated

Schema drift prevention mechanism:
The schema gate prevents reintroduction of the original wedge (producer emitting bool verdict while consumer parses string) by:
1. Schema lock: readiness-checks.schema.json is authoritative source of truth
2. Producer binding: buildPreClaimIntakePrompt cites schema (line 639)
3. Consumer alignment: readinessVerdict.UnmarshalJSON implements all schema forms (lines 97-125)
4. Test lock: TestReadinessChecksSchema validates schema/decoder alignment in all cases
5. Prompt lock: TestBuildPreClaimIntakePrompt_UsesDocumentedReadinessSchema catches prompt drift
6. CI enforcement: make readiness-schema runs before merge

Any change to schema, prompt, or decoder that breaks alignment fails the CI gate.

The worker still treats `mixed_commit_and_no_changes_rationale` as a recoverable no-progress outcome even when the just-created children still point back to the attempted bead. That leaves the same bead getting reclaimed forever while the children are structurally blocked.

ROOT CAUSE
`cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go` already has no-progress and candidate-skip machinery, but it does not check for the specific back-edge case where a fresh child carries the attempted bead in its deps. Without that guard, the worker continues to re-claim the parent instead of escalating.

PROPOSED FIX
When a run ends with `mixed_commit_and_no_changes_rationale` and any freshly-created child still depends on the attempted bead, emit a durable `operator_attention` event with reason `parent_back_edge`, park the bead, and stop re-claiming it. Add a regression named `TestWorkHaltsOnParentBackEdge` that proves the worker exits the loop rather than retrying the parent.

NON-SCOPE

- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop_test.go
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_intake_decompose_test.go (if the test fixture needs child deps)

Out-of-scope:
- cli/internal/bead/store.go
- cli/internal/bead/doctor.go
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go
- cli/internal/agent/lifecycle_dispatch.go","events_attachment":"ddx-0836dde5/events.jsonl","id":"ddx-0836dde5","issue_type":"task","labels":["ac-quality:needs-refinement","area:decomposition","area:lifecycle","area:tracker","kind:correctness","kind:safety"],"parent":"ddx-e19185aa","priority":0,"queue-rank":1770,"schema_version":1,"session_id":"eb-1089ef73","status":"closed","title":"Halt work on parent_back_edge no-progress loops","updated_at":"2026-06-13T13:58:34.771417389Z"}

Parent bead ddx-e19185aa is too broad for one execution pass. This child owns part 3 of 3 of that parent scope.

ROOT CAUSE
The decomposition action (currently invoked when a bead is too large for one execution and the agent splits it into child beads) is writing the parent's ID as a dep edge on every child. This produces an unbreakable deadlock:

  e29892a4 (parent, open) -- parent --> child_A
  e29892a4 -- parent --> child_B
  child_A.deps = [e29892a4]           <- BAD: parent is also a dep
  child_B.deps = [child_A, e29892a4]  <- BAD

The parent stays open until its children close. Every child is blocked on the parent. Nothing can be claimed. ddx work loops on the parent, the orchestrator emits 'mixed_commit_and_no_changes_rationale', the worker retries, and burns claim budget without progress.

Observed scenario (niflheim, 2026-06-04 04:30-05:13 UTC):
- Parent: niflheim-e29892a4 ('feat(delta): async write-behind ...')
- 10 children created during planning loops (23223661, a44a8ceb, 054c89b7, 0470ba0d, f10e9e35, f076b08c, 6c041162, 94e1df73, 9da62c2d, 5e4f9fb2)
- Every child carried deps=[..siblings.., niflheim-e29892a4]
- ~50 minutes of agent time + 4 strong-policy claude/opus attempts with zero feature commits; only tracker chores
- Unwedged manually by closing the parent via 'ddx bead close', after which siblings drained correctly in foundational order

Root cause is structural, not model-capability — auto-upgrading to a stronger model does NOT fix this. The orchestrator already detects the no-progress state but its only remediation is 'retry parent', which re-enters the same loop.

There is also a 'project root mutation rejected (continuing)' warning emitted by the readiness check during this state; that's a downstream symptom of the same problem, not the cause.

PROPOSED FIX
Implement only this child acceptance slice:
- The orchestrator's 'project root mutation rejected (continuing)' warning, when emitted alongside the parent-back-edge state, is upgraded from WARN to ERROR with the offending bead ID logged. Test: dispatch_logs_error_on_root_mutation_with_back_edge.
- Lifecycle schema documents that 'parent' and 'deps' MUST be disjoint and that 'deps' MUST be acyclic with the parent-tree. docs/ddx/lifecycle-rules.md (or equivalent) updated; ADR file if no governing artifact already exists.
- Existing valid graphs continue to pass 'ddx bead doctor' without --fix and without regression — regression test against a pre-existing tracker snapshot.

NON-SCOPE
Do not implement parent acceptance criteria assigned to sibling child beads. Preserve every parent non-scope constraint unless this child explicitly narrows it.","events_attachment":"ddx-0c96c8b1/events.jsonl","id":"ddx-0c96c8b1","issue_type":"task","labels":["ac-quality:needs-refinement","area:decomposition","area:lifecycle","area:tracker","decomposed","kind:correctness","kind:safety"],"parent":"ddx-e19185aa","priority":0,"queue-rank":1810,"schema_version":1,"session_id":"eb-80d6ce75","status":"closed","title":"Reject bead dep edges that re-introduce the parent or any ancestor: part 3","updated_at":"2026-06-13T13:58:34.923249996Z","work-no-changes-count":2}

2. `TestExecuteBeadMixedCommitAndBlockedNoChangesRationalePreservesEvidence` or equivalent proves a mixed result containing `status: blocked` preserves the rationale text in result/evidence and exposes a clear `mixed_commit_and_no_changes_rationale`-style detail.
3. Existing legitimate zero-diff no_changes coverage still passes, including `TestNoChangesRationaleWithVerificationCommandClosesAlreadySatisfied` or the local equivalent for verification_command behavior.
4. Existing normal commit success coverage still passes when no `no_changes_rationale.txt` is present.
5. `cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... -run "TestExecuteBead.*NoChanges|TestNoChanges.*|Test.*Mixed.*NoChanges" -count=1` passes.
6. `cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... ./internal/bead/...` passes or any pre-existing unrelated failure is recorded in the bead evidence without being converted to success.
7. `lefthook run pre-commit` passes.","archived_at":"2026-05-15T00:08:56Z","closing_commit_sha":"694426cc7e302ed91531fbf6a082c6b7c5a0d21c","created_at":"2026-05-12T05:23:44.206444045Z","description":"PROBLEM
A DDx execute-bead attempt can produce both a substantive implementation commit and `.ddx/executions/<attempt>/no_changes_rationale.txt`, and DDx still records the attempt as `success`/`merged`. Live evidence: attempt `20260512T050728-bb18a61f` for `ddx-ef9e8aec` landed commit `15772fc85`, merged evidence commit `9e1ab8506`, and closed the bead, while `.ddx/executions/20260512T050728-bb18a61f/no_changes_rationale.txt` says `status: blocked` because the required `cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... ./internal/bead/...` gate timed out/failed. That turns a self-reported blocked verification into a successful close.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go:1413-1428` classifies `task_succeeded` solely from `resultRev != baseRev`; it only reads `no_changes_rationale.txt` when `resultRev == baseRev`.
- `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go:615-616` deliberately excludes `.ddx/executions/*/no_changes_rationale.txt` from synthesized code commits, so the file can coexist with a synthesized implementation commit without affecting `resultRev`.
- `cli/internal/agent/try/no_changes.go:56-90` already parses structured `status: blocked|open|proposed` no_changes rationales, but that parser is bypassed when any implementation commit exists.
- `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:1360-1407` treats the returned report as authoritative; by the time the loop sees the result, the contradictory no_changes signal has been lost behind a success/merged outcome.

PROPOSED FIX
- Make execute-bead treat an implementation diff plus `no_changes_rationale.txt` as a non-successful mixed-signal result before close/merge success is reported. A worker must choose one lane: commit green implementation, or no_changes/status rationale.
- Preserve the rationale text in evidence and surface a clear failure/detail such as `mixed_commit_and_no_changes_rationale` so the worker can retry or a follow-up bead can handle the stated blocker.
- Ensure `status: blocked` in the rationale cannot close a bead as success when a commit also exists.
- Keep legitimate `no_changes` behavior unchanged for zero-diff attempts and keep ordinary green implementation commits unchanged when no rationale file exists.

IN-SCOPE FILES
- `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go`
- `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_*test.go` or `cli/internal/agent/try/*test.go` as needed for focused coverage

NON-SCOPE
- Do not rewrite the already-landed `ddx-ef9e8aec` history.
- Do not change Fizeau routing/model/provider selection.
- Do not weaken legitimate already-satisfied no_changes verification.

PARENT/DEPS
Parent: ddx-cc0641a7. .ddx/beads-archive.jsonl:360:{"archived_at":"2026-07-02T22:52:12Z","closing_commit_sha":"277f6be2aa285202336cd399c669bd1f8f03cf81","created_at":"2026-06-28T04:37:40.306667675Z","description":"PROBLEM
The test TestPreDispatchDirtyPreserveRequiresStableImplementationDirt at cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop_stay_alive_test.go:836 fails on a normally-loaded developer machine because it uses a fixed 150ms context.WithTimeout for an in-process worker.Run() call that has to claim + execute + land + close one bead and continue polling. The 150ms budget is too tight; observed outcome on base revision edbcd9aa5: err is nil (work completes before deadline OR worker exits cleanly via another path), so require.ErrorIs(t, err, context.DeadlineExceeded) at line 882 fails.

This is a release-blocker: lefthook run pre-commit fails, blocking every subsequent bead from landing. Diagnosed in ddx-47d9100a's no_changes_rationale (the implementer correctly identified it as out-of-scope pre-existing breakage from commit 4555a083f, introduced by ddx-df319cf9).

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop_stay_alive_test.go:871 — context.WithTimeout 150*time.Millisecond
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop_stay_alive_test.go:882 — require.ErrorIs(t, err, context.DeadlineExceeded)
- Adjacent test ~line 726 (TestPreDispatchDirtyPreservePersistsStableImplementationDirt) uses the correct pattern: 30s outer timeout, worker.Run in a goroutine, require.Eventually polling for bead closure, then cancel(). See the comment at lines 772-774 explaining why 750ms was too tight.

PROPOSED FIX
Rewrite TestPreDispatchDirtyPreserveRequiresStableImplementationDirt to match the surrounding require.Eventually pattern:
1. Outer ctx timeout: 30*time.Second.
2. Run worker.Run in a goroutine, push result+err to a runCh.
3. require.Eventually (25s budget, 50ms tick) polls store.Get("ddx-int-0001") until Status == StatusClosed.
4. cancel() the outer ctx; <-runCh to collect outcome.

","archived_at":"2026-07-02T22:52:12Z","closing_commit_sha":"8cde0b39a03e58d509551e8de4a4c12fabb5cc72","created_at":"2026-06-13T23:42:40.773782316Z","description":"PROBLEM

During dogfood on 2026-06-13, `ddx work --once` for ddx-c02ce93c ran a provider attempt that edited implementation files and exited 0, but produced no commit and no `no_changes_rationale.txt`. DDx reported `no_evidence_produced` with dirty paths, then removed the isolated attempt worktree. The operator could not rescue the provider patch from the worktree and had to manually reimplement from partial observed output. A worker surface that destroys dirty provider output after a successful process exit is not acceptable: DDx must preserve or export recoverable evidence for underspecified provider/commit failures.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE

- `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go:1416-1427` classifies clean-exit/no-commit/no-rationale as `task_no_evidence` and records dirty paths, but it does not convert dirty no-evidence into a preserved recoverable attempt.
- `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go:943-954` defers attempt cleanup for all non-preserved worktree attempts; because `task_no_evidence` is not preserved, cleanup proceeds.
- `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go:1577-1588` removes every non-success attempt worktree unless a preserve flag is set, including dirty `task_no_evidence` attempts.
- `cli/internal/agent/gitops_real.go:35-37` ignores `git worktree remove --force` errors, so cleanup failures would also be underreported.

PROPOSED FIX

Treat dirty `task_no_evidence` as recoverable provider output: before cleanup, preserve the worktree with a `refs/ddx/iterations/<bead>/<attempt>` ref or export a patch/artifact that contains every dirty path and enough metadata to reconstruct the attempt. Worker output and bead events must name the preserve ref or patch path. Only clean no-evidence attempts may be removed normally.

IN-SCOPE FILES

- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go
- cli/internal/agent/gitops_real.go
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_integration_test.go
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_e2e_test.go

NON-SCOPE

Do not change legitimate `no_changes_rationale.txt` handling. Do not preserve clean no-evidence attempts without dirty paths. Do not kill or change provider subprocess supervision; ddx-c02ce93c handled that separately. Do not hand-edit `.ddx/beads.jsonl`.

DOGFOOD EVIDENCE

Attempt `20260613T225213-a01c938c` for ddx-c02ce93c exited 0 after editing `cli/cmd/work_status_test.go`, `cli/internal/agent/agent_runner_service.go`, `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go`, `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go`, provider child files, liveness/status files, and then DDx deleted `/Users/erik/Projects/.ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-ddx-c02ce93c-20260613T225213-a01c938c`.

A live Niflheim worker audit on 2026-06-06 showed that `ddx work` is not yet a stable release-grade autonomous worker. Strong-model escalation exists, but it is not reliably tied to the class of failure. The worker spent repeated attempts on structural or system failures that stronger models cannot fix, while automatic review evidence was empty on attempts that were recorded as successful. This violates the DDx engineering principle "Cheapest Viable, Escalate on Evidence" (docs/dev/engineering-principles.md:39-45): review, runtime, or retry evidence should determine escalation, and only lower-power inability to meet acceptance criteria should push to stronger routes. It also threatens FEAT-010 task-execution reliability because beads can be retried, escalated, or closed without a typed, auditable reason.

This bead is a Niflheim incident/regression closure bead. It is not a duplicate of the narrower queue items listed under INTERSECTIONS; instead it captures the external evidence DDx workers will not otherwise have, and requires a DDx-side regression suite proving those observed scenarios classify correctly before rerun, escalation, operator attention, or close.

BACKGROUND AND OBSERVED NIFLHEIM EVIDENCE
The evidence was gathered from Niflheim worker logs and result bundles, but those files are not load-bearing for this bead. The relevant facts are copied here because an isolated DDx worker cannot access the Niflheim repository or `/tmp` logs.

1. Decomposed parent kept consuming attempts and escalated to a stronger model anyway.
- Niflheim bead: `niflheim-64217cc5`, title "Instrument cold replicator with structured per-operation S3 tracing".
- Observed result bundle: `.ddx/executions/20260606T152006-a4ca7254/result.json` plus worker logs from 2026-06-05/2026-06-06.
- Routing showed escalation exists: repeated routes started at `gpt-5.4-mini` with requested min powers 5, 7, 8, and 9, then reached `gpt-5.5` at requested min power 10.
- Metrics on the bead showed `attempt_count=24`, `total_tokens=19371353`, and average duration about `244112ms`.
- Despite triage recommending `close_decomposed_or_mark_execution_ineligible`, retries continued until the latest event recorded:
  `mixed_commit_and_no_changes_rationale`
  `powerClass=standard`
  `rationale: status: open`
  `orchestrator_action: decompose`
  `reason: parent bead exceeds the Step 0 size threshold and is already decomposed into focused child beads for tracing, counters, tests, and runbook work.`
  `child_beads: niflheim-f03c61a2, niflheim-df407aa8, niflheim-7a421ede, niflheim-0bbed6c9`
  `outcome_reason=decomposed`
- Expected DDx behavior: once the worker has reliable evidence that the bead is a decomposed parent or execution-ineligible parent, no further implementation attempt or model escalation should occur. It should mark execution-ineligible/proposed/operator-attention according to policy and move on.

2. Land/evidence coordination failures were treated like implementation failure and retried expensively.
- Niflheim bead: `niflheim-c75aba30`, title "Materializer per-merge memory footprint OOMs even at concurrent_limit=8".
- Two expensive attempts both used `gpt-5.4-mini`; no useful stronger-model escalation was observed.
- Metrics showed `attempt_count=2`, `total_tokens=65513510`, and average duration about `3306461.5ms`.
- First attempt result: `.ddx/executions/20260606T113550-7b29ee42/result.json`.
  - `status=execution_failed`, `outcome=error`.
  - Implementation result commit was `7aaeb0712...`; a merge commit `2c6f4d6e6` was later observed as an ancestor of HEAD, but the result still recorded a land coordination failure.
  - Error detail:
    `land coordination failed: land advancer: restoring refs/heads/main to ba2d3059f14e834dcb147f2a6db3b294d83b0fac after evidence commit failed: git update-ref refs/heads/main: fatal: update_ref failed for ref 'refs/heads/main': cannot lock ref 'refs/heads/main': is at 5a176b332b182f2a959614f63769d835c47ffd97 but expected 2c6f4d6e6961b8a93742cde66bceec056934655a`
  - `cycle_trace[0].final_decision="success"` and `cycle_trace[0].review_result={}`.
- Second attempt result: `.ddx/executions/20260606T142223-94f8f0f8/result.json`.
  - `status=execution_failed`, `outcome=error`.
  - Error detail:
    `land coordination failed: land advancer: landing worktree has staged changes after waiting 2s: A .ddx/executions/20260606T150832-af40450b/result.json`
  - `cycle_trace[0].final_decision="success"` and `cycle_trace[0].review_result={}`.
- At observation time the worker had claimed it again (`20260606T152024-a21d8c54`) and edited six files in a fresh isolated worktree:
  `crates/niflheim-core/src/config/mod.rs`, `crates/niflheim-core/src/config/tests.rs`, `crates/niflheim-core/src/config/types.rs`, `crates/niflheim-delta-writer/src/materializer/config.rs`, `crates/niflheim-delta-writer/src/materializer/executor.rs`, and `crates/niflheim-delta-writer/src/materializer/tests.rs`.
- Expected DDx behavior: land/ref/index/evidence-staging failures are system or coordination failures, not model-quality failures. The retry policy should reconcile a successfully landed candidate when possible, retry only the land/evidence step when safe, or park/operator-attention the bead when the landing worktree is dirty. It should not blindly rerun implementation at the same or stronger route.

3. Pre-claim intake/system dirt caused repeated warning churn instead of a clear operator lane.
- Niflheim bead: `niflheim-a62d31ce`, title "Publish competitive benchmark artifacts and provenance metadata".
- A live worker repeatedly ran readiness/intake. The log showed readiness timeout after five minutes, then:
  `preserved pre-dispatch implementation changes in /home/erik/Projects/niflheim under refs/ddx/pre-dispatch/20260606T153517-f7a32618; released niflheim-a62d31ce and continuing watch. dirty paths: .ddx/harness-sessions/-2929681.json. recover with git stash apply refs/ddx/pre-dispatch/20260606T153517-f7a32618`
- Bead events included `pre_claim_intake.warn` with timeout/system-unready reason and generic suggested action.
- Expected DDx behavior: repeated same/systemic pre-claim warnings and dirty generated DDx artifacts should become an operator-visible/system-unready state after the configured threshold, not an indefinite poll/release loop.

4. Automatic reviews were not forcing meaningful quality improvement.
- Recent Niflheim "success" attempts had empty review results:
  - `niflheim-9dea95da`, result `.ddx/executions/20260606T043655-845c77ee/result.json`: closed success, `cycle_trace.review_result={}`, `final_decision="success"`, model `gpt-5.4-mini`, result commit `78e51a168...`.
  - `niflheim-da4e1311`, result `.ddx/executions/20260606T085358-d80ada3e/result.json`: closed success, `cycle_trace.review_result={}`, `final_decision="success"`, model `gpt-5.4-mini`, result commit `d43be3a3...`.
  - `niflheim-c75aba30` attempts listed above: `cycle_trace.final_decision="success"` with `review_result={}` despite overall land failures.
- Manual Claude review earlier improved bead wording and completeness, but the automatic worker path did not show enforceable per-AC review evidence.
- Expected DDx behavior: unless review is explicitly skipped with a durable reason (`runtime.NoReview` or a valid `review:skip` + reason policy), an empty review result must be a review error or operator-attention signal. It must not be treated as approval, success, or evidence of quality improvement.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- `cli/internal/agent/failclass/failclass.go:103-142` contains the normative routing table: only `OutcomeGenuineFailure` can escalate; dirty worktree, transport, quota, auth, setup, timeout, and merge-conflict do not escalate. The live Niflheim behavior shows execute-bead/work outcomes are not consistently reduced to this typed table before retry/escalation decisions.
- `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_orchestrator.go:305-309` wraps `LandingAdvancer` errors as generic `land advancer` errors. The caller can record an overall `execution_failed` without distinguishing landed-candidate reconciliation, ref/index contention, staged evidence dirt, or dirty landing worktree from implementation failure.
- `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:1967-2072` emits pre-claim timeout/system-unready outcomes as warn-only by default and continues implementation when strict intake blocking is off. This permits repeated same-fingerprint/system warnings to look like a live worker instead of a system-unready/operator lane.
- `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:2175-2315` handles `PreClaimIntakeTooLargeDecomposed`, but missing/empty decomposition paths can warn and continue. `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:3826-3831` can recognize execution-ineligible/decomposed state only after the bead has the right metadata. The Niflheim parent evidence shows structural/decomposed-parent outcomes can still reach repeated implementation attempts and power escalation.
- `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:3042-3138` treats success disposition/status as closeable once candidate-cycle review/landing has allowed success, and the old post-merge review is optional. `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_review_test.go:799-823` explicitly proves a worker with `Reviewer: nil` can skip review and close the bead. `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_review.go:168-193` defines `ReviewResult` with all fields optional except the zero-value enum field; no invariant rejects `{}` as a success-path review artifact.

PROPOSED FIX
- Add compact Niflheim-style regression fixtures under `cli/internal/agent/testdata/niflheim_worker_evidence/` or equivalent. The fixtures should encode the four scenario families above: empty review success, land coordination/ref-lock/staged-evidence failure, decomposed-parent no-changes rationale with child beads, and repeated pre-claim system-unready warning. Do not depend on Niflheim checkout paths.
- Introduce or tighten a single execute-bead retry classification boundary that maps attempt result, land result, review result, no-changes rationale, and pre-claim intake outcome into exactly one action: close, retry same tier, escalate min power/profile, reconcile land/evidence, execution-ineligible/proposed, or operator-attention. Wire this boundary into `ddx work`/`ddx try` so `failclass.Classify` is not merely a standalone helper.
- Enforce review evidence on success. For non-skipped success paths, `cycle_trace.review_result` or the equivalent result field must contain a non-empty verdict, per-AC evidence or documented reason, reviewer routing/session metadata when available, and a classification. `{}` is invalid and must prevent close or produce a review-error/operator-attention event. Explicit review skips remain allowed only when they are durable, reasoned, and visible in result JSON.
- Classify land/ref/index/evidence-staging failures separately from implementation/model failures. If the candidate commit or merge is already landed or is an ancestor of the target, reconcile and close/continue without rerunning implementation. If the landing worktree has staged generated evidence or DDx metadata, retry/preserve the land/evidence step or move to operator attention according to the existing local reliability policy; do not escalate the model for this class.
- Classify decomposed-parent/no-changes rationale as execution-ineligible or operator work before another implementation attempt. Stronger models cannot fix parent beads that are already split into executable children.
- Make worker result JSON and event bodies carry enough audit fields to explain the decision: `failure_class`, `retry_action`, `escalation_count`, requested/actual power/profile/model, review status/verdict, and land/reconcile status.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not change Fizeau route selection or hard-code concrete provider/model names. DDx should continue to pass power/profile intent and let Fizeau choose concrete routes.
- Do not edit Niflheim beads or Niflheim implementation code.
- Do not duplicate the low-level index-contention fix tracked by `ddx-2965925e`; reuse or align with it.
- Do not duplicate the post-commit review/land timeout work tracked by `ddx-b2846610`; this bead requires typed classification and review evidence, not another timeout implementation.
- Do not duplicate the repeated pre-claim warn operator-attention bead `ddx-d67f124e`; this bead uses the Niflheim observation as cross-scenario regression coverage.
- Do not implement a full operator-lane product workflow beyond the state transitions/events needed to keep the worker from blind retry or false close.

INTERSECTIONS
- Parent: `ddx-3106674f` (multi-worker hardening and local reliability gates). This bead is the external Niflheim regression/incident closure layer under that reliability epic.
- Dependency: `ddx-2965925e` covers tracker commit index contention. This bead must not reimplement that fix; it must prove land/ref/index/evidence failures classify separately from model failure once the low-level path is hardened.
- Dependency: `ddx-b2846610` covers bounded post-commit review/land. This bead must still require typed review/land outcomes after the timeout wedge is fixed.
- Dependency: `ddx-d67f124e` covers same-fingerprint pre-claim warning escalation. This bead adds the Niflheim `niflheim-a62d31ce` family as regression evidence.
- Related but not a dependency: `ddx-e1a576a7` is an execution-ineligible decomposed parent and a local precedent for the `niflheim-64217cc5` class.
- Related quality framework: `ddx-a9d130d0` and `ddx-ac10e7af` own broader bead lifecycle/operator-lane policy; this bead is concrete worker behavior and evidence gating.","events_attachment":"ddx-334c0958/events.jsonl","execution-eligible":false,"id":"ddx-334c0958","issue_type":"bug","labels":["adr:024","area:agent","area:execute-bead","area:review","area:workers","incident:niflheim-2026-06-06","kind:bug","kind:reliability","phase:iterate","prevention","severity:critical","spec:FEAT-010","spec:TP-021"],"notes":"decomposed into ddx-044cd257, ddx-3a6f1ad6, ddx-d4817c9b, ddx-01aa7b18, ddx-1865eadc","parent":"ddx-3106674f","priority":0,"queue-rank":1850,"schema_version":1,"spec-id":"FEAT-010","status":"closed","title":"execute-bead worker: gate success on review evidence and classify Niflheim retry failures","updated_at":"2026-06-17T20:08:03.826920996Z","work-no-changes-count":1}

Parent bead ddx-130cf9ab is too broad for one execution pass. This child owns part 3 of 3 of that parent scope.

ROOT CAUSE
PROBLEM
Parent bead ddx-e19185aa is too broad for one execution pass. This child owns part 2 of 3 of that parent scope.

ROOT CAUSE
The decomposition action (currently invoked when a bead is too large for one execution and the agent splits it into child beads) is writing the parent's ID as a dep edge on every child. This produces an unbreakable deadlock:

  e29892a4 (parent, open) -- parent --> child_A
  e29892a4 -- parent --> child_B
  child_A.deps = [e29892a4]           <- BAD: parent is also a dep
  child_B.deps = [child_A, e29892a4]  <- BAD

The parent stays open until its children close. Every child is blocked on the parent. Nothing can be claimed. ddx work loops on the parent, the orchestrator emits 'mixed_commit_and_no_changes_rationale', the worker retries, and burns claim budget without progress.

Observed scenario (niflheim, 2026-06-04 04:30-05:13 UTC):
- Parent: niflheim-e29892a4 ('feat(delta): async write-behind ...')
- 10 children created during planning loops (23223661, a44a8ceb, 054c89b7, 0470ba0d, f10e9e35, f076b08c, 6c041162, 94e1df73, 9da62c2d, 5e4f9fb2)
- Every child carried deps=[..siblings.., niflheim-e29892a4]
- ~50 minutes of agent time + 4 strong-policy claude/opus attempts with zero feature commits; only tracker chores
- Unwedged manually by closing the parent via 'ddx bead close', after which siblings drained correctly in foundational order

Root cause is structural, not model-capability — auto-upgrading to a stronger model does NOT fix this. The orchestrator already detects the no-progress state but its only remediation is 'retry parent', which re-enters the same loop.

There is also a 'project root mutation rejected (continuing)' warning emitted by the readiness check during this state; that's a downstream symptom of the same problem, not the cause.

PROPOSED FIX
Implement only this child acceptance slice:
- Programmatic decomposition path (the action that fans a too-large bead into children) MUST NOT add the parent as a dep. Existing action templates / system prompts are audited and amended; one golden e2e test (decompose_does_not_self_dep) creates a bead with N children and asserts every child's deps set excludes the parent.
- 'ddx bead doctor' (existing subcommand) gains a check 'parent_ancestor_in_deps' that scans the tracker, lists violating beads, and (with --fix) removes the offending edges. Output is stable / scriptable.

The execute-bead prompt template tells the agent to commit:
  '4. Commit once, when everything is green. Stage only the files you intentionally changed with git add <specific-paths>...'

So agent self-commit is the contract. But:

(a) When typecheck/lint/test fails, the agent is told to fix or write no_changes_rationale.txt — but apparently many agents are bailing silently without writing the rationale either, so the harness sees a clean worktree (no diff staged or committed) and treats it as 'agent made no commits'. The iteration then carries only the auto-generated execution evidence.

(b) There may also be a path where the agent commits but a pre-commit hook silently rejects it. Have not confirmed; the worktree was reaped before I could inspect.

The issue spans multiple harnesses (claude, codex via embedded openrouter), so it is not specific to one provider's commit semantics. Either:
  - The harness should reject 'agent made no commits AND no no_changes_rationale.txt' as a separate failure class instead of silently letting ddx-land-coordinator land an empty iteration
  - The agent prompt should make rationale-writing on incomplete work non-optional with stronger language
  - The harness should run a final-stage check 'did the agent produce ANY signal — commit OR rationale — and if not, treat as harness-execution-failed and surface to the operator'","id":"ddx-4e0a4a59","issue_type":"bug","labels":["area:agent","area:harness","area:execute-bead","kind:correctness"],"owner":"erik","priority":1,"schema_version":1,"status":"closed","title":"execute-bead: agents make edits but skip git commit; iteration commits land only execution evidence","updated_at":"2026-04-30T12:43:46.370829758Z","work-heartbeat-at":"2026-04-30T12:43:25.451101407Z"}

Documentation: update FEAT-006 (or wherever the cooldown policy is documented) to describe Disrupted vs no-progress and the policy rationale.","archived_at":"2026-05-07T04:29:14Z","claimed-at":"2026-05-03T02:17:24Z","claimed-machine":"eitri","claimed-pid":"163374","closing_commit_sha":"89473d084d436e925934549d31f560e5d6b8702a","created_at":"2026-05-03T01:47:21.609526243Z","description":"The current cooldown logic treats any ExecutionFailed status as evidence the model can't make progress, applying noProgressCooldown=6h when BaseRev == ResultRev (cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:1074-1086 via shouldSuppressNoProgress at line 1550). This silently freezes important work whenever a worker is disrupted (killed, server restart, network blip, OS issue) — disruption produces ExecutionFailed + no commit, indistinguishable from "model genuinely tried and produced nothing."

OBSERVED (today, 2026-05-02)
After the picker fix investigation found the four "starved" P0 beads (ddx-dc157075, ddx-aee651ec, ddx-29058e2a, ddx-9d55601f) all had work-retry-after timestamps ~3 hours in the future. Investigation per ddx-9d55601f confirmed these were NOT genuine model failures — they were prior worker disruptions during this development session (worker kills, server restarts) being treated as model-gave-up.

User directive: "Cooldown should be reserved for cases where the model is truly unable to make progress on a task."

CURRENT COOLDOWN POLICY (cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go)
- LandConflictCooldown = 15min — short cooldown after merge/land conflicts (line 64)
- noProgressCooldown = 6h default — when shouldSuppressNoProgress is true (line 367)
- MaxLoopCooldown = 24h — push_failed, push_conflict, decomposition decline (line 57)

shouldSuppressNoProgress (line 1550): returns true if BaseRev != "" AND ResultRev != "" AND BaseRev == ResultRev. This catches:
- Model wrote nothing AND came back cleanly (true no_progress) ✓
- Worker killed mid-execution before commit (FALSE no_progress — disruption)
- Network/provider error before model started (FALSE no_progress — disruption)
- Routing preflight rejection (FALSE no_progress — config issue)

The mis-classification is the bug.

PROPOSED FIX

Add a Disposition::Disrupted variant (or equivalent flag on ExecuteBeadReport) that explicitly marks results that are NOT evidence of model inability:
- ctx.Err() != nil (cancellation, deadline, parent died)
- syscall.SIGTERM / SIGINT received during execution
- Provider-side 5xx, connection refused, timeout (transport disruption)
- Worker process killed (parent never received clean result)
- Server-restart-detected marker
- Routing preflight rejection (operator config issue, not model failure)

For Disrupted: skip cooldown entirely (or use a tiny defer like 30s) and unclaim the bead so the next worker pickup is immediate.
For genuine no-progress (model returned clean with no commit AND rationale present): keep current 6h cooldown.

ALTERNATIVE: exponential backoff per bead. First failure: 5min; second: 30min; third: 2h; cap at 24h. Resets on any successful attempt or operator clear. This is more forgiving than the current flat 6h on the first hiccup.

PROBABLY BOTH: Disrupted skips cooldown entirely; non-Disrupted failures use exponential backoff starting at 5min, not flat 6h.

DETECTION POINTERS

- ctx.Err() check at the call site that produces ExecutionFailed (line 513, 629, 637, 1011)
- Check for `.ddx/executions/<run-id>/no_changes_rationale.txt` presence — if missing, the model never wrote one (likely disruption)
- Check the executor's exit code: SIGKILL/SIGTERM = disruption; clean exit with empty result = no-progress
- Track parent process restart count or boot ID; if changed since the attempt started, mark Disrupted

NOT IN SCOPE
- Changing the cooldown duration constants themselves
- Reworking the per-bead retry policy framework (separate concern)
- Changing land-conflict or push-failed cooldowns (those are correct as-is)
- The exponential-backoff alternative is OPTIONAL; primary fix is Disrupted classification

INTERSECTION WITH OTHER BEADS
- ddx-dc157075 (worker stay-alive): the stay-alive fix prevents some classes of disruption (worker no longer exits on empty poll), but does NOT fix the cooldown-on-disruption mis-classification
- ddx-076147ee (worker client-server architecture TD): if workers become long-lived clients, server-restart disruption goes away by construction. But this fix is needed BEFORE that TD is implemented because the disruption classification is needed regardless.
- ddx-9228a484 C9 (StopCondition enum): may absorb this — the StopCondition enum could include Disrupted as a state. 3. cd cli && go test ./... passes.
4. lefthook run pre-commit passes.","archived_at":"2026-07-02T22:52:12Z","closing_commit_sha":"2bbb93e87a3920ca453101fdeaea9565f382c185","created_at":"2026-06-06T03:22:30.475161508Z","description":"PROBLEM
Parent bead ddx-e19185aa is too broad for one execution pass. This child owns part 1 of 3 of that parent scope.

ROOT CAUSE
The decomposition action (currently invoked when a bead is too large for one execution and the agent splits it into child beads) is writing the parent's ID as a dep edge on every child. This produces an unbreakable deadlock:

  e29892a4 (parent, open) -- parent --> child_A
  e29892a4 -- parent --> child_B
  child_A.deps = [e29892a4]           <- BAD: parent is also a dep
  child_B.deps = [child_A, e29892a4]  <- BAD

The parent stays open until its children close. Every child is blocked on the parent. Nothing can be claimed. ddx work loops on the parent, the orchestrator emits 'mixed_commit_and_no_changes_rationale', the worker retries, and burns claim budget without progress.

Observed scenario (niflheim, 2026-06-04 04:30-05:13 UTC):
- Parent: niflheim-e29892a4 ('feat(delta): async write-behind ...')
- 10 children created during planning loops (23223661, a44a8ceb, 054c89b7, 0470ba0d, f10e9e35, f076b08c, 6c041162, 94e1df73, 9da62c2d, 5e4f9fb2)
- Every child carried deps=[..siblings.., niflheim-e29892a4]
- ~50 minutes of agent time + 4 strong-policy claude/opus attempts with zero feature commits; only tracker chores
- Unwedged manually by closing the parent via 'ddx bead close', after which siblings drained correctly in foundational order

Root cause is structural, not model-capability — auto-upgrading to a stronger model does NOT fix this. The orchestrator already detects the no-progress state but its only remediation is 'retry parent', which re-enters the same loop.

There is also a 'project root mutation rejected (continuing)' warning emitted by the readiness check during this state; that's a downstream symptom of the same problem, not the cause.

PROPOSED FIX
Implement only this child acceptance slice:
- 'ddx bead dep add <bead> <dep>' HARD-REJECTS the edge when <dep> is <bead>'s parent OR any ancestor in the parent chain. Error message names the offending ancestor and prints the relevant parent-chain. Test: ddx_bead_dep_add_rejects_parent_ancestor.
- 'ddx bead create --depends-on X --parent Y' with X == Y (or X an ancestor of Y) HARD-REJECTS at creation time with the same error shape as AC #1. No silent rewrite.

NON-SCOPE
Do not implement parent acceptance criteria assigned to sibling child beads. Preserve every parent non-scope constraint unless this child explicitly narrows it.","events_attachment":"ddx-63780ef6/events.jsonl","id":"ddx-63780ef6","issue_type":"task","labels":["area:tracker","area:lifecycle","area:decomposition","kind:safety","kind:correctness","ac-quality:needs-refinement","decomposed"],"parent":"ddx-e19185aa","priority":0,"queue-rank":1790,"schema_version":1,"session_id":"eb-7e9e7432","status":"closed","title":"Reject bead dep edges that re-introduce the parent or any ancestor: part 1","updated_at":"2026-06-13T13:58:34.845842756Z","work-no-changes-count":4}

This is the test gap the opus test-plan review identified as the most critical coverage hole: every "concurrency" or "merge race" test today uses in-memory fakes with per-worker orchestrators, so the shared-writer invariant the meta-bead design depends on is never actually exercised.

## Test infrastructure helper

Build a shared helper `newScriptHarnessRepo(t *testing.T) (projectRoot, initialRev string)` in a new file `cli/internal/agent/integration_helper_test.go` that:

1. Creates a temp dir via t.TempDir()
2. Runs `git init` with scrubbed GIT_* env (so the test's own pre-commit hook env doesn't leak)
3. Creates and commits an initial seed commit on `main`
4. Seeds .ddx/beads.jsonl with N ready beads (parameterized by test)
5. Returns projectRoot + the initial SHA

The helper must NOT inherit any GIT_DIR from the parent test environment.

## Test cases

Create cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_integration_test.go with these cases. Each runs against the script harness and real git:

1. **TestIntegration_ScriptHarness_SingleBead_AppendLine_Merged** — one bead, directive appends a line and commits, asserts git log main shows +1 commit, worktree is cleaned up, bead closed with closing_commit_sha.

2. **TestIntegration_ScriptHarness_NoOp_ClassifiedAsNoChanges** — directive no-op, asserts outcome=no-changes, bead stays open (below adjudication threshold), no commit on main.

3. **TestIntegration_ScriptHarness_DirtyWorktreeSynthesized** — directive creates files but omits explicit commit, asserts SynthesizeCommit fires, outcome=merged, single synthetic commit on main with the expected files.

4. **TestIntegration_ScriptHarness_FailedExit_WithCommits_Preserved** — directive commits then set-exit 1, asserts outcome=preserved, refs/ddx/iterations/<bead>/<ts>-<sha> exists, main unchanged.

5. **TestIntegration_ScriptHarness_FiveConcurrentBeads_LinearHistory** — seed 5 distinct beads, spawn 5 workers concurrently (single store, single projectRoot), assert `git log --merges main | wc -l` is 0, `git rev-list main ^initial | wc -l` is 5, every bead closed. **This is the ddx-6aa50e57 acceptance test that cannot be written today.**

6. **TestIntegration_ScriptHarness_TwoWorkersSameBead_ClaimedOnce** — seed 1 bead, spawn 2 workers against the same store, assert exactly one executes and the other reports no_ready_work. **This is the ddx-3315dce2 acceptance test.**

7. **TestIntegration_ScriptHarness_RebaseConflict_Preserved** — two beads both modify the same line in the same file, workers start from the same base, first lands cleanly, second gets rebase conflict and is preserved under refs/ddx/iterations/. Main has exactly +1 commit, ref exists for bead 2. (Only passes once the land coordinator from ddx-8746d8a6 is in place.)

8. **TestIntegration_ScriptHarness_ContextCancelBetweenIterations** — two beads, cancel context after first completes, assert second bead is not claimed. **This is the regression for commit 21d16e6.**

9. **TestIntegration_ScriptHarness_NoChangesRationale_ClosesBeadFast** — directive writes a no_changes_rationale.txt citing a commit SHA, asserts the bead closes as already_satisfied without the 3-strike cooldown wait.

Each test runs in isolation, cleans up its temp dir, and completes in under 10s. The full suite runs in under 60s. Use `-race` to catch any coordinator goroutine races.

## Out of scope

- Server-layer wiring (that's bead C)
- Real-AI acceptance tests (gated tier 4)
- Multi-project isolation (a separate bead, not filed here)
- Cross-machine scenarios (multi-machine topology bead)","id":"ddx-6f6bf12a","issue_type":"task","labels":["area:agent","area:testing","kind:integration","phase:build"],"parent":"ddx-8746d8a6","priority":0,"schema_version":1,"session_id":"eb-43009cc","status":"closed","title":"Integration tests for work orchestration using script harness + real git","updated_at":"2026-04-14T15:26:48.671814475Z"}

.ddx/beads-archive.jsonl:1083:{"acceptance":"1. `TestDefaultVerificationCommandRunnerTimeoutKillsProcessGroup` proves a timed-out nested shell command (`sh -lc 'sleep ...'` or equivalent fixture) leaves no child/grandchild process alive after the runner returns.
2. `TestDefaultVerificationCommandRunnerAllowsConfiguredLongGate` or equivalent proves the verification timeout is configurable/worker-derived and can run a command longer than 60s without forcing `no_changes_unverified`.
3. `TestExecuteBeadWorkerNoChangesVerifiedLongCommandCloses` proves a `verification_command` that runs longer than the old 60s limit but exits 0 closes the bead as `already_satisfied` and emits `no_changes_verified`.
4. `TestExecuteBeadWorkerNoChangesVerificationTimeoutKeepsOpenAndReaps` proves timeout keeps the bead open with `triage:no-changes-unverified`, records timeout evidence, and leaves no verification process tree behind.
5. `cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... -run 'Test(DefaultVerificationCommandRunner|ExecuteBeadWorkerNoChanges.*Verification)' -count=1` passes.
6. `lefthook run pre-commit` passes.","archived_at":"2026-07-02T22:52:12Z","closing_commit_sha":"f609f22ef84a0d7b05ba89dd3e9fd5b92fbfa446","created_at":"2026-05-15T13:09:07.942514913Z","description":"PROBLEM
A `no_changes_rationale.txt` with `verification_command:` can time out, leave its child process tree running, and let `ddx work --watch` move on to another bead while the old verification is still consuming the project. Live reproduction on 2026-05-15 in `/Users/erik/Projects/fizeau`: `fizeau-bc9586d7` returned `verification_command: sh -lc "go test ... && lefthook run pre-commit"`; the watcher logged `• fizeau-bc9586d7 → no_changes` and started `fizeau-7af3f548` at 13:07:21Z, while `ps` still showed orphaned PID 3510889 (`sh -lc go test ...`) and child PID 3512539 (`go test ./... -count=1`) under PPID 1.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
`cli/internal/agent/try/no_changes.go:117-128` hard-codes `DefaultVerificationCommandTimeout = 60 * time.Second` and runs the command with `exec.CommandContext(cctx, "/bin/sh", "-c", command)`. `exec.CommandContext` kills only the direct shell on timeout; because workers commonly emit nested shell commands such as `sh -lc "go test ..."`, the actual test process survives as an orphan. The 60s timeout is also shorter than normal bead acceptance gates like `go test ./...` plus `lefthook run pre-commit`, so legitimate already-satisfied checks are classified as unverified/no-progress instead of closing the bead.

PROPOSED FIX
Run no_changes verification commands with the same process-tree discipline used by agent executors: start the shell in its own process group on Unix, terminate the group on timeout/cancel, and wait/reap before returning. Replace the hard-coded 60s ceiling with a worker/config-derived timeout that is long enough for normal acceptance gates, or make it configurable with a conservative default. Preserve the contract: exit 0 closes as `already_satisfied`; non-zero/timeout keeps the bead open with clear `no_changes_unverified` evidence.

NON-SCOPE
Do not weaken no_changes verification semantics. Do not let verification run asynchronously. Do not close a bead without a passing command. Do not change readiness classification or route selection.","events_attachment":"ddx-8a0aea8e/events.jsonl","id":"ddx-8a0aea8e","issue_type":"bug","labels":["area:agent","area:work","kind:bug","operator-ux","downstream:fizeau","dirty-tree"],"priority":0,"queue-rank":600,"schema_version":1,"session_id":"eb-af6f8fc7","status":"closed","title":"work: reap and correctly budget no_changes verification commands","updated_at":"2026-06-13T13:58:29.795710244Z"}
