There is no automated post-merge review step validating the commit against the bead's AC. Real-world practice requires a second-opinion agent (typically a higher-capability tier) to produce APPROVE / REQUEST_CHANGES.

## Principle

Review is on-by-default with zero flags. The tool picks a tier >= the implementation tier (defaulting to smart). The user overrides only in exceptional cases.

## Default flow

- Implementation agent runs at current tier (see ddx-dc231144 tier-based escalation)
- On successful merge, tool automatically renders a review prompt via 'ddx bead review' (ddx-fdf031c9)
- Review agent runs at max(impl_tier + 1, smart)
- Review result is structured JSON: { verdict: APPROVE | REQUEST_CHANGES | BLOCK, findings: [...] }
- APPROVE → close bead, attach review evidence
- REQUEST_CHANGES → reopen bead via 'ddx bead reopen' (ddx-44084b3e), append review body, let the tier-escalation policy pick the next attempt
- BLOCK → stop escalating, flag for human

## Rare overrides

- --no-review (skip review entirely — e.g., doc-only beads in a tight loop)
- --review-harness / --review-model (explicit reviewer override for debug)
- Per-bead opt-out via a 'review:skip' label on the bead

## Dependencies

- ddx-fdf031c9 (ddx bead review command) provides the review prompt template
- ddx-44084b3e (ddx bead reopen) provides the atomic reopen primitive
- ddx-518b2690 (plain --output text) makes extracting the review verdict trivial
- ddx-5bac3c6b (provider health probe) feeds the reviewer tier selection

## Context

Observed during axon ADR-018 work:
- qwen3-coder-next merged A1 with real AC gaps (missing round-trip tests, zero Postgres coverage, scope creep)
- Without review, the bead would have closed silently and the gaps would surface only at epic rollup — much later and much more expensive to fix
- Review took 41s + a few minutes of reading. Cheap relative to the real cost of shipping broken code downstream.","events_attachment":"ddx-a317208c/events.jsonl","id":"ddx-a317208c","issue_type":"task","labels":["helix","area:agent","area:exec","kind:feature","gap:review-loop"],"notes":"<operator-feedback 2026-04-15>
Critical gap discovered during axon ADR-018 work:

When a bead is reopened after a REQUEST_CHANGES review, the REVIEW FINDINGS must be surfaced to the next execute-bead attempt. Today this fails silently because:

1. Review findings posted via 'ddx bead update --notes' are NOT included in the execute-bead prompt.md. Execute-bead threads only <description> and <acceptance> into the XML payload.

2. Therefore a reopen-via-notes does NOT communicate the review findings to the next agent. The agent reads the original description + AC, sees the existing code already matches the description, and declares 'already_satisfied' — auto-closing the bead.

3. The workaround I had to use was updating the DESCRIPTION directly with the review findings. But then the description is no longer a stable spec — it's a moving target carrying review history.

## Required behavior

When a bead is reopened with a review verdict attached, execute-bead's generated prompt MUST include the most recent REQUEST_CHANGES review findings as a distinct section, e.g.:

  <review-findings iteration="2" reviewer="claude-opus-4-6">
  BLOCKING: UserIdentity field 'subject' should be 'external_id' per ADR-018 §3
  PARTIAL: aud-array test asserts serde_json::Error instead of AuthError::CredentialMalformed
  ...
  </review-findings>

And the execute-bead instructions should read: 'If <review-findings> is present, address each BLOCKING finding before claiming the work complete. Do not declare already_satisfied if any BLOCKING finding is unaddressed.'

## Observed failure mode

Sonnet at iter 2 of axon-43ed769b read the prompt (which had the original description with 'subject' and the unchanged AC), verified all type shapes match, ran tests green, and wrote a no_changes_rationale declaring the work complete. The 'already_satisfied' close path auto-closed the bead. The actual BLOCKING issue (wrong field name) was invisible to the execute-bead layer. I had to manually reopen and rewrite the description with fix-up instructions inline.

## This should be part of the post-review epic scope

Making review findings round-trip through the prompt is half of what 'post-review agent in work' means. Adding to this bead's scope:

- Review verdict JSON includes a 'findings' array with severity (blocking|partial|nit) and location
- Reopen path persists the findings to the bead
- execute-bead prompt rendering pulls latest review findings and injects them as a distinct XML section
- Execute-bead instructions teach the agent to treat blocking findings as first-class work items
- no_changes / already_satisfied paths check for unaddressed blocking findings before closing

When a spoke node starts with --hub-address, it must register with the hub and maintain a heartbeat so the hub's FederationRegistry knows the spoke is alive. Today no --hub-address flag exists and no spoke lifecycle (register / heartbeat / stale detection / URL-change re-register) is implemented.

ROOT CAUSE
- cli/internal/server/server.go: no --hub-address flag wired.
- cli/internal/federation/: no spoke-side registration client or heartbeat loop exists.
- The hub's FederationRegistry (from ddx-90f48012, a dep) provides the register endpoint and stale detection, but the spoke side has no code to call it.
- Per locked decisions from the federation planning: register is idempotent on stable node_id; duplicate node_id rejected (not silently merged); heartbeat jittered at 30s; stale threshold at 2 minutes.

PROPOSED FIX
- Add --hub-address string flag to the server command (cli/cmd/server.go or equivalent).
- Add cli/internal/federation/spoke_lifecycle.go:
  - RegisterWithHub(ctx, hubAddress, nodeID, localURL): idempotent POST to hub register endpoint.
  - StartHeartbeat(ctx, hubAddress, nodeID): jittered 30s heartbeat loop; on failure → stale detection.
  - HandleURLChange(ctx, hubAddress, nodeID, newURL): re-register when local URL flips.
- Stale threshold: hub marks spoke offline after 2 minutes without heartbeat.

NON-SCOPE
- Hub-side registry implementation (ddx-90f48012, a dep).
- Federation query fan-out (existing fanout.go).
- Mutation routing (S15-7b / ddx-eb75a32d).","events_attachment":"ddx-b69f04f8/events.jsonl","id":"ddx-b69f04f8","issue_type":"task","labels":["phase:2","story:14","area:server","kind:feature","triage:no-changes-unjustified"],"notes":"REVIEW:BLOCK

Beads stored in .ddx/beads.jsonl and .ddx/beads-archive.jsonl cannot be migrated to the axon backend without a dedicated migration command. Operators switching to the axon backend must manually reconstruct state or risk data loss.

ROOT CAUSE
- cli/cmd/ does not contain a 'ddx bead migrate' subcommand or --to flag.
- cli/internal/bead/ has backend_jsonl.go (JSONL reader) but no migration path to write into the axon Backend interface (ddx-95ec5ed5, a dep).
- Without an idempotent migration, re-running risks duplicates; without a round-trip check, data loss is silent.

PROPOSED FIX
- Add ddx bead migrate --to axon command under cli/cmd/ reading .ddx/beads.jsonl and .ddx/beads-archive.jsonl and writing losslessly into the axon backend via the Backend interface.
- Idempotent: re-running on an already-migrated store is a no-op (dedup by bead ID).
- Does not delete source files (operator removes after verification).
- Add test fixture under cli/internal/bead/testdata/ covering: representative beads.jsonl + archive.

NON-SCOPE
- Axon backend implementation (ddx-95ec5ed5, a dep).
- Migration to backends other than axon.
- Automatic deletion of source files.","events_attachment":"ddx-cb63cdfc/events.jsonl","id":"ddx-cb63cdfc","issue_type":"task","labels":["phase:2","area:beads","area:storage","kind:tool","backend-migration","triage:no-changes-unjustified"],"notes":"REVIEW:BLOCK

A parent bead that has already been decomposed can still re-enter the worker as if it were an executable task. That keeps spending attempts on a structurally ineligible parent instead of moving on to its children.

ROOT CAUSE
`cli/internal/agent/recovery_decompose.go:130-210` already marks decomposed children with `Parent=beadID` and sets the parent execution-eligible flag to false, but the main worker loop still has paths that continue past decomposition-like intake and retry handling. `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:2175-2315` handles some intake decomposition cases, and `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:3818-3840` continues processing other outcome branches without consistently parking a parent whose own `no_changes` rationale says to decompose.

PROPOSED FIX
- When the worker sees `mixed_commit_and_no_changes_rationale` with `orchestrator_action: decompose`, child IDs, and `outcome_reason=decomposed`, mark the parent execution-ineligible or proposed before another implementation attempt.
- Do not schedule another implementation run or min-power escalation for that parent.
- Keep the parent visible as a decomposition boundary, not as a retriable implementation failure.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not change child-bead content, land handling, or review gating in this child.
- Do not alter the decomposer output format beyond what is needed to detect the decomposed-parent state.","events_attachment":"ddx-d4817c9b/events.jsonl","id":"ddx-d4817c9b","issue_type":"bug","labels":["phase:iterate","area:agent","area:execute-bead","area:workers","area:review","kind:bug","kind:reliability","severity:critical","spec:FEAT-010","spec:TP-021","adr:024","incident:niflheim-2026-06-06","prevention"],"parent":"ddx-334c0958","priority":0,"queue-rank":2050,"schema_version":1,"session_id":"eb-5e04821f","spec-id":"FEAT-010","status":"closed","title":"agent: park decomposed parents before retrying implementation","updated_at":"2026-06-13T13:58:35.835830846Z"}

AC4. Post-merge typecheck/build is run as a final gate (project-level command from .ddx/config.yaml or sensible default like 'cargo check' / 'bun run build'). If post-merge build fails, AUTOMATICALLY revert the merge and preserve the result instead. This is the safety net for cases AC2 doesn't catch.

AC5. Documentation in docs/ explains the safety gates, how to override them when intentional (e.g. for genuine large deletions), and what 'preserved-needs-review' means operationally.

The same merge also corrupted a second test file (1043 → 158 lines, overwritten with unrelated content). Master became unbuildable: vite-plugin-svelte threw 'Expected token }' because the policies/+page.svelte file lost its <script lang="ts"> wrapper along with the entire HTML template and style sections.

The damaging merge:

  commit be1073f4333c289e48680cf8c05ceefa7ea9c075
  Merge: 0c5557b 2f35ad2
  Author: ddx-land-coordinator <coordinator@ddx.local>
  Date:   Wed Apr 29 20:56:46 2026 -0400

      Merge bead axon-80979cb8 attempt 20260430T005434- into master

       .../databases/[database]/policies/+page.svelte     | 1448 --------------------
       1 file changed, 1448 deletions(-)

The post-merge codex reviewer DID block subsequent attempts that built on the broken base ('the diff only adds execution metadata' / 'the new Playwright coverage is incorrect... the collection view never loads'), but the original 1448-line deletion went through unchecked. Five subsequent worker attempts ran on top of the now-broken master, each adding ~80 lines of partial reconstructions, none producing a valid Svelte file.

Net damage on master before manual recovery:
  ui/src/routes/.../policies/+page.svelte      1552 → 186 lines (1366 lines lost)
  ui/tests/e2e/policy-enforcement.spec.ts      1043 → 158 lines (885 lines lost)
  master state: vite-plugin-svelte fails to compile, UI bundle cannot build, docker e2e harness cannot start

Manual recovery (commit bc4b492 on axon master): git checkout HEAD~14 of both files, re-stage, commit, push. 2415 lines restored across 3 files.

Why this happened (hypothesis):
  1. The bead-attempt branch had a partial reconstruction of the file (104 lines of just imports/state, no template).
  2. The land-coordinator did a 3-way merge with master at base_rev. The base_rev was ALSO the partial reconstruction (because EARLIER attempts had already truncated it).
  3. Three-way merge produced a 'clean' delete of the 1448 lines that existed only on master's side because base_rev was already broken.

.ddx/beads-archive.jsonl:1833:{"acceptance":"(1) Root cause documented in code comment next to SynthesizeCommit. (2) SynthesizeCommit completes with exit 0 when only change is in a gitignored directory. (3) Regression test: a project with non-empty .ddx/agent-logs and no real changes yields SynthesizeCommit → (false, nil) (no commit, no error). (4) Regression test: a project with one real modification + non-empty .ddx/agent-logs yields (true, nil) with only the real change committed. (5) ddx work --once drains ddx-672cf2bf or another ready bead end-to-end without hitting staging-changes exit 1.","archived_at":"2026-05-07T04:29:14Z","created_at":"2026-04-20T14:57:38.058551192Z","description":"Blocks `ddx work` whenever the gitignored runtime directories `.ddx/agent-logs/` and `.ddx/workers/` have any content. Every real-world project hits this after the first agent run.

## Symptom

Every tier attempt in the work aborts:

```
pre-execute-bead checkpoint: staging changes: exit status 1
```

## Root cause

cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go:363 `RealGitOps.SynthesizeCommit` runs:

```
git -C <dir> add -A -- . ':(exclude).ddx/agent-logs' ':(exclude).ddx/workers' ':(exclude).ddx/executions/*/embedded' ':(exclude).ddx/executions/*/no_changes_rationale.txt' ':(exclude).claude/skills' ':(exclude).agents/skills'
```

When `.ddx/agent-logs/` and `.ddx/workers/` exist as untracked directories (normal after the first agent run), and both are in .gitignore (lines 421-422), git add emits:

```
The following paths are ignored by one of your .gitignore files:
.ddx/agent-logs
.ddx/workers
hint: Use -f if you really want to add them.
```

AND exits with status 1. The `:(exclude)` magic pathspec is not sufficient to silence this exit-1 in git's add behavior with `-C` form — even `-c advice.addIgnoredFile=false` does not suppress it.

## Fix candidates (not pre-decided)

1. Check `git status --porcelain` first; only run `git add` if there are tracked changes to stage.
2. Enumerate dirty files in Go (via `git status --porcelain`), filter out excluded paths, pass the survivors explicitly to `git add --`.
3. Distinguish stderr "benign ignored-files warning" from real errors; accept exit 1 when that's the only message.
4. Move `.ddx/agent-logs/` and `.ddx/workers/` out of the repo tree so they're outside the pathspec entirely.

Prefer (1) or (2) — they're honest about the intent "stage only real modifications." Option (3) is fragile against git message changes; (4) touches a lot of downstream tooling.

## Evidence

Worker: worker-20260420T145025-61dc. Bead ddx-672cf2bf. All three tier attempts (cheap/standard/smart) failed identically. Confirmed reproducible via manual run of the exact add command in `/Users/erik/Projects/ddx`.

## Relationship to other beads

Independent of ddx-672cf2bf (silent-exit on epic queues) — this is loud-exit on staging. Independent of v0.7.0. rationale: Bead ddx-27936512 is a duplicate of work already merged in commit 0b1b3964
("feat(server): run-detail resolver + tool-call persistence + bundle-file
confinement [ddx-6d132f62]"). All four acceptance criteria are already
satisfied by existing code:

AC1: Run resolver returns full detail (lossless)
  - cli/internal/server/state_runs_detail.go:250 loadRunDetail() populates
    prompt/response/stderr from session bodies and bundleFiles via
    listRunBundleFiles(); invoked from state_runs.go:520 on run(id:) lookup.
  - cli/internal/server/graphql/resolver_runs.go wires Run, RunToolCalls,
    RunBundleFile resolvers to the state methods.

AC2: ToolCallEntry persisted at drain time (NOT log-parsing)
  - agent.SessionIndexEntry carries a ToolCalls []ToolCallEntry field;
    SessionIndexEntryFromResult copies result.ToolCalls into the entry,
    so the persistence happens once at drain time rather than on each
    resolver call. GetRunToolCallsGraphQL reads only the persisted slice.

AC3: bundleFile endpoint refuses path traversal
  - resolveRunBundlePath (state_runs_detail.go:111) rejects absolute paths,
    "..", paths whose Clean escapes the root, and symlink targets that
    EvalSymlinks resolves outside bundleRoot. Surfaced via
    GetRunBundleFileGraphQL → Query.runBundleFile.

AC4: Tests cover all three
  - state_runs_detail_test.go covers: dotdot/absolute/under-workdir
    rejection, symlink-escape rejection, size cap (>64KB → truncated=true),
    whitelist enforcement (only allowed extensions inline), missing-path
    404, bundleFiles listing with size+mime, drain-time tool-call
    persistence (TestRunToolCalls_PersistedAtDrain and
    TestSessionIndexEntryFromResult_PersistsToolCalls), and the
    resolveRunBundlePath helper directly. All pass at base-rev b10e224f.

.ddx/attachments/ddx-dfb1c6d7/events.jsonl:3:{"actor":"erik","body":"status: blocked_by_dependency

what is done:
  - Read cli/internal/bead/chaos_test.go end-to-end (5 chaos tests:
    ConcurrentAppendSafety, AtomicStatusTransitions,
    ConcurrentCloseAndAppend, ConcurrentCloseNotLost,
    JSONLRoundTripIntegrity).
  - Inspected the current Backend interface in
    cli/internal/bead/backend.go: still the legacy 4-method shape
    (Init / ReadAll / WriteAll / WithLock) with constants only for
    BackendJSONL, BackendBD, BackendBR. There is no BackendAxon
    constant and no axon package under cli/internal/bead/.
  - Inspected dependency status in .ddx/beads.jsonl:
      ddx-6bb51a52 (TD: design axon-backend integration)         closed
      ddx-bbdd7564 (Introduce selectable bead Backend interface) OPEN
      ddx-95ec5ed5 (Implement axon Backend for bead tracker)     OPEN
      ddx-c9ef6c4f (Implement axon backend behind backend flag)  closed
        (closed via no_changes_rationale on attempt
        20260503T024421-4ede954b — i.e. the implementation bead
        itself reported blocked_by_dependency and did NOT actually
        implement the backend).
  - Confirmed the chaos tests use the unparameterized
    newTestStore(t) helper, which today returns the JSONL-backed
    Store from store.go. There is no axon Store, no axon Backend
    registration, and no axon test harness in this repo.

what is blocking:
  This bead's acceptance criteria require running the chaos suite
  *against the axon backend* and asserting multi-worker fairness,
  claim correctness under contention, and crash/recovery semantics.
  None of that is verifiable today because:

  1. The axon Backend implementation (ddx-95ec5ed5) is open. There
     is no axon/ subpackage, no generated gRPC client under
     cli/internal/bead/axon/pb/, and no factory entry that maps
     "axon" to a Store. Parameterizing chaos_test.go on a backend
     that does not exist would either (a) fail at construction
     time, or (b) require introducing a stub backend that the AC
     would not accept.

  2. The selectable Backend interface refactor (ddx-bbdd7564) is
     also open. TD-030 (docs/helix/02-design/technical-designs/
     TD-030-axon-bead-backend.md §"Backend interface (Go)")
     explicitly says the current 4-method interface is too coarse
     for axon and must be replaced with a per-entity surface
     (Get / Create / Update with OCC Version, AppendEvent,
     AddDep / RemoveDep, Ready, Close, Export / Import,
     ErrVersionMismatch). Until that refactor lands, there is no
     interface boundary to parameterize the chaos tests against:
     newTestStore(t) returns a concrete *Store, not a Backend, and
     the chaos tests call Store-only methods (Create, Update, Close,
     Get, ReadAll) that are not yet on the Backend contract.

  3. AC #3 ("multi-worker contention scenario verified — no
     double-claim, no lost-update") implies a claim API that does
     not exist on the JSONL Store either; it is part of the TD-030
     redesign (per-entity AppendEvent + OCC Version). Writing the
     claim-contention scenario now would have to invent an API that
     the upstream beads will define.

  4. AC #4 ("CI runs the suite for both backends") requires a
     running axon-server (per TD-030 §D1 / §D4 the axon backend
     refuses all ops when axon is unreachable). No such server is
     available in this execution environment, and there is no
     vendored test harness or "skip if AXON_ADDR unset" gate yet.

  Implementing this bead now would require either:
    (a) reproducing the axon backend, the interface refactor, the
        gRPC client generation, and a test harness in a single
        execute-bead pass — far outside the scope this bead was
        sized for, and explicitly the work of three other beads;
    (b) committing red or stub code (parameterized harness
        targeting a non-existent backend) — which the execute-bead
        contract forbids ("Do not commit partial, exploratory, or
        red code hoping the reviewer will accept it").

what a follow-up attempt would need:
  - ddx-bbdd7564 closes with the TD-030-shaped per-entity Backend
    interface, and chaos_test.go's newTestStore is refactored to
    return that Backend (or a thin adapter exposing the methods the
    chaos tests need: Create, Update, Close, Get, ReadAll, plus the
    new claim/append-event surface AC #3 references).
  - ddx-95ec5ed5 closes with a real axon Backend registered in
    cli/internal/bead/registry.go, plus a test harness AC #4 can
    rely on (either a docker-compose axon-server entry, a
    `go test`-managed server-under-test, or an explicit
    "skip if AXON_ADDR unset" gate consistent with the project's
    test-tier conventions).
  - A prerequisite bead (not yet filed, as far as I can see) for
    the axon gRPC client generation: vendor axon.proto with sha256
    pin, wire `make gen-axon-client`, commit the generated tree
    under cli/internal/bead/axon/pb/.
  - Then this bead becomes focused: parameterize newTestStore on a
    backend factory (table-driven sub-tests for "jsonl" and "axon"
    using the harness above), add the multi-worker claim/contention
    scenario the original chaos suite does not yet cover (AC #3),
    add a CI matrix entry that runs the suite against both
    backends (AC #4), and fix any axon-side issues that the new
    contention scenario surfaces.

  Recommendation for the queue: enforce the dep graph in `ddx bead
  ready` so this bead does not become eligible until ddx-bbdd7564
  AND ddx-95ec5ed5 are both closed. The textual "Depends on …"
  language in the description is not enough; a concrete
  `ddx bead dep add ddx-dfb1c6d7 ddx-95ec5ed5` (and same for
  ddx-bbdd7564) would prevent further premature dispatches that
  burn cost on no_changes_rationale outcomes.

    interface, and chaos_test.go's newTestStore is refactored to
    return that Backend (or a thin adapter exposing the methods the
    chaos tests need: Create, Update, Close, Get, ReadAll, plus the
    new claim/append-event surface AC #3 references).
  - ddx-95ec5ed5 closes with a real axon Backend registered in
    cli/internal/bead/registry.go, plus a test harness AC #4 can
    rely on (either a docker-compose axon-server entry, a
    `go test`-managed server-under-test, or an explicit
    "skip if AXON_ADDR unset" gate consistent with the project's
    test-tier conventions).
  - A prerequisite bead (not yet filed, as far as I can see) for
    the axon gRPC client generation: vendor axon.proto with sha256
    pin, wire `make gen-axon-client`, commit the generated tree
    under cli/internal/bead/axon/pb/.
  - Then this bead becomes focused: parameterize newTestStore on a
    backend factory (table-driven sub-tests for "jsonl" and "axon"
    using the harness above), add the multi-worker claim/contention
    scenario the original chaos suite does not yet cover (AC #3),
    add a CI matrix entry that runs the suite against both
    backends (AC #4), and fix any axon-side issues that the new
    contention scenario surfaces.

  Recommendation for the queue: enforce the dep graph in `ddx bead
  ready` so this bead does not become eligible until ddx-bbdd7564
  AND ddx-95ec5ed5 are both closed. The textual "Depends on …"
  language in the description is not enough; a concrete
  `ddx bead dep add ddx-dfb1c6d7 ddx-95ec5ed5` (and same for
  ddx-bbdd7564) would prevent further premature dispatches that
  burn cost on no_changes_rationale outcomes.

revision 727cf03a. The earlier REVIEW:BLOCK note ("Diff contains only an
execution result.json artifact") referred to a prior attempt; in this base
the actual source landed via commit 81e6d338 ("feat(bead): operator-prompt
issueType, proposed/cancelled status, no-self-mutation guard
[ddx-a8939a2b]") and is present in HEAD's tree.

Evidence per AC:

1. issueType=operator-prompt accepted by validator
   - cli/internal/bead/schema/bead-record.schema.json: issue_type is open
     string (minLength 1); operator-prompt accepted.
   - cli/internal/bead/types.go: const IssueTypeOperatorPrompt =
     "operator-prompt".
   - Test: TestSchemaAcceptsOperatorPromptIssueType.

2. 'proposed' status recognized in state machine with valid transitions
   (proposed→ready/open, proposed→cancelled)
   - schema enum: ["open","in_progress","closed","blocked","proposed",
     "cancelled"].
   - types.go: StatusProposed/StatusCancelled constants; CanonicalStatuses
     includes both; IsValidStatusTransition allows proposed→open and
     proposed→cancelled and rejects every other proposed transition.
   - Test: TestStatusTransitionsForProposed (covers allow/deny matrix
     including proposed self-loop and terminal closed/cancelled).

3. Template applied on create populates labels/tier/auto-AC
   - types.go: NewOperatorPromptBead applies labels
     [kind:operator-prompt, source:web-ui], clamps tier to
     MinPriority..MaxPriority, sets Acceptance =
     OperatorPromptDefaultAcceptance, preserves prompt body verbatim in
     Description and uses first non-empty line as Title (with
     "(empty operator prompt)" fallback).
   - Tests: TestStoreAcceptsOperatorPromptBead, TestNewOperatorPromptBeadDefaults.

4. Structural AC check skipped for issueType=operator-prompt
   - OperatorPromptDefaultAcceptance stub stands as the contract; the
     template populates Acceptance unconditionally so structural checks
     that key on a non-empty acceptance pass without enforcing pre-authored
     AC. Default value asserted in TestStoreAcceptsOperatorPromptBead.

5. Runtime guard prevents operator-prompt beads from mutating
   operator-prompt beads
   - types.go: OperatorPromptMutationGuard implements the 2x2 matrix
     (only operator-prompt actor + operator-prompt target denied).
   - cli/internal/bead/store.go (~line 462): Store.Create calls the guard
     with os.Getenv("DDX_ACTOR_ISSUE_TYPE") and rejects self-mutation.
   - Tests: TestOperatorPromptMutationGuardMatrix (allow/deny matrix),
     TestStoreCreateRejectsOperatorPromptSelfMutation (live store path
     keyed off DDX_ACTOR_ISSUE_TYPE).

All listed tests pass under `go test ./internal/bead/` against this base
(see verification_command above).

This bead does not require a code change in the current execution worktree.

Evidence:
- `cli/internal/server/frontend/src/routes/nodes/[nodeId]/projects/[projectId]/efficacy/+page.svelte:332-405` already renders the "Top performers" dashboard tile with `data-testid="top-performers-tile"` and top-N cards.

- `cli/internal/server/frontend/src/routes/nodes/[nodeId]/projects/[projectId]/efficacy/+page.ts:5-24` already requests `sparkline` in the GraphQL query and exposes it on `EfficacyRow`.
- `cli/internal/server/frontend/e2e/efficacy.spec.ts:209-223` already has `US-096.a2: efficacy sparkline column and top-performers tile render`, which asserts the sparkline column, tile, top-performer cards, and the row-level sparkline bars.

Conclusion:
- The requested feature is already implemented and covered by tests in the current tree.
- No additional file edits are necessary for this bead.

  - TestExecuteBeadResult_NiflheimEvidence_ReviewDecisionAudit (with subtest "pre claim warning escalation carries audit in emitted and durable events")

AC3: lefthook run pre-commit passes (clean worktree, no staged files, all hooks skipped as expected).

The Niflheim-style pre-claim fixture covering repeated system_unready/timeout warnings with generated DDx artifact dirt (.ddx/harness-sessions/...) is already implemented in cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_decision_audit_test.go:239 (TestExecuteBeadResult_NiflheimEvidence_PreClaimDecisionAudit). .ddx/attachments/ddx-57c4cd99/events.jsonl:3:{"actor":"erik","body":"status: blocked_by_dependency

what is done:
  Verified the current state of the dependency chain and the codebase
  surface this bead would build against.

what is blocking:
  This bead's <description> declares: "Depends on the axon backend
  implementation child." The parent epic ddx-5d49b14e was decomposed
  on 2026-05-02 23:10 into seven children. The migration tool sits
  downstream of:

    - ddx-6bb51a52  TD: design axon-backend integration         (status: ?)
    - ddx-bbdd7564  Introduce selectable bead Backend interface (status: ?)
    - ddx-95ec5ed5  Implement axon Backend for bead tracker     (status: open)

  ddx-95ec5ed5 is still open. The repo confirms this: there is no
  axon package under cli/internal/bead/ (no `axon/` directory, no
  axon_backend.go), no generated gRPC client (no `cli/internal/bead/
  axon/pb/`), no `axon.proto` checked in, and no `BackendAxon` constant
  in cli/internal/bead/backend.go (the file still lists only
  BackendJSONL, BackendBD, BackendBR and a 4-method Backend interface
  WriteAll/ReadAll/Init/WithLock — i.e. the pre-TD-030 surface).

  Without the axon Backend implementation, there is no target API to
  migrate into. Per TD-030 §"Migration plan" the migration command
  must call `CreateEntity`, `CreateLink`, and `CommitTransaction`
  against the axon backend; none of those entry points exist in the
  Go tree yet. Building the migration tool now would require either:
    (a) inventing the axon backend in this same pass — out of scope,
        and ddx-95ec5ed5 is sized as its own bead, or
    (b) writing the migrator against a stub interface that will be
        churned once ddx-95ec5ed5 lands — wasted code that cannot be
        verified end-to-end, and AC #5 (integration test running
        migration on a fixture repo and asserting equivalence) is
        not satisfiable without a real backend to round-trip against.

  The acceptance criteria explicitly require lossless migration plus
  a verification pass; both are unverifiable until the backend exists.

what a follow-up attempt would need:
  ddx-95ec5ed5 (and its upstreams ddx-bbdd7564 and ddx-6bb51a52) must
  close first. Once the axon Backend is registered in the factory
  (cli/internal/bead/registry.go) and exposes the per-entity surface
  described in TD-030 §"Backend interface (Go)" — Create / Get /
  Update / AppendEvent / AddDep / Close / Export / Import — this bead
  becomes straightforward: extend cli/cmd/bead_migrate.go with a
  `--to=axon` flag, iterate beads.jsonl + beads-archive.jsonl + any
  per-bead .ddx/attachments/<id>/events.jsonl sidecars, drive the
  axon Backend per the TD-030 Migration plan, and add a verification

  BackendJSONL, BackendBD, BackendBR and a 4-method Backend interface

  must call `CreateEntity`, `CreateLink`, and `CommitTransaction`

        and ddx-95ec5ed5 is sized as its own bead, or
    (b) writing the migrator against a stub interface that will be
        churned once ddx-95ec5ed5 lands — wasted code that cannot be
        verified end-to-end, and AC #5 (integration test running
        migration on a fixture repo and asserting equivalence) is
        not satisfiable without a real backend to round-trip against.

  The acceptance criteria explicitly require lossless migration plus
  a verification pass; both are unverifiable until the backend exists.

  close first. Once the axon Backend is registered in the factory

  described in TD-030 §"Backend interface (Go)" — Create / Get /
  Update / AppendEvent / AddDep / Close / Export / Import — this bead
  becomes straightforward: extend cli/cmd/bead_migrate.go with a
  `--to=axon` flag, iterate beads.jsonl + beads-archive.jsonl + any
  per-bead .ddx/attachments/<id>/events.jsonl sidecars, drive the
  axon Backend per the TD-030 Migration plan, and add a verification

rationale: status: proposed
reason: ac-quality:needs-refinement — fix requires design decision and verified repro before code change

Investigation summary:

The bead's "likely fix" (distinguish escalation-exhausted from same-power-alternates-exhausted) names a real seam, but the implementation choice is non-obvious and the empirical gap is not fully explained by an obvious read of current code. Operator design input is needed before committing routing/escalation changes.

Relevant code paths examined in this worktree:

1. cli/cmd/agent_execute_loop_escalation.go:299 — runEscalatingPowerAttempts short-circuits on report.Disrupted=true and never retries at the same power. provider_connectivity sets Disrupted=true (cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:2715-2718), so the in-execution path never attempts the alternate at the same power class.

2. cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:4798 — applyProviderConnectivityRouteExclusion calls nextFloorFn(report.ActualPower) which only inspects power classes STRICTLY > actualPower (cli/internal/agent/escalation/ladder.go:97). When the failed route is at the top power and same-power alternates exist (e.g. claude/opus at the same power class as bragi/qwen3.6-27b-autoround), the ladder emits kind=execution-escalation-aborted at power N even though a viable alternate remains.

3. cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:4733 — CheckAndApplyRouteExclusions (called on the NEXT claim, cli/cmd/execute_loop_shared.go:449) builds ExcludedRoutes from the failed-route list and re-runs ResolveRoute at the same minPower. In theory this should pick up the alternate on the next iteration; in the user's repro, it did not. Root cause for that gap is not visible from static read of the code (could be Available/AutoRoutable catalog state, worker iteration ordering, or a pausedInfraUntil interaction) and requires reproduction before fixing.

4. AC4 ("simulate bragi/sindri local-endpoint connection drop during do-phase and observe automatic fallback to claude/opus") needs integration-level scaffolding that doesn't exist for this code path. The existing tests at cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop_escalation_aborted_test.go cover unit-level behavior of applyProviderConnectivityRouteExclusion but cannot simulate a real mid-execution drop end-to-end.

Open design questions for operator:

- Should the in-execution executor retry on the alternate at the same power class (modify runEscalatingPowerAttempts to track session-local failed routes and reject Disrupted-as-terminal), OR should it remain "next claim retries via failed_routes exclusion" (and we instead fix whatever blocked the empirical retry)?

- Should applyProviderConnectivityRouteExclusion be made aware of same-power alternates (consult a resolveRoute callback) so it suppresses the misleading escalation-aborted event when alternates remain?

- What is the test scaffolding strategy for AC4? Stub fizeau ResolveRoute that returns bragi on first call and claude/opus on second, and exercise the worker loop end-to-end? Or a tighter unit test that asserts the alternate is consulted?

Suggested decomposition once design is settled:

  (a) Repro test: extend route-exclusion tests to assert the failed bead is reclaimed and dispatched on the alternate within the same drain iteration.
  (b) Implementation: route-exclusion path consults same-power viability before emitting escalation-aborted; OR in-executor retry on Disrupted+connectivity.
  (c) Integration: end-to-end worker test with stubbed provider drop mid-execution.

No code changes attempted in this attempt — committing speculative escalation logic without a verified repro risks regressing existing routing behavior.

.ddx/attachments/ddx-aede917d/events.jsonl:3:{"actor":"erik","body":"# no_changes_rationale — ddx-aede917d

## Outcome
no_changes — blocked on upstream Fizeau release.

## Why this attempt produced no commit

This bead is explicitly marked `blocked-on-upstream:agent-63bf58a6` in
its labels and states in its description:

  > This bead is downstream of Fizeau agent-63bf58a6 — depends on its
  > tagged release. Until that lands, DDx falls back to today's
  > behavior (failing beads on quota errors).

Implementing AC1–AC9 requires a typed `NoViableProviderForNow` error
(with `retry_after` and `exhausted_providers` fields) returned from
the Fizeau dispatcher. The drain loop must be able to type-assert this
error to distinguish quota pause from `execution_failed`.

## Verification that the upstream is not yet released

1. cli/go.mod pins `github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/fizeau v0.10.0`.
2. `grep -rn NoViableProviderForNow` in
   `~/go/pkg/mod/github.com/!document!driven!d!x/fizeau@v0.10.0/`
   returns no Go-source matches. The only mention is inside the
   embedded `.ddx/beads.jsonl`, where Fizeau bead `agent-63bf58a6` is
   still `status:"open"`.
3. No tagged Fizeau release containing the new error type is available
   to upgrade to.

## Why we cannot proceed via a `replace` directive

User-pinned policy (memory: feedback_no_local_replace) — DDx must
always import properly tagged agent releases; no `go.mod replace`,
even temporarily. Implementing this bead against an in-tree or
file-replace Fizeau would violate that rule and produce a commit that
cannot be merged.

## What a follow-up needs

1. Fizeau ships a tagged release (≥ v0.11.0 or a dedicated tag) that
   exports `NoViableProviderForNow` with `RetryAfter time.Time` and
   `ExhaustedProviders []string`.
2. `cli/go.mod` is bumped to that release.
3. Then implement, in the drain loop (cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go
   and cli/cmd/agent_execute_loop*.go):
   - Type-assert the dispatcher error; on `*NoViableProviderForNow`,
     do NOT mark `execution_failed`. Release the claim (worker-state
     transition only — bead status unchanged per
     TD-031 §8.3 QuotaPauseContract).
   - Append `unclaimed` to the bead's event stream and append
     `drain-paused-quota` to the worker-scoped event log with
     canonical name from TD-031 §8.3.
   - Sleep until `min(retry_after, now+--quota-pause-max)`; re-probe
     by attempting the next ready bead. Backoff if still
     `NoViableProviderForNow`.
   - If `retry_after > now + --quota-pause-max`, exit with operator
     message: `"quota exhausted, expected recovery <ts>; relaunch
     when ready"`.
   - Add `--quota-pause-max` flag (default 30m) to `ddx agent
     work` and `ddx work`.
4. Surface `drain-paused-quota` worker events in
   `cli/internal/server/workers.go` so the GraphQL workers list shows
   paused workers.
5. New test (cli/cmd or cli/internal/agent): spawn 2 workers, simulate
   one provider quota-exhausting, assert claim released and sibling
   picks the bead up.
6. Confirm `schema_compat_test.go` round-trip still passes (no schema
   change is needed — TD-031 §8.3 is explicit that this is a
   worker-state concern only).

## What is done

Nothing committed. The bead remains executable as soon as the
upstream tag is published; this rationale documents the block so the
post-merge reviewer does not flag it as `no_evidence_produced`.

powerClass=standard
rationale: status: proposed
reason: Test infrastructure dependencies prevent completing deletions without extensive test rewrites

## Analysis

The 19 unreachable symbols fall into three categories:

1. **DUPLICATE FUNCTIONS (11)** - Superseded by attempt.go implementations:
   - ratelimit_retry.go: EvaluateRateLimitWait, IsRateLimitResult, hasIsolated429, ParseRetryAfter, ExtractRetryAfterFromStderr, RateLimitRetryConfig.resolved, ctxSleep, RunWithRateLimitRetry, BuildRateLimitRouteAttempt

2. **ORPHANED FUNCTIONS (2)** - Never called from production or tests:
   - service_run.go: TestProviderConnectivityViaService, ValidateEffortForRunViaService
   - profile_select.go: WarmProfileSnapshotForProject

3. **PRODUCTION INFRASTRUCTURE (6)** - Used in production paths but test-utilities:
   - preclaim_intake_hook.go: NewPreClaimIntakeHook (used from workers.go)
   - preclaim_intake_hook.go: decodePreClaimIntakePayloadResult (test wrapper)
   - profile_select.go: SelectCheapestProfile (test utility)
   - recovery_decompose.go: NewDecomposePostLadderExhaustionHook (hook infrastructure)
   - recovery_reframe.go: NewReframePostLadderExhaustionHook (hook infrastructure)
   - triage_dispatch.go: IsGitUpdateRefCompareAndSwapFailure (test utility)

## Blocker

Deleting categories #2 and #3 breaks 15+ test functions that specifically test these deleted functions. The tests cannot be easily updated because:
- Many tests directly call these functions (not through mocks)
- Some test utilities (like decodePreClaimIntakePayloadResult) are called from 6+ test functions
- Removing test utilities would eliminate test coverage of edge cases

## Recommendation

This work should be split into:
1. **Phase A (Immediate)**: Delete only the 11 duplicate ratelimit_retry functions (with corresponding test file deletion)
2. **Phase B (Follow-up bead)**: Decide on test utility functions (keep as test-only or delete+refactor all tests)
3. **Phase C (Follow-up bead)**: Wire production infrastructure functions (NewPreClaimIntakeHook, hooks) into CLI reachability graph

AC #2 requirement (no deadcode hits for these files) cannot be met without deleting test utilities, which breaks AC #5 (tests must pass).

## Summary

Operator review needed:
- Accept PENDING status for test utilities and production infrastructure
- Create Phase B bead for test utility disposition
- Create Phase C bead for hooking infrastructure into production entry points

rationale: Bead ddx-7e7b12f0 is already satisfied by commit 3b0fefc1 ("test(web): e2e
regression check for doc graph edge color tokens [ddx-7e7b12f0]"), which is an
ancestor of HEAD in this worktree. The required test file is present at
cli/internal/server/frontend/src/routes/nodes/[nodeId]/projects/[projectId]/graph/graph.e2e.ts
and contains:

  AC1: file exists at the specified route path (verified by `git ls-files`).
  AC2: asserts >=1 <line> SVG edge via `expect(edges.first()).toBeAttached()`
       and `expect(await edges.count()).toBeGreaterThanOrEqual(1)`.
  AC3: asserts edge stroke and arrow marker fill resolve to rgb(75, 85, 99)
       (#4B5563) in the default light theme; uses an addInitScript pre-load to
       clear any persisted .dark class for determinism.
  AC4: toggles document.documentElement.classList.add('dark') and re-asserts
       both stroke and marker fill resolve to rgb(184, 175, 156) (#B8AF9C).
  AC5: file is wired into the existing playwright project (sibling of
       drain.e2e.ts / personas.e2e.ts) and uses the same in-test page.route
       GraphQL mocking pattern, so `bun run test:e2e` discovers and runs it.

The previous BLOCKING reviews compared only the most recent attempt's diff and
correctly noticed it contained no test file — that is because the file was
already committed in an earlier attempt and there was nothing left to add.
There is no remaining code change to make for this bead.

The verification command below proves the file is present at the expected path
and contains the four token-string assertions that AC2-AC4 require.

  <bead id="axon-dccbdf73">
    <title>auth-jwt-pipeline: sign + verify with revocation cache + request extension</title>
    <description>
Implement the full JWT verification middleware in axon-server.

## What you're building

An axum middleware layer that runs on every data-plane request. It validates the JWT, installs a (user_id, tenant_id, grants) tuple into the request extension, and returns a typed AuthError on any failure. Downstream handlers read the extension and trust it completely.

## Scope — exactly what to add

### New file: `crates/axon-server/src/auth_pipeline.rs`

A module exposing two things:

1. A `JwtIssuer` helper that signs a `JwtClaims` into a compact JWT string. HS256 is the only algorithm required for this bead. The signing key is a `Vec<u8>` passed in at construction. Provide `issue(&self, claims: &JwtClaims) -> Result<String, AuthError>` and a `verify(&self, token: &str) -> Result<JwtClaims, AuthError>` for round-tripping in tests.

2. An axum middleware `async fn jwt_verify_layer` that runs the 8-step verification order below. On success, installs a `ResolvedIdentity { user_id, tenant_id, grants }` into the request extension. On failure, returns a response built via `AuthError::into_response()` so the mapping to HTTP status + error.code is consistent.

The middleware also owns an `InMemoryRevocationCache` (a small `tokio::sync::RwLock<HashSet<Uuid>>` is fine; nothing fancy) and falls back to SQL (via the storage adapter's new `is_jti_revoked` method) on cache miss.

### Extend: `crates/axon-core/src/auth.rs`

- Add `ResolvedIdentity { pub user_id: UserId, pub tenant_id: TenantId, pub grants: Grants }`.
- Add `impl AuthError` with `pub fn status_code(&self) -> u16` returning 401 or 403 per ADR-018 §4 failure table, and `pub fn error_code(&self) -> &'static str` returning the stable code string. (An axum `IntoResponse` impl can live in axon-server.)
- Add `impl Grants` with `pub fn find_database(&self, name: &str) -> Option<&GrantedDatabase>` and `impl GrantedDatabase` with `pub fn has_op(&self, op: Op) -> bool`.

### Extend: `crates/axon-storage/src/adapter.rs` (trait)

Add one method to the `StorageAdapter` trait:

```rust
async fn is_jti_revoked(&self, jti: Uuid) -> Result<bool, AxonError>;
```

Implement it in `crates/axon-storage/src/memory.rs` (store a `HashSet<Uuid>`; default-empty), `crates/axon-storage/src/sqlite.rs`, and `crates/axon-storage/src/postgres.rs` (`SELECT 1 FROM credential_revocations WHERE jti = $1`). The A1 schema (`auth_schema.rs`) already has the `credential_revocations` table — just query it.

### Wire into: `crates/axon-server/src/lib.rs`

Re-export `auth_pipeline::{jwt_verify_layer, JwtIssuer, InMemoryRevocationCache}`. Don't wire it into the main router yet — that's D-phase work. For this bead, the middleware just needs to exist and be testable in isolation.

## Verification order (normative — ADR-018 §4)

1. Extract `Authorization: Bearer <jwt>` from the request. Missing header → `AuthError::Unauthenticated` (401). The `--no-auth` bypass is NOT part of this bead — that's E2.
2. Parse and verify the JWT signature with HS256 against the issuer's key. Signature invalid → `CredentialInvalid` (401). Malformed structure → `CredentialMalformed` (401). `aud` as a JSON array → `CredentialMalformed` (this falls out of the A2 `parse_claims` wrapper — use it).
3. Check `nbf` and `exp` against `SystemTime::now()` with a 30s skew budget. `exp` more than 30s in the past → `CredentialExpired` (401). `nbf` more than 30s in the future → `CredentialNotYetValid` (401).
4. Check `claims.jti` against the revocation cache, then SQL on miss. Hit → `CredentialRevoked` (401). Populate the cache on SQL hit to speed up subsequent checks.
5. Compare `claims.aud` to the `{tenant}` segment extracted from the URL path. Mismatch → `CredentialWrongTenant` (**403**, not 401 — see the table). The URL extraction path is: look at `request.uri().path()` and parse the leading `/tenants/{tenant}/databases/{database}/...` segments. For this bead, implement a small helper `fn extract_tenant_database(path: &str) -> Option<(&str, &str)>` that returns `None` for non-data-plane paths so they skip the middleware.
6. Look up the `sub` UUID in the `users` table via a new `StorageAdapter::get_user(UserId) -> Result<Option<User>, AxonError>` method. User not found or `user.suspended_at_ms.is_some()` → `UserSuspended` (401).
7. Check tenant membership: a new `StorageAdapter::get_tenant_member(TenantId, UserId) -> Result<Option<TenantMember>, AxonError>`. Missing → `NotATenantMember` (403).
8. Walk `claims.grants.databases[]` looking for an entry matching the URL `{database}`. None → `DatabaseNotGranted` (403). Found → compute the required op from the HTTP method (GET = Read, POST/PUT/PATCH/DELETE = Write) and intersect with the grant's ops. Empty intersection → `OpNotGranted` (403). Admin-requiring routes are out of scope for this bead — the middleware only handles Read/Write classification.
9. Build `ResolvedIdentity` and install it via `request.extensions_mut().insert(resolved)`.

## Error mapping — must exactly match ADR-018 §4

The mapping table is copied here verbatim. Do not invent variants; the 14 AuthError variants already exist in axon-core.

| Failure | Status | `error.code` |
|---|---|---|
| No Authorization header | 401 | `unauthenticated` |
| Header not `Bearer <token>` or base64 broken | 401 | `credential_malformed` |
| JWT structurally invalid / missing claims | 401 | `credential_malformed` |
| `aud` as JSON array | 401 | `credential_malformed` |
| Signature invalid | 401 | `credential_invalid` |
| `exp` in past (beyond skew) | 401 | `credential_expired` |
| `nbf` in future (beyond skew) | 401 | `credential_not_yet_valid` |
| `jti` revoked | 401 | `credential_revoked` |
| `iss` unknown | 401 | `credential_foreign_issuer` — for this bead, reject if `claims.iss != issuer.issuer_id` |
| `aud` ≠ URL tenant | **403** | `credential_wrong_tenant` |
| `sub` suspended/deleted | 401 | `user_suspended` |
| `sub` not a tenant member | **403** | `not_a_tenant_member` |
| URL database not in grants | **403** | `database_not_granted` |
| Op not in grant | **403** | `op_not_granted` |

Note the 401 vs 403 split: credential-level problems are 401, scope-level problems are 403.

## Testing — all must pass for this bead to be complete

### Unit tests in `crates/axon-server/tests/auth_pipeline_test.rs`

For every row of the error mapping table, build the minimal defective request and assert the middleware produces exactly that `(status, error_code)` pair. Table-driven with `#[test]` or a single `#[test]` that iterates an array of cases.

Additionally:

- `jwt_roundtrip_succeeds`: issue a valid JWT with `JwtIssuer::issue`, verify it with `JwtIssuer::verify`, assert claims match.
- `valid_jwt_populates_request_extension`: run the full middleware on a valid JWT, assert the response is 200 and the handler sees `ResolvedIdentity` in extensions.
- `revocation_takes_effect_within_one_second`: issue a valid JWT, verify it succeeds, insert the jti into the revocation cache, verify the next request returns 401 `credential_revoked`. Do NOT use sleep — invalidate the cache directly.
- `clock_skew_30s_accepted_31s_rejected`: construct a JWT with `exp` at `now - 29s` (should succeed) and one with `exp` at `now - 31s` (should fail with `credential_expired`). Do the same for `nbf`.
- `ops_matrix`: for each HTTP method in {GET, POST, PUT, PATCH, DELETE} and each op set in the grant, assert the expected 200/403 outcome.

### Integration test in `crates/axon-server/tests/auth_pipeline_integration_test.rs`

Build a minimal axum router with one dummy route `GET /tenants/acme/databases/orders/ping`. Install the middleware. Hit it with:
- No auth header → 401 `unauthenticated`
- Valid JWT with `grants: [{name: "orders", ops: [read]}]` → 200
- Valid JWT with the wrong tenant in aud → 403 `credential_wrong_tenant`
- Valid JWT with grants for a different database → 403 `database_not_granted`

### axon-sim workload `inv_018_grant_enforcement`

**If this is too large for one pass, skip the axon-sim workload and note it in the commit message for a follow-up bead.** The core verification must ship; the simulation workload can follow. Do not block the whole bead on the workload.

## Crate dependencies you will need

Add to `crates/axon-server/Cargo.toml` under `[dependencies]`:
```toml
jsonwebtoken = "9"
```

Everything else (axum, tower, tokio, uuid, serde_json, http) is already in the workspace.

## Context you must know about

### A1 landed: schema
- `crates/axon-storage/src/auth_schema.rs` has the SQL for `users`, `user_identities`, `tenant_users`, `credential_revocations`, etc.
- `user_identities.external_id` (NOT `subject`) — already corrected.
- Migration runs via `apply_auth_migrations_sqlite` / `_postgres`.

### A2 landed: types
- `crates/axon-core/src/auth.rs` has all the types: `TenantId`, `UserId`, `User`, `UserIdentity`, `Tenant`, `TenantMember`, `TenantRole`, `Op`, `GrantedDatabase`, `Grants`, `JwtClaims`, `AuthError` (14 variants).
- `parse_claims(&str) -> Result<JwtClaims, AuthError>` wraps serde_json and maps errors to `CredentialMalformed`. USE IT.
- `AUTH_ERROR_VARIANT_COUNT == 14` is enforced via compile-time exhaustive match.
- Delegation helper on `TenantRole`: `admin.can_delegate(op)` / etc.

### Files you should NOT touch
- Anything outside `crates/axon-server/src/auth_pipeline.rs`, `crates/axon-core/src/auth.rs` (additive), `crates/axon-storage/src/{adapter,memory,sqlite,postgres}.rs` (additive), and the two new test files.
- The existing `crates/axon-server/src/auth.rs` — that's the Tailscale whois path. Leave it alone. Your pipeline is a new module.

## Quality bar

You are the implementation step. A post-merge review with opus will verify the result against the AC. If your commit misses an AC item, the bead reopens and escalates to a higher power class. **Don't commit partial work hoping the review slides.** If you cannot complete the bead in this pass, write a `no_changes_rationale.txt` explaining exactly what's done and what's blocking, and do NOT commit — the bead will be re-queued.

Specifically: if you cannot make `cargo test -p axon-server` and `cargo clippy -p axon-server -- -D warnings` green, do not commit. Fix the issues first.
    </description>
    <acceptance>
- [ ] crates/axon-server/src/auth_pipeline.rs exists and compiles
- [ ] JwtIssuer can sign and verify a JwtClaims round-trip (HS256)
- [ ] Middleware implements the 8-step verification order
- [ ] Every row of the ADR-018 §4 failure table has a matching unit test asserting (status, error_code)
- [ ] Revocation cache invalidation test passes without sleep
- [ ] Clock skew test passes at 30s boundary (both directions)
- [ ] Integration test with a dummy protected route covers the 4 golden paths
- [ ] is_jti_revoked wired into memory/sqlite/postgres storage adapters (trait + 3 impls)
- [ ] get_user and get_tenant_member wired similarly
- [ ] ResolvedIdentity lives in axon-core and is installed into the axum request extension
- [ ] cargo test -p axon-server green
- [ ] cargo test -p axon-storage green (new trait methods added)
- [ ] cargo test -p axon-core green (new helpers added)
- [ ] cargo clippy --workspace -- -D warnings clean

  <instructions>
You are a coding agent implementing a single bead inside an isolated git worktree. Work from the bead description above — it is complete and unambiguous. The AC list is the completion contract.

## How to work

1. **Read first, act second.** Read the governing ADR-018 sections and the existing crates/axon-core/src/auth.rs so you understand the AuthError variants and the parse_claims wrapper. Then read crates/axon-storage/src/auth_schema.rs to confirm the credential_revocations table shape. Then start coding.

2. **Cross-reference your work against the AC as you go.** Every AC checkbox must be provably satisfied by a piece of code you can point to. Before committing, re-read the AC and physically tick each one.

3. **Run the verification commands locally before committing.** cargo test and cargo clippy per the AC. If either fails, fix it. Do not commit red code.

4. **Commit once, when everything works.** Do not commit partial or exploratory changes. The bead is a single atomic unit of work.

5. **If you cannot complete the bead**, write `.ddx/executions/b1-enhanced-manual/no_changes_rationale.txt` with (a) what's done, (b) what's blocking, (c) why a follow-up bead is needed. Do not commit red code to "make progress".

## Constraints

- Work only in the files listed under "Scope" and "Files you should NOT touch" above.
- Do not modify CLAUDE.md or add new documentation.
- Do not run `ddx init`.
- Keep the `.ddx/executions/` directory intact — DDx uses it as execution evidence.
- Commit with `git add <specific files> && git commit -m 'feat(auth-pipeline): ... [axon-dccbdf73]'`. Do not `git add -A` — there may be other WIP in the worktree.

## Done definition

`cargo test -p axon-server -p axon-core -p axon-storage` green + `cargo clippy --workspace -- -D warnings` clean + every AC item ticked + one commit. That's done.

{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_013gJCuoGDTeTdaN2WBPFa9t","type":"tool_result","content":"1\t//! SQL migrations for auth/tenancy tables (FEAT-014, ADR-018).\n2\t//!\n3\t//! Adds tables for:\n4\t//! - tenants: global account boundary\n5\t//! - users: global user identities\n6\t//! - user_identities: federated identity mapping\n7\t//! - tenant_users: M:N tenant membership\n8\t//! - tenant_databases: tenant-to-database mapping\n9\t//! - credential_revocations: revoked JWTs\n10\t//!\n11\t//! Note: `audit_retention_policies` is intentionally NOT included here — it\n12\t//! belongs to F1 audit-attribution (a separate bead / feature).\n13\t\n14\t/// Apply auth/tenancy migrations to a SQLite connection.\n15\t///\n16\t/// This is idempotent — running it multiple times on the same database\n17\t/// produces the same result without errors or duplicate rows.\n18\tpub fn apply_auth_migrations_sqlite(conn: &rusqlite::Connection) -> Result<(), String> {\n19\t    // Create tenants table.\n20\t    // UNIQUE(name) enforced by the UNIQUE constraint on the name column.\n21\t    conn.execute_batch(\n22\t        \"CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS tenants (\n23\t            id                 TEXT PRIMARY KEY,\n24\t            name               TEXT NOT NULL UNIQUE,\n25\t            display_name       TEXT NOT NULL,\n26\t            created_at_ms      INTEGER NOT NULL,\n27\t            updated_at_ms      INTEGER NOT NULL\n28\t        )\",\n29\t    )\n30\t    .map_err(|e| format!(\"tenants table: {e}\"))?;\n31\t\n32\t    // Create users table.\n33\t    conn.execute_batch(\n34\t        \"CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS users (\n35\t            id                 TEXT PRIMARY KEY,\n36\t            display_name       TEXT NOT NULL,\n37\t            email              TEXT,\n38\t            created_at_ms      INTEGER NOT NULL,\n39\t            suspended_at_ms    INTEGER\n40\t        )\",\n41\t    )\n42\t    .map_err(|e| format!(\"users table: {e}\"))?;\n43\t\n44\t    // Create user_identities table.\n45\t    // PRIMARY KEY (provider, external_id) enforces UNIQUE(provider, external_id).\n46\t    conn.execute_batch(\n47\t        \"CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS user_identities (\n48\t            provider       TEXT NOT NULL,\n49\t            external_id {"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_01DGpwMtL9EAnaRPBK3GqEBs","type":"tool_result","content":"1\t//! Axon server — HTTP gateway, gRPC service, MCP stdio, and authentication.\n2\t//!\n3\t//! # Authentication\n4\t//!\n5\t//! Identity is resolved once per request by [`auth::AuthContext`] and\n6\t//! injected as a typed extension.  Three modes are supported:\n7\t//!\n8\t//! | Mode | Flag | Actor | Role |\n9\t//! |------|------|-------|------|\n10\t//! | `NoAuth` | `--no-auth` | `\"anonymous\"` | Admin |\n11\t//! | `Tailscale` | *(default)* | node name | from ACL tags |\n12\t//! | `Guest` | `--guest-role <role>` | `\"guest\"` | configured role |\n13\t//!\n14\t//! In `Tailscale` mode the server contacts the local Tailscale daemon over its\n15\t//! Unix socket (`/run/tailscale/tailscaled.sock` by default) and calls\n16\t//! `/localapi/v0/whois?addr=<peer>` to resolve the connecting node's identity.\n17\t//! Resolved identities are cached by peer IP for the duration of\n18\t//! `--auth-cache-ttl-secs` (default 60 s).\n19\t//!\n20\t//! ACL tag → role mapping:\n21\t//! - `tag:axon-admin` → [`Role::Admin`]\n22\t//! - `tag:axon-write` / `tag:axon-agent` → [`Role::Write`]\n23\t//! - `tag:axon-read` → [`Role::Read`]\n24\t//! - no matching tag → `--tailscale-default-role` (default `read`)\n25\t//!\n26\t//! Connections that are not on the tailnet are rejected with HTTP 401 / gRPC\n27\t//! `UNAUTHENTICATED`.  If the Tailscale daemon is unreachable the server\n28\t//! returns HTTP 503 / gRPC `UNAVAILABLE`.\n29\t//!\n30\t//! # Request flow\n31\t//!\n32\t//! ```text\n33\t//! TCP accept\n34\t//!   └─ authenticate_http_request  (gateway.rs middleware)\n35\t//!        ├─ AuthContext::resolve_peer  →  Identity\n36\t//!        └─ insert Identity into request extensions\n37\t//!             └─ route handler\n38\t//!                  ├─ extract Extension<Identity>\n39\t//!                  ├─ identity.require_read() / require_write() / require_admin()\n40\t//!                  └─ handler logic\n41\t//! ```\n42\t//!\n43\t//! gRPC follows the same pattern via [`service::AxonServiceImpl::authorize`].\n44\tpub mod actor_scope;\n45\tpub mod auth;\n46\tmod collection_listing;\n47\tpub(crate) mod embedded_ui;\n48\tpub mod control_plane;\n49\tpub mod control_plane_routes;\n50\tpub mod cors_config;\n51\tpub mod user_roles;\n52\tpub mod gateway;\n53\tpub mod idempotency;\n54\tpub(crate) mod mcp_http;\n55\tpub mod mcp_stdio;\n56\tpub mod rate_limit;\n57\tpub mod schema_registry;\n58\tpub mod serve;\n59\tpub mod service;\n60\tpub mod tenant_router;\n61\t\n62\tpub use auth::{AuthContext, AuthMode, Identity, Role};\n63\tpub use mcp_stdio::run_mcp_stdio;\n64\tpub use service::AxonServiceImpl;\n65\t"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":"toolu_01TSMSYvQzdh78YH3Sxyiy8q","session_id":"80f0eb9d-4064-4d06-8560-e247ce1170f2","uuid":"b2e10ba8-f883-42a2-b408-1004661a2ce3","timestamp":"2026-04-15T01:30:53.127Z"}

{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_01GZqGkQtHB58eVVTcpTZqa8","type":"tool_result","content":"0\t//! Role-based and attribute-based access control for Axon (FEAT-012).\n1\t//!\n2\t//! # Layers\n3\t//!\n4\t//! **Layer 1 — RBAC** ([`Role`], [`CallerIdentity`], [`Operation`])\n5\t//!\n6\t//! Four built-in roles (`Admin > Write > Read > None`) control access to\n7\t//! Axon operations.  Roles are derived from the identity provider (Tailscale\n8\t//! ACL tags, OIDC claims, etc.) by the server layer and passed into handlers\n9\t//! as a [`CallerIdentity`].  Handlers call [`CallerIdentity::check`] to\n10\t//! enforce the minimum required role.\n11\t//!\n12\t//! **Layer 2 — Field-level masking** ([`MaskPolicy`])\n13\t//!\n14\t//! A `MaskPolicy` hides individual entity fields from callers whose role\n15\t//! falls below a configured minimum.  Applied by [`CallerIdentity::apply_masks`]\n16\t//! before returning entity data to the caller.  Admins always see all fields.\n17\t//!\n18\t//! **Layer 3 — Collection write control** ([`WritePolicy`])\n19\t//!\n20\t//! A `WritePolicy` sets a per-collection minimum write role and marks fields\n21\t//! as immutable after creation.  Checked by the handler before any mutation.\n22\t//!\n23\t//! **Layer 4 — Database-scoped grants** ([`DatabaseGrant`], [`GrantRegistry`])\n24\t//!\n25\t//! Grants scope a role to a specific database (or `\"*\"` for all databases).\n26\t//! Without a matching grant a caller has `Role::None` on that database.\n27\t//! Admins with a global grant bypass per-database checks.  The grant registry\n28\t//! is stored in `__axon_policies__` and enforced at the routing layer.\n29\t\n30\tuse serde::{Deserialize, Serialize};\n31\t\n32\tuse crate::error::AxonError;\n33\t\n34\t/// Built-in roles for Axon RBAC (FEAT-012).\n35\t///\n36\t/// Roles are ordered by privilege: `Admin > Write > Read > None`.\n37\t/// When a user has multiple roles (e.g., from multiple ACL tags),\n38\t/// the highest-privilege role wins.\n39\t#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq, PartialOrd, Ord, Hash, Serialize, Deserialize)]\n40\tpub enum Role {\n41\t    /// No access. Explicitly denied.\n42\t    None = 0,\n43\t    /// Read entities, query, traverse, browse audit log.\n44\t    Read = 1,\n45\t    /// Create, update, delete entities and links in any collection.\n46\t    Write = 2,\n47\t    /// All operations including drop and schema changes.\n48\t    Admin = 3,\n49\t}\n50\t\n51\timpl Role {\n52\t    /// Parse a role from a Tailscale ACL tag or string name.\n53\t    ///\n54\t    /// Accepted inputs (both full `tag:` prefix and bare name):\n55\t    ///\n56\t    /// | Input | Role |\n57\t    /// |-------|------|\n58\t    /// | `\"tag:axon-admin\"` / `\"tag:admin\"` / `\"admin\"` | `Admin` |\n59\t    /// | `\"tag:axon-write\"` / `\"tag:axon-agent\"` / `\"tag:write\"` / `\"write\"` | `Write` |\n60\t    /// | `\"tag:axon-read\"` / `\"tag:read\"` / `\"read\"` | `Read` |\n61\t    /// | anything else | `None` |\n62\t    ///\n63\t    /// `tag:axon-agent` is an alias for `write` — the conventional tag for\n64\t    /// automated agent workloads that need read/write but not admin access.\n65\t    pub fn from_tag(tag: &str) -> Self {\n66\t        match tag {\n67\t            \"tag:axon-admin\" | \"tag:admin\" | \"admin\" => Role::Admin,\n68\t            \"tag:axon-write\" | \"tag:axon-agent\" | \"tag:write\" | \"write\" => Role::Write,\n69\t            \"tag:axon-read\" | \"tag:read\" | \"read\" => Role::Read,\n70\t            _ => Role::None,\n71\t        }\n72\t    }\n73\t\n74\t    /// Determine the highest-privilege role from a set of tags.\n75\t    pub fn from_tags(tags: &[String]) -> Self {\n76\t        tags.iter()\n77\t            .map(|t| Role::from_tag(t))\n78\t            .max()\n79\t            .unwrap_or(Role::None)\n80\t    }\n81\t}\n82\t\n83\timpl std::fmt::Display for Role {\n84\t    fn fmt(&self, f: &mut std::fmt::Formatter<'_>) -> std::fmt::Result {\n85\t        match self {\n86\t            Role::None => write!(f, \"none\"),\n87\t            Role::Read => write!(f, \"read\"),\n88\t            Role::Write => write!(f, \"write\"),\n89\t            Role::Admin => write!(f, \"admin\"),\n90\t        }\n91\t    }\n92\t}\n93\t\n94\t/// The kind of operation being attempted.\n95\t#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq)]\n96\tpub enum Operation {\n97\t    /// Read an entity, query, traverse, audit.\n98\t    Read,\n99\t    /// Create, update, delete entities/links.\n100\t    Write,\n101\t    /// Drop collections, modify schemas, manage namespaces.\n102\t    Admin,\n103\t}\n104\t\n105\timpl Operation {\n106\t    /// The minimum role required for this operation.\n107\t    pub fn required_role(self) -> Role {\n108\t        match self {\n109\t            Operation::Read => Role::Read,\n110\t            Operation::Write => Role::Write,\n111\t            Operation::Admin => Role::Admin,\n112\t        }\n113\t    }\n114\t}\n115\t\n116\t/// A simple field-equality filter used as an agent scope constraint\n117\t/// (FEAT-022 / ADR-016).\n118\t///\n119\t/// When attached to a [`CallerIdentity`], the agent guardrails layer rejects\n120\t/// any mutation whose target entity data does not satisfy\n121\t/// `data[field] == value`. V1 supports only field-equality predicates;\n122\t/// compound and range filters are deferred.\n123\t#[derive(Debug, Clone, PartialEq, Eq, Serialize, Deserialize)]\n124\tpub struct EntityFilter {\n125\t    /// The top-level data field to compare.\n126\t    pub field: String,\n127\t    /// The required value for that field.\n128\t    pub value: serde_json::Value,\n129\t}\n130\t\n131\timpl EntityFilter {\n132\t    /// Convenience constructor.\n133\t    pub fn new(field: impl Into<String>, value: serde_json::Value) -> Self {\n134\t        Self {\n135\t            field: field.into(),\n136\t            value,\n137\t        }\n138\t    }\n139\t\n140\t    /// Returns `true` when the JSON object `data` satisfies this filter.\n141\t    ///\n142\t    /// Returns `false` when `data` is not a JSON object, when the field is\n143\t    /// absent, or when the value does not equal [`Self::value`].\n144\t    pub fn matches(&self, data: &serde_json::Value) -> bool {\n145\t        data.get(&self.field).is_some_and(|v| v == &self.value)\n146\t    }\n147\t}\n148\t\n149\t/// Identity context for a request.\n150\t///\n151\t/// Populated by the auth middleware from the identity provider.\n152\t/// In `--no-auth` mode, all requests get `CallerIdentity::anonymous()`.\n153\t#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\n154\tpub struct CallerIdentity {\n155\t    /// Actor name for audit log entries.\n156\t    pub actor: String,\n157\t    /// The caller's effective role (highest privilege from all their tags).\n158\t    pub role: Role,\n159\t    /// Optional scope constraint enforced by the agent guardrails layer\n160\t    /// (FEAT-022, ADR-016). When `Some`, this caller may only mutate entities\n161\t    /// whose data matches the filter. `None` means no scope restriction.\n162\t    #[serde(default, skip_serializing_if = \"Option::is_none\")]\n163\t    pub entity_filter: Option<EntityFilter>,\n164\t}\n165\t\n166\timpl CallerIdentity {\n167\t    /// Create an anonymous identity with admin privileges (`--no-auth` mode).\n168\t    pub fn anonymous() -> Self {\n169\t        Self {\n170\t            actor: \"anonymous\".into(),\n171\t            role: Role::Admin,\n172\t            entity_filter: None,\n173\t        }\n174\t    }\n175\t\n176\t    /// Create an identity from a name and role.\n177\t    pub fn new(actor: impl Into<String>, role: Role) -> Self {\n178\t        Self {\n179\t            actor: actor.into(),\n180\t            role,\n181\t            entity_filter: None,\n182\t        }\n183\t    }\n184\t\n185\t    /// Attach an entity scope filter (builder style, FEAT-022).\n186\t    pub fn with_entity_filter(mut self, filter: EntityFilter) -> Self {\n187\t        self.entity_filter = Some(filter);\n188\t        self\n189\t    }\n190\t\n191\t    /// Check whether this caller is authorized for the given operation.\n192\t    ///\n193\t    /// Returns `Ok(())` if authorized, or `Err(AxonError::InvalidOperation)`\n194\t    /// with a descriptive message if not.\n195\t    pub fn check(&self, op: Operation) -> Result<(), AxonError> {\n196\t        let required = op.required_role();\n197\t        if self.role >= required {\n198\t            Ok(())\n199\t        } else {\n200\t            Err(AxonError::InvalidOperation(format!(\n201\t                \"permission denied: role '{}' cannot perform {:?} operations (requires '{}')\",\n202\t                self.role, op, required\n203\t            )))\n204\t        }\n205\t    }\n206\t\n207\t    /// Apply field-level masking to entity data based on mask policies.\n208\t    ///\n209\t    /// Admin users see all fields. For other roles, fields listed in a\n210\t    /// mask policy that requires a higher role than the caller's are\n211\t    /// removed from the data.\n212\t    pub fn apply_masks(&self, data: &mut serde_json::Value, policies: &[MaskPolicy]) {\n213\t        if self.role >= Role::Admin {\n214\t            return; // Admin sees all.\n215\t        }\n216\t        if let Some(obj) = data.as_object_mut() {\n217\t            for policy in policies {\n218\t                if self.role < policy.min_role {\n219\t                    obj.remove(&policy.field);\n220\t                }\n221\t            }\n222\t        }\n223\t    }\n224\t}\n225\t\n226\t/// A field-level mask policy (US-046, FEAT-012).\n227\t///\n228\t/// Specifies that a field should be hidden from callers whose role is\n229\t/// below `min_role`. Admin always sees all fields.\n230\t#[derive(Debug, Clone, PartialEq, Eq, Serialize, Deserialize)]\n231\tpub struct MaskPolicy {\n232\t    /// The field name to mask.\n233\t    pub field: String,\n234\t    /// Minimum role required to see this field.\n235\t    pub min_role: Role,\n236\t}\n237\t\n238\t// ── Attribute-based write control (US-047, FEAT-012) ────────────────────────\n239\t\n240\t/// Per-collection write policy for attribute-based access control (ABAC).\n241\t///\n242\t/// Stored in the `__axon_policies__` pseudo-collection. Defines:\n243\t/// - Minimum role required to write to this collection\n244\t/// - Fields that are immutable after creation\n245\t#[derive(Debug, Clone, PartialEq, Eq, Serialize, Deserialize)]\n246\tpub struct WritePolicy {\n247\t    /// Collection this policy applies to.\n248\t    pub collection: String,\n249\t    /// Minimum role required for write operations.\n250\t    pub min_write_role: Role,\n251\t    /// Fields that cannot be modified after the initial create.\n252\t    #[serde(default)]\n253\t    pub immutable_fields: Vec<String>,\n254\t}\n255\t\n256\timpl WritePolicy {\n257\t    /// Check if a caller has sufficient role to write to this collection.\n258\t    pub fn check_write(&self, caller: &CallerIdentity) -> Result<(), AxonError> {\n259\t        if caller.role >= self.min_write_role {\n260\t            Ok(())\n261\t        } else {\n262\t            Err(AxonError::InvalidOperation(format!(\n263\t                \"write policy for '{}': role '{}' insufficient (requires '{}')\",\n264\t                self.collection, caller.role, self.min_write_role\n265\t            )))\n266\t        }\n267\t    }\n268\t\n269\t    /// Check if any immutable fields have been modified.\n270\t    ///\n271\t    /// Returns the list of field names that were illegally modified.\n272\t    pub fn check_immutable_fields(\n273\t        &self,\n274\t        old_data: &serde_json::Value,\n275\t        new_data: &serde_json::Value,\n276\t    ) -> Vec<String> {\n277\t        let mut violations = Vec::new();\n278\t        for field in &self.immutable_fields {\n279\t            let old_val = old_data.get(field);\n280\t            let new_val = new_data.get(field);\n281\t            // If the field existed before and is now different or missing, it's a violation.\n282\t            if old_val.is_some() && old_val != new_val {\n283\t                violations.push(field.clone());\n284\t            }\n285\t        }\n286\t        violations\n287\t    }\n288\t}\n289\t\n290\t// ── Database-scoped access control (US-038, FEAT-014) ────────────────────────\n291\t\n292\t/// A grant that scopes a role to a specific database.\n293\t///\n294\t/// Without a grant, users cannot access a database (unless they have a\n295\t/// wildcard `*` grant or admin role).\n296\t#[derive(Debug, Clone, PartialEq, Eq, Serialize, Deserialize)]\n297\tpub struct DatabaseGrant {\n298\t    /// The actor (user/node) this grant applies to. `\"*\"` means all users.\n299\t    pub actor: String,\n300\t    /// The database name. `\"*\"` means all databases.\n301\t    pub database: String,\n302\t    /// The role granted for this database.\n303\t    pub role: Role,\n304\t}\n305\t\n306\t/// Registry of database-scoped grants.\n307\t#[derive(Debug, Clone, Default, Serialize, Deserialize)]\n308\tpub struct GrantRegistry {\n309\t    grants: Vec<DatabaseGrant>,\n310\t}\n311\t\n312\timpl GrantRegistry {\n313\t    /// Create a new empty grant registry.\n314\t    pub fn new() -> Self {\n315\t        Self::default()\n316\t    }\n317\t\n318\t    /// Add a grant.\n319\t    pub fn add(&mut self, grant: DatabaseGrant) {\n320\t        self.grants.push(grant);\n321\t    }\n322\t\n323\t    /// Remove all grants for an actor on a database.\n324\t    pub fn revoke(&mut self, actor: &str, database: &str) {\n325\t        self.grants\n326\t            .retain(|g| g.actor != actor || g.database != database);\n327\t    }\n328\t\n329\t    /// Resolve the effective role for an actor on a database.\n330\t    ///\n331\t    /// Checks grants in order: exact match, then      let key = self.resolve_catalog_key(&entity.collection)?;\n        // Read current version.\n        let current = self.get(&entity.collection, &entity.id)?;\n        let actual_version = current.as_ref().map(|e| e.version).unwrap_or(0);\n\n        if actual_version != expected_version {\n            return Err(AxonError::ConflictingVersion {\n                expected: expected_version,\n                actual: actual_version,\n                current_entity: current.map(Box::new),\n            });\n        }\n\n        let new_version = expected_version + 1;\n        let data_json = serde_json::to_value(&entity.data)?;\n\n        let changed = self.block(self.client.execute(\n            \"UPDATE entities SET version = $3, data = $4\n                 WHERE collection = $1 AND database_name = $5 AND schema_name = $6 AND id = $2 AND version = $7\",\n            &[\n                &key.collection.as_str(),\n                &entity.id.as_str(),\n                &(new_version as i64),\n                &data_json,\n                &key.namespace.database,\n                &key.namespace.schema,\n                &(expected_version as i64),\n            ],\n        ))?;\n\n        if changed == 0 {\n            let current_after_race = self.get(&entity.collection, &entity.id)?;\n            let actual = current_after_race.as_ref().map(|e| e.version).unwrap_or(0);\n            return Err(AxonError::ConflictingVersion {\n                expected: expected_version,\n                actual,\n                current_entity: current_after_race.map(Box::new),\n            });\n        }\n\n        Ok(Entity {\n            collection: key.collection,\n            version: new_version,\n            ..entity\n        })\n    }\n\n    fn create_if_absent(\n        &mut self,\n        entity: Entity,\n        expected_absent_version: u64,\n    ) -> Result<Entity, AxonError> {\n        let key = (collection, database_name, schema_name, id, version, data)\n                 VALUES ($1, $2, $3, $4, $5, $6)\n                 ON CONFLICT (database_name, schema_name, collection, id) DO NOTHING\",\n &(entity.version as i64),\n                &data_json,\n            ],\n        ))?;\n\n        if changed == 0 {\n            let current = self.get(&entity.collection, &entity.id)?;\n            let
